title: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-08
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-08 PayPal pilots digital wallet in Germany 🇩🇪, eToro targets $4Bn valuation 🚀, Stripe's AI foundation model for payments 💲 6 Fintech Predictions By Plaid CEO Zach Perret (Sponsor) Will the AI hype pay off? Is fintech funding re-surging? How will rising fraud rates impact digital finance? Get Plaid Co-founder and CEO Zach Perret's takes on the top trends shaping modern fintech—from stablecoins to regulatory policies. Find out where the industry is headed and what it means for you. Read all of Zach's predictions here → Fintech Predictions: Six trends to know 📱 News & Trends PayPal pilots digital wallet in Germany amid stablecoin push (3 minute read) PayPal is launching its first mobile wallet in Germany, aiming to increase its share of in-store payments. The wallet allows users to make tap-to-pay purchases at Mastercard-accepting merchants and offers cashback incentives at select retailers. This move aligns with PayPal's broader strategy to promote its PYUSD stablecoin, which now offers a 3.7% yield to users holding it in PayPal or Venmo accounts. The wallet also plans to support "Pay Later" options, enabling German users to pay in installments ranging from 3 to 24 months. Stripe unveils AI foundation model for payments, reveals ‘deeper partnership' with Nvidia (5 minute read) Stripe announced on Wednesday a slew of new product launches at its annual Stripe Sessions user event. The highlights include a new AI foundation model for payments, stablecoin-powered accounts, a new Orchestration offering, and a recent migration with chip behemoth Nvidia. eToro targets $4 billion valuation in upcoming U.S. IPO (3 minute read) eToro is seeking to raise $500 million in a U.S. IPO at a ~$4 billion valuation, following a previously failed SPAC deal. The trading platform, which serves over 40 million users, reported strong 2024 financial results and plans to list on Nasdaq under the ticker "ETOR." 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports ) Toast eyes the throne: Challenging POS giants Micros and NCR (3 minute read) Toast, a restaurant-focused POS provider, has grown significantly since its 2015 valuation of $122 million, now boasting a market cap exceeding $20 billion. Despite this growth, industry leaders Micros and NCR still dominate, each holding approximately 25% market share. Toast's expansion into areas like payroll, HR, and marketing, along with processing $159 billion in payments in 2024, positions it to challenge these incumbents. With services in 134,000 restaurant locations and $832 million in annual recurring revenue, Toast is poised to reshape the POS landscape. 💻 Launches & Products Visa and Stripe's Bridge partner to streamline stablecoin cards (5 minute read) Visa is partnering with Stripe's Bridge to launch a platform that will allow fintechs to quickly start their own stablecoin-linked card programs for customers. The platform will enable developers to launch card programs in multiple countries at once, starting with six countries in Latin America, with plans to add support for countries in Europe, Africa, and Asia. Plaid's MCP server integrates Anthropic's AI assistant Claude (3 minute read) Plaid has embedded Anthropic's Claude AI into its Multi-Channel Partner (MCP) Server to help institutions automate complex workflows and improve customer interactions. The move enhances real-time decisioning across onboarding, fraud prevention, and support—key friction points in modern fintech operations. By fusing AI-driven insights with Plaid's data infrastructure, the company is positioning itself as a deeper intelligence layer in the fintech stack. Klarna becomes preferred payments partner for BigCommerce (5 minute read) BigCommerce, a leading provider of open, composable commerce solutions for B2C and B2B brands, retailers, manufacturers, and distributors, announced that Klarna, an AI-powered payments and commerce network, has become a global preferred payments partner. As a global preferred partner, Klarna will bring its flexible, interest-free payment options to merchants worldwide, enhancing the shopping experience and driving growth with one single integration. 🎁 Miscellaneous CFPB spurns BNPL rule (5 minute read) The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau has reversed its earlier push to hold buy now, pay later firms to the same standards as traditional credit card issuers. After announcing plans to cut its own workforce by 90%, the CFPB indicated it would shift enforcement and supervisory responsibilities to state regulators and step back from direct oversight of nonbank lenders. The move reflects a broader retreat from federal involvement in fintech regulation. AvidXchange agrees to be acquired by TPG in partnership with Corpay (3 minute read) AvidXchange, a fintech platform that automates accounts payable and payment workflows for mid-market businesses, is being taken private in a $2.5 billion all-cash acquisition led by TPG Capital, in partnership with Corpay. The move signals continued private equity interest in fintech infrastructure as public market performance lags behind growth expectations. Cashless wave sweeps Latin America amid mobile, fintech boom (5 minute read) Latin America is undergoing a dramatic shift in how money moves. However, it isn't traditional financial institutions that are leading the charge. Instead, mobile wallets and real-time payment systems are rapidly gaining ground across the region, and these 21st century solutions to the age-old problem of money movement are even overtaking banks in the race to digitize transactions. ⚡️ Quick Links Coinbase unveils internet payments protool for AI agents (5 minute read) Coinbase is resurrecting a long-buried protocol for sending payments over the Internet to create an agentic payments layer that requires no human intervention. NYC Fintech Mapping made by Rex at Cambrian VC (3 minute read) Rex Salisbury has created an interactive map highlighting fintech companies across New York City, inviting community contributions to ensure its accuracy and completeness. Coatue Tech fund backed by Jeff Bezos courts individual investors (3 minute read) Coatue Management is launching a tech-focused fund targeting individual investors, backed by family offices including Jeff Bezos and Michael Dell. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-12
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-12 Klarna pivots back to human customer service 👱, Coinbase to acquire Deribit 🤝, X to launch xMoney payment service 💲 📱 News & Trends Coinbase strikes $2.9 billion deal to buy crypto options giant Deribit (3 minute read) Coinbase will acquire Deribit, the world's largest bitcoin and ether options exchange, for $2.9 billion in cash and stock. The move marks Coinbase's biggest acquisition yet and a bold expansion into crypto derivatives. It follows rising trading volumes and a friendlier U.S. regulatory environment under the Trump administration. Ramp and Stripe launch stablecoin-backed corporate cards in LatAm (3 minute read) Ramp has partnered with Stripe to launch stablecoin-backed corporate cards designed to enable faster, lower-cost, and more predictable cross-border payments. The new product aims to protect businesses from currency volatility. It is initially rolling out in Latin America. X to launch xMoney payment service in 2025, advancing Musk's fintech vision (1 minute read) Elon Musk's X platform is preparing to launch xMoney, a peer-to-peer payment service that aims to turn X into an “everything app” combining social media, payments, and e-commerce. The new feature is a major component of Musk's broader vision to transform the platform into a fully integrated financial and social ecosystem. If successful, xMoney could challenge established players like PayPal and Venmo by offering payment capabilities directly inside a social network. Klarna pivots back to human customer service after AI-led cost cuts (5 minute read) Klarna's CEO, Sebastian Siemiatkowski, is launching a recruitment drive to ensure customers can always speak to a real person, as he believes the company's commitment to AI has its limits. The company is piloting a new cohort of customer-service agents who can work remotely, with the goal of eventually replacing the few thousand human agents Klarna currently outsources. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports PayPal's Embedded Finance Vision: Michelle Gill reveals how cash flow lending is reshaping SMB access to capital (22 minute podcast) PayPal's SVP of Lending, Michelle Gill, outlines the company's push to expand credit access for small businesses through its PayPal Working Capital and Business Loan products. With over $25 billion in capital already deployed, PayPal aims to use its real-time platform data to offer more personalized and accessible funding to underserved merchants. When chatbots replace search bars, who wins at checkout? (8 minute read) Perplexity started it in November of 2024 with Buy with Pro. Then OpenAI with ChatGPT followed in April 2025. These two LLMs are rapidly evolving from friendly chatbots into helpful AI shopping assistants capable of turning a 150-word search prompt into a purchase without the user ever visiting a store or hitting a buy button. It's a transformation that could fundamentally reshape retail. Open Finance is hanging by a thread (20 minute read) Open finance in the U.S. is at a crossroads as the CFPB plans to vacate and rewrite the key 1033 open banking rule, creating uncertainty after a decade of industry progress. Despite regulatory setbacks, market-driven innovation has made the U.S. the world's most vibrant open finance ecosystem, with aggregators and fintechs driving real consumer value. Regulatory approaches in Europe and elsewhere have underdelivered, often resulting in fragmentation and limited use cases, while the U.S. market has rapidly advanced in areas like payments, cashflow underwriting, and fraud prevention. 💻 Launches & Products Balance integrates RTP rails for B2B ACH payments (2 minute read) Balance, a financial infrastructure platform for B2B commerce, announced the launch of Instant Bank Connection, a new capability powered by Real-Time Payment (RTP) rails that simplifies ACH setup for buyers and speeds up payments to merchants, improving cash flow and reducing processing costs. Stripe introduces stablecoin accounts in more than 100 Countries (4 minute read) Stripe is launching USDC stablecoin payment and payout support across over 100 countries, expanding crypto-based transactions beyond traditional banking rails. Businesses can now hold balances in USDC via Stripe-hosted wallets, enabling faster global transfers and settlement. The move signals Stripe's growing interest in decentralized finance as a complement to its core payments infrastructure. 🎁 Miscellaneous Embedded Finance Unlocks Value for Underserved Small Business Sectors (5 minute read) Embedded payments are driving growth in historically underserved markets and transforming verticals like legal, financial services, accounting firms, nonprofits, and logistics operators. Payments processors are adapting to this rapid growth by partnering with ISVs to enable transactions where and when consumers prefer. Startup SimpleClosure sees boom in giving fellow startups peace of mind to die (5 minute read) SimpleClosure is capitalizing on a painful reality in the startup world: lots of companies need help shutting down cleanly. The YC-backed startup automates the messy legal and financial steps of winding down—from paying creditors to dissolving entities—cutting a process that usually drags for months down to just weeks. As more founders face failure in a tighter funding environment, SimpleClosure is quickly becoming the go-to option for a graceful exit. Fintech Payoneer, once worth $3.3 billion, suspends earnings guidance and seeks a buyer, sources say (3 minute read) Payoneer is reportedly in early talks to explore a potential sale, marking a dramatic shift for the once high-flying fintech firm that went public via SPAC in 2021. The company, which provides cross-border payment services for small businesses and freelancers, has seen its valuation fall from $3.3 billion to under $1 billion amid broader fintech market headwinds. Sources say discussions are preliminary and may not result in a deal, but the move signals increasing pressure on legacy fintech players to consolidate or pivot. ⚡️ Quick Links Why 74% of banking customers are ready to leave (5 minute read) Nearly three-quarters of banking customers are open to switching providers due to poor service, lack of personalization, and weak digital engagement. Top SaaS vendors on Ramp by new customer count and card spend in May 2025 (3 minute read) Ramp's May 2025 report highlights a surge in AI-focused SaaS adoption, with OpenAI leading in new customer acquisitions. Innovations ⚙️ and trends 📈 in financial markets 🌐 and fintech 💳. Subscribe Join 310,000 readers for one daily email Privacy Careers Advertise

text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-15 Chime files for IPO 🚀, Robinhood acquires WonderFi 🤝, Perplexity partners with PayPal 🤖 🎁 FREE Access to 31+ AI Models for TLDR FinTech readers (Sponsor) You.com is giving you its Team plan ($375 value) that includes: 🧠 Access to 31+ latest AI models in one place – includes ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Grok, DeepSeek, and more 📊 5 ARI reports – deep research agent that analyzes 400+ sources across private, public, and premium data to produce fully-customizable, McKinsey-style reports in < 5 mins. 🤖 Finance AI Agents – automate equity research, summarize earnings calls, run due diligence, competitive analysis, track regulations & compliance, and more According to benchmarks, ARI beats OpenAI's Deep Research on complex investment research. ↗️ Claim your FREE gift & try now. Offer expires 5/31.↗️ 📱 News & Trends Fintech company Chime files for Nasdaq IPO (4 minute read) Chime has officially filed to go public on the Nasdaq, revealing its ambitions to become one of the largest neobanks to hit the public markets. The filing shows Chime generated nearly $1 billion in revenue in 2023, with a net loss of $340 million, signaling a strong growth trajectory despite ongoing profitability challenges. The company plans to list under the ticker “CHME,” aiming to capitalize on investor appetite for consumer fintech platforms. Robinhood acquires WonderFi to expand crypto presence in Canada (3 minute read) Robinhood is acquiring Canadian crypto firm WonderFi for C$250 million (~$179 million USD) in an all-cash deal, marking a significant step in its international expansion. WonderFi operates regulated exchanges Bitbuy and Coinsquare, which processed over C$3.6 billion in trading volume in 2024. The acquisition aims to bolster Robinhood's presence in the Canadian crypto market and integrate WonderFi's team into its existing operations. Monzo prepares for IPO with £6–7bn valuation target (3 minute read) Monzo is gearing up for a potential IPO in early 2026, aiming for a valuation between £6 billion and £7 billion. The digital bank has enlisted Morgan Stanley to lead investor meetings and is considering London as the primary listing venue, though internal discussions about a U.S. listing persist. With over 11 million retail customers and 600,000 business accounts, Monzo reported its first annual profit in 2024 and is expanding into new markets, including Ireland. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Reverse-Engineering Chime's Success: Unpacking Their ARPAM-Boosting Strategies (10 minute read) Chime, a digital bank known for its no-fee consumer financial products, has officially filed its IPO paperwork, revealing strong growth. Revenue soared to $1.67 billion in 2024, while losses shrank to just $25 million, down from $203 million in 2023. With $519 million already banked in Q1 2025, Chime looks poised to hit $2 billion this year and flirt with profitability. Investors may be circling, but the S-1 still leaves plenty of blanks, including share pricing and insider sell-offs. Online sports betting is draining household savings (3 minute read) Legalized online sports betting is crowding out savings, not other entertainment. Tracking 230k U.S. households from 2018‑23, researchers found that almost 8 % now wager online, spending about $1,100 a year. Each $1 bet corresponds to $2 less flowing into investment accounts, yielding a 14% drop in net investing. Financially constrained bettors are hit hardest, with rising credit‑card debt, overdrafts, and shrinking credit lines—evidence that betting deepens, rather than substitutes for, household financial risk. One year after Synapse, how much have we really learned? (8 minute read) Despite shifts at the CFPB and potential rollback of key enforcement actions, there's a risk that regulatory fragmentation could increase compliance burden on banks and FinTechs. One year post-Synapse bankruptcy, we may not have learned as much as we should have or needed to. 💻 Launches & Products Nubank launches tap-to-pay Pix (2 minute read) Brazilian digital banking giant Nubank is rolling out a feature that lets customers tap their phones for in-store transactions via the country's Pix instant payments system. Customers who have a Pix key registered with Nubank and an Android device will be able to make contactless payments on any enabled point-of-sale terminal, with confirmation via biometrics or password. Perplexity partners with PayPal for in-chat AI shopping (3 minute read) Perplexity is integrating PayPal into its AI assistant, enabling users to shop directly within chat by asking questions like “What are the best noise-canceling headphones under $200?” The assistant will provide product recommendations and facilitate purchases through PayPal, streamlining the shopping experience. This partnership aims to simplify e-commerce by combining AI-driven search with seamless payment options. Square launches handheld POS device for on-the-go businesses (3 minute read) Square has introduced a new $399 handheld point-of-sale (POS) device designed for mobility and versatility in various business settings. It supports traditional card transactions, tap-to-pay, and inventory management, making it a comprehensive solution for businesses seeking a portable POS system. Square has also updated its POS app to include seven customizable modes tailored to different industries, such as Quick Service, Bar, and Retail. Plaid updates identity verification to combat GenAI fraud (2 minute read) Plaid has updated its identity verification (IDV) product to counter the threat posed by fraudsters who use generative artificial intelligence. New features added to the product include deepfake and synthetic media detection, facial duplicate detection to catch repeat fraud attempts, age estimation to flag impersonation risks, and risk-based flows that adapt in real time to streamline trusted users and escalate high-risk ones. 🎁 Miscellaneous JP Morgan could hit $1 trillion market cap within 3 years, says Wells Fargo (3 minute read) Wells Fargo analyst Mike Mayo projects JPMorgan could become the first bank to reach a $1 trillion valuation by 2028, joining tech giants like Apple and Nvidia. The forecast hinges on continued economic growth, easing regulation, and effective deployment of capital, though risks like a recession could delay that milestone. Coinbase to join S&P 500, replacing Discover Financial (3 minute read) Coinbase will be added to the S&P 500 index on May 19, replacing Discover Financial Services, which is being acquired by Capital One. This marks the first time a cryptocurrency exchange joins the benchmark index, reflecting the growing mainstream acceptance of digital assets. Following the announcement, Coinbase shares surged nearly 20%, signaling strong investor enthusiasm for the inclusion. The FinTech Power Rankings By Revenue Type (13 minute read) This article breaks down the core revenue models driving the fintech sector, ranking them by gross profit durability. SaaS and usage-based revenue models top the list for their sticky, recurring nature and high margins, followed by payments, AUM-based fees, trading, debit card interchange, float, insurance, and lending. ⚡️ Quick Links eToro IPO shares debut on Nasdaq under ETOR symbol (3 minute read) This IPO marks a significant moment for eToro's U.S. growth ambitions, as it looks to expand its footprint in a competitive retail trading market. CFPB drops lawsuit against Walmart and FinTech company Branch (2 minute read) The CFPB reportedly dropped a lawsuit against Walmart and Branch that it filed in December. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-19
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-19 Capital One completes Discover acquisition 🤝, Acorns acquires Earlybird 🚀, How Stripe built AI for payments 🧠 Ask Luca! “Help me reconcile invoices and payments” (Sponsor) Meet Luca from Leapfin, your AI copilot for revenue data exploration and reporting – built for Finance teams who want more control, faster answers, and fewer bottlenecks. Easily uncover potential discrepancies like revenue leakage, cash collection deficits, incomplete fee allocations, overused discounts – and much more. Make reconciliation faster and easier with the help of AI. Trusted by Canva, Reddit, and Poshmark. See how Luca works! 📱 News & Trends Capital One finalizes $35 billion Discover acquisition (3 minute read) Capital One has officially completed its $35 billion all-stock acquisition of Discover, creating the largest U.S. credit card issuer by loan volume. The merger, approved by federal regulators in April, grants Capital One control over Discover's payment network, positioning it to compete more directly with Visa and Mastercard. While the deal faced opposition from some lawmakers and consumer advocates, Capital One asserts that the combined entity will deliver enhanced products and experiences for consumers and merchants. Acorns acquires family wealth and digital memory platform Earlybird (3 minute read) Acorns has acquired Earlybird, a fintech platform focused on helping families invest and preserve memories for their children's future. The move expands Acorns' footprint in generational wealth-building by combining Earlybird's custodial investment accounts and memory-sharing tools with its own micro-investing services. This acquisition signals a deeper push by Acorns into family-focused financial planning. Evolve Bank accused of fraudulently reversing $1.2 million in ACH transactions (5 minute read) Evolve Bank & Trust faces a lawsuit from fintech platform Cheq that alleges the bank unlawfully reversed over $1.2 million in ACH transactions between April 9 and 11. Cheq claims Evolve misused ACH reversal codes, falsely indicating customer requests, to reclaim funds without proper authorization. This legal action adds to Evolve's ongoing challenges, including a significant data breach and scrutiny over missing customer funds linked to its partnership with the now-bankrupt Synapse. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports AI breaks every moat in fintech (15 minute read) If it takes weeks to build products that would have taken months or years, building a “finance app” with features won't differentiate you. This destroys moats, and AI can make delivery happen 100x faster, but it can't replace taste, experience, the need for security, and the human touch. Dismiss it at your peril, because this isn't if, it's when. Today's tiny, niche, and not very complete products are tomorrow's giants. How Stripe built AI but for payments (8 minute read) Stripe has recently unveiled a groundbreaking development: a transformer-based payments foundation model, akin to a "GPT for payments." In simple terms, it built an AI but for payments. The new model, trained on tens of billions of transactions, learns the "language" of payments. It processes transactions as sequences of tokens - much like words in a sentence - and creates dense, general-purpose vector embeddings for each. 4 nuggets for banks in fintech Chime's IPO filing (6 minute read) Chime's S-1 revealed how it carved out a stronghold among the 79 million U.S. households earning less than $100,000 a year. The company is now setting its sights on an additional 31 million households earning between $100K and $200K. It says this next segment still struggles with traditional banking fees and is underserved by legacy financial institutions. To deepen engagement, Chime plans to layer in more products like retirement accounts and investing tools, signaling a long-term strategy to grow share of wallet. 💻 Launches & Products MoonPay launches stablecoin card with Mastercard (3 minute read) MoonPay is rolling out a new debit card that lets users spend stablecoins, like USDC, anywhere Mastercard is accepted. The offering combines MoonPay's crypto payment infrastructure with Mastercard's network, enabling real-time conversion of stablecoins into fiat at the point of sale. The launch marks a step toward mainstream crypto usage in daily transactions, targeting both crypto-native and traditional consumers. Peek hits #1 on Product Hunt (2 minute read) Peek, a new money app that helps users track spending by simply texting it, just pulled off a rare feat: 22x growth to 5K downloads, and the #1 spot on Product Hunt. The app turns financial planning into a lightweight, text-first experience that feels more like chatting than budgeting. The momentum signals growing demand for more human, conversational tools in personal finance. Monzo launches Undo Payments tool (2 minute read) Monzo has launched a new feature called Undo Payments aimed at enhancing confidence and safety for both personal and business users making bank transfers. Nearly one in three Brits admitted to sending money to the wrong person or sending the incorrect amount within the last year. The new tool is designed to tackle this growing issue and offer a moment of pause in what can be an otherwise irreversible process. 🎁 Miscellaneous Capital One settles $425 million lawsuit over savings account interest rates (3 minute read) Capital One has agreed to a $425 million settlement to resolve a nationwide lawsuit alleging it misled 360 Savings account holders by not informing them about higher-yielding 360 Performance Savings accounts. The settlement includes $300 million for lost interest and $125 million for current account holders, covering customers since September 2019. While Capital One did not admit wrongdoing, the case coincides with its pending $35.3 billion acquisition of Discover Financial Services. Revolut begins internal mortgage testing and more than doubles profit (3 minute read) Revolut has started testing its own mortgage product internally, signaling a deeper move into personal finance as it eyes long-term growth in the UK home loan market. The neobank more than doubled its profit last year, reaching £438 million, and says mortgages are a natural extension of its ambition to become a “financial super app.” If successful, the mortgage launch could help Revolut better lock in high-value users and diversify its revenue streams beyond payments and FX. ⚡️ Quick Links US BNPL transactions hit $175 billion amid shifting consumer demands (3 minute read) The traditional pillars of consumer credit are undergoing a profound transformation as “pay later” options, once a niche for specific shoppers, are now redefining how Americans spend and borrow. Fiserv's Clover faces growth slowdown amid strategic international expansion (3 minute read) Fiserv's Clover POS platform grew 8% YoY in Q1 2025, down from 14% in Q4, as a major client migration wrapped up. Inside Revolut's Growth Engine: A Conversation with Index Ventures Partner Martin Mignot (6 minute read) Revolut's journey to super-app status was no accident—it stemmed from relentless iteration, deep founder-product obsession, and a culture that prioritized speed over structure. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-22
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-22 Brex partners with Zip 🤝, Klarna doubles Q1 losses 📈, Visa streamlines fintech integrations 🧠 Payment industry insights: can AI solve problems faster than it creates them? (Sponsor) AI is both a threat and an opportunity for the payments industry. While AI enables new forms of fraud, top merchants are using machine learning to detect suspicious patterns, recover failed payments without adding customer friction, and increase approval rates. This guide by FlexPay breaks down: The role of AI and ML in reducing chargebacks and unauthorized transactions, recovering failed payments, and more Using AI-powered authentication methods to improve security without degrading the customer experience Preventing fraud before it happens with predictive analytics Regulatory and compliance considerations that merchants must navigate in an AI-driven world Get your free copy now 📱 News & Trends Klarna doubles Q1 losses amid rising credit defaults and IPO delay (3 minute read) Klarna reported a net loss of $99 million in Q1 2025, driven by a 17% increase in customer credit losses amid economic uncertainty in the US, more than doubling from the previous year. Despite a 15% year-over-year revenue growth to $701 million and a 33% surge in US revenue, the company has paused its planned US IPO due to market instability and tariff concerns. Klarna is focusing on cost-saving measures, including a 39% workforce reduction over two years and leveraging artificial intelligence to cut expenses. Bank startup Airwallex valued at $6.2Bn with new funding (3 minute read) Airwallex has raised $200 million in fresh funding, pushing its valuation to $6.2 billion and reaffirming investor confidence in the cross-border payments startup. The round was led by existing backer Lone Pine Capital, with support from Sequoia and Tencent, signaling strong momentum despite a broader tech funding slowdown. Airwallex, which enables businesses to manage international payments and finances, now plans to accelerate expansion in the U.S. and introduce new AI-driven financial tools. Brex partners with former competitor Zip to streamline enterprise spend management (3 minute read) Brex has announced a strategic partnership with procurement startup Zip to integrate its virtual corporate cards directly into Zip's platform. The collaboration aims to streamline procurement and payment workflows for enterprises, prevent unauthorized spending, and simplify global operations with a unified card program. This move is part of Brex's broader strategy to diversify revenue streams beyond interchange fees and reduce cash burn ahead of a potential IPO. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Circle S-1 and the future of money (5 minute read) A few weeks ago, Circle filed its S-1 to go public on the NYSE, offering the first real look into its financials. This article analyzes the S-1 filing to uncover the strengths and weaknesses of Circle's business model and place it in the broader context of the stablecoin industry. Chime IPO: S-1 breakdown reveals strong growth and interchange reliance (3 minute read) Chime filed to go public with $1.67 billion in revenue for 2024, 8.6 million active users, and $251 in annual revenue per user. The neobank's business model leans heavily on Durbin-exempt interchange, which drives ~90% of revenue, posing regulatory risk. Despite thin profit margins, Chime posted $13 million in net income and is pursuing a dual-class share structure for founder control. How Open Banking is reshaping how we interact with financial services (8 minute read) Open Banking (OB) isn't a feature - it's the blueprint for banks to stay relevant in an APIsed economy. However, exposing a few APIs is not innovation. OB is reshaping how we access and interact with financial services. At its core, it's about unlocking data and making it securely available through modern infrastructure rails called APIs. However, the impact goes far beyond banking. OB is becoming the key enabler of today's two most dominant business models: Platform economics and Embedded finance 💻 Launches & Products Google adds AI-powered shopping features for discovery and easy check-out (3 minute read) Google has introduced several AI-driven shopping enhancements within its Search platform's AI Mode. These include personalized product recommendations, dynamic price tracking with automated checkout via Google Pay, and a virtual try-on feature that allows users to visualize clothing on themselves by uploading full-length photos. These features aim to streamline the online shopping experience by providing tailored suggestions and simplifying the purchasing process. Visa launches program to streamline fintech integrations (2 minute read) Visa is launching the Visa Commercial Integrated Partners program to help fintechs more easily embed its commercial card solutions into their platforms. The initiative offers faster onboarding, co-marketing opportunities, and access to Visa's APIs to support B2B payments and expense management tools. The goal is to accelerate the adoption of embedded finance across sectors like SaaS, neobanking, and procurement tech. 🎁 Miscellaneous Block says it's extended over $100bn in credit to consumers and SMBs (5 minute read) Since the launch of its credit products, Block has extended over $100 billion in credit while keeping its loss rates “low.” The company uses a combination of near real-time data, technology, and customer-first design, rather than traditional credit scores. Block has provided over $22 billion in small business financing through Square Loans, provided nearly $15 billion in short-term credit to more than 9 million active members through Cash App Borrow, and served over 20 million active consumers through Afterpay. Visa launches AR manager tool, targeting suppliers' biggest business payments bottleneck (3 minute read) Virtual cards offer buyers enhanced control, security, and working capital benefits, but they can impose manual processing burdens on suppliers, leading to delays in payment reconciliation. Visa's launch of its AR Manager tool automates accounts receivable processes for suppliers, without requiring development work from banks or suppliers. Revolut to invest €1 Billion in France, pursue banking license (3 minute read) Revolut will invest over €1 billion in France and apply for a local banking license, setting up its Western European HQ in Paris and hiring 200+ staff over three years. It currently has 5 million French users and aims to double that to 10 million by 2026 while expanding offerings like crypto, loans, and trading. The move is part of a broader global licensing push, with 10 applications underway as the company scales beyond its Lithuanian and UK permits. ⚡️ Quick Links JPMorgan will let clients buy Bitcoin, says CEO Jamie Dimon (3 minute read) JPMorgan will now allow clients to buy Bitcoin, a notable reversal from CEO Jamie Dimon's long-standing skepticism. AI agents now choose 80% of new databases on Neon, thanks to app builders like V0 and Replit (3 minute read) According to Wing VC's Tanay Jaipuria, 80% of new Neon databases are created by AI agents, not humans, due to defaults in tools like V0 and Replit. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-26
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-26 Apple Pay gains popularity 🚀, Revolut CEO raises $250M fund 💲, How AI agents are reshaping commerce 🤖 📱 News & Trends Apple Pay climbs the ranks as shoppers seek speed & security (3 minute read) Apple Pay is now the fifth most widely available online payment option, present on 5.25% of websites globally, surpassing Shopify Pay and Google Pay. Its growth was driven by consumers' desire for faster, more secure checkouts, with features like biometric authentication and tokenized transactions enhancing privacy. Adoption is particularly strong in regions like Saudi Arabia and U.S. states such as Hawaii and Wyoming. Revolut CEO wants to revolutionize Venture Capital with $250M AI-Powered Fund (5 minute read) Nik Storonsky, founder and CEO of Revolut, has just secured $250 million for his venture capital firm QuantumLight, marking yet another step in bringing artificial intelligence to investment decision-making. The London-based firm, co-founded with CEO Ilya Kondrashov, is redefining how venture investments are made through a data-driven approach that aims to minimize human bias. US to kill off the penny (2 minute read) The US Treasury has confirmed plans to phase out the penny. One-cent coins will no longer be produced from next year. There are around 114 billion pennies in circulation, says the Treasury, with 3.2 billion minted just last year. According to the US Mint's annual report, the cost of making and distributing a one-cent coin was 3.69 cents in 2024, a sharp rise from the previous year. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Moving from ML to LLM/GPT in payments: Stripe sets the standard (5 minute read) Stripe is embedding large language models across its payments ecosystem to supercharge fraud detection, customer support, and developer tooling. Its legacy systems had a 59% detection rate for attacks on large users - Stripe's new GPT-powered model turbo-charged this to 97%. The shift marks a defining moment in AI-first fintech infrastructure, with Stripe setting a new industry standard. Mercury's high-risk, high-reward strategy expands into consumer banking (3 minute read) Mercury has launched Mercury Personal, a consumer banking product that offers debit cards, auto-transfers, and up to $5 million in FDIC insurance to complement its startup-focused services. The expansion comes as the company faces regulatory scrutiny tied to its banking partners, prompting a renewed focus on compliance. Despite these risks, Mercury raised $300 million in March, doubling its valuation to $3.5 billion. The checkout page is dead: AI agents are reshaping commerce (4 minute read) The traditional checkout page is becoming obsolete as AI agents begin to handle purchases autonomously. With features like Google's "shop with AI Mode," transactions can occur before a user even visits a website, shifting critical functions like fraud detection and conversion optimization upstream to points of intent such as ads and chatbots. This evolution challenges merchants to adapt their strategies for a future where AI agents, rather than humans, are the primary consumers. 💻 Launches & Products Curve Pay challenges Apple Pay with iOS launch in EEA (3 minute read) Curve Pay has become the first wallet in the EEA to compete directly with Apple Pay on iOS. Apple has opened up its NFC technology under EU regulation. Curve Pay now allows iPhone users to make contactless payments, expanding consumer choice and challenging Apple's payments dominance in the region. Chestnut, the first AI mortgage lender (3 minute read) Chestnut is launching as the first AI-powered mortgage lender, aiming to streamline how Americans qualify and apply for home loans. The platform uses LLM agents to underwrite, price, and fund mortgages in real time, completing approvals in minutes instead of weeks. By eliminating human loan officers, Chestnut claims it can offer faster decisions and cheaper loans, which could resonate in a high-rate, low-trust lending environment. Affirm and Kayak expand pay-later partnership to Canada (3 minute read) Affirm has extended its partnership with travel search engine Kayak to Canada, allowing approved travelers to split the cost of flights, accommodations, and car rentals into monthly payments through Affirm at checkout on Kayak's Canadian website. This expansion builds on their U.S. collaboration that began in 2023 and reflects growing demand for flexible payment options among Canadian travelers. The move also aligns with Kayak's broader innovation efforts, including the launch of Kayak.ai, a generative AI-powered travel assistant designed to provide real-time, bookable travel recommendations. 🎁 Miscellaneous Major U.S. Banks Explore Joint Stablecoin Initiative (3 minute read) JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Citigroup, and Wells Fargo are reportedly in early discussions to develop a joint U.S. dollar-pegged stablecoin. The initiative, still in its conceptual phase, aims to create a digital asset usable by a broader banking consortium, potentially including institutions beyond the co-owners of the Clearing House and Early Warning Services. This move aligns with the crypto-friendly stance of the Trump administration, which advocates for integrating digital assets into the traditional banking system to bolster the U.S. dollar's global dominance. Small business, big opportunity: Square, Stripe, Ramp, and Brex expand into banking services (4 minute read) Fintech companies like Square, Stripe, Ramp, and Brex are broadening their services to become comprehensive banking partners for small businesses. By introducing offerings such as checking and savings accounts, credit cards, and cash management tools, these firms aim to capture operational deposits and provide integrated financial solutions. This strategic shift positions them to compete directly with traditional banks by embedding financial services into their existing platforms. The AI Middleman Expanding in the Consumer-Bond Bonanza (3 minute read) Pagaya Technologies, an AI-driven consumer-lending firm, is entering the "buy now, pay later" (BNPL) market by issuing a $300 million bond backed by point-of-sale loans from Klarna. This marks Pagaya's shift from bundling unsecured personal and auto loans to tapping into the fast-growing BNPL segment, which represented 8% of holiday purchases last year. Unlike Affirm, which primarily serves high-credit-score customers, Pagaya uses AI to underwrite "second look" loans for consumers with slightly lower credit scores, enabling companies like Klarna to expand customer approvals. TD Bank to cut 2% of workforce, wind down $3B portfolio (3 minute read) TD Bank will lay off approximately 2% of its workforce, or about 2,000 employees, as part of a restructuring plan initiated in the second quarter of 2025. The bank anticipates incurring restructuring charges between C$600 million and C$700 million over the coming quarters, aiming for annual savings up to C$650 million. Additionally, TD is winding down a $3 billion U.S. point-of-sale financing portfolio to focus on core businesses and reinvest in digital and AI capabilities. ⚡️ Quick Links Commitment as leverage: the third tool to transform businesses (3 minute read) The prevailing VC–PE spectrum of “code vs. capital” misses a critical third lever: commitment. The Chime test: Fintech's Rorschach moment (5 minute read) Chime is like a Rorschach test, reflecting observers' biases and beliefs about fintech's future. Credit Card Installments Have Reshaped Impulse Buying (5 minute read) In the past year, more than one-third of shoppers spent at least $250 on an emergency purchase, with the same share making an impulse purchase of that size in the last three months. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-29
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-05-29 Apple expands Tap to Pay 🤳, Block to enable Bitcoin payments 🪙, Neobank metrics comparison 🏦 Free expert-led course: Learn how to spot and stop KYB fraud (Sponsor) Business fraud is a growing problem. In the U.K. alone, 4,000 new businesses are registered each day, and roughly 1 in 5 are fake . Is your KYB process catching these fakes? This expert-led course breaks down how to catch and stop business fraud using an adaptable KYB strategy, without slowing down onboarding for legitimate businesses or creating unwanted friction. Enroll now to learn about: The 4 biggest misconceptions about business fraud How fraudsters pull off business impersonation schemes Building a signals-based KYB strategy to detect impersonation, uncovering fraud rings, and monitor ongoing risk 🙋 The course is delivered by Persona's Know Your Business (KYB) product lead. 👉 Enroll now (free) 👈 📱 News & Trends Block to enable Bitcoin payments on Square terminals (2 minute read) Block will roll out Bitcoin payment support across its Square point-of-sale terminals starting later this year, with full availability in 2026 pending regulatory approval. Customers will pay by scanning a QR code, using the Lightning Network to complete transactions. This builds on Block's broader Bitcoin strategy, which includes Cash App crypto features, the Bitkey wallet, and the Proto mining initiative. Stablecoin issuer Circle, holders seek $624 Million in IPO (3 minute read) Investors in Circle, the company behind the USDC stablecoin, are planning to sell up to $624 million worth of shares in a long-awaited IPO. The offering could value Circle at about $5 billion, significantly less than the $9 billion valuation from a previously scrapped SPAC deal. This move comes amid renewed interest in regulated crypto infrastructure, with Circle positioning itself as a more transparent alternative in the wake of past industry turmoil. Apple expands Tap to Pay on iPhone to eight more European countries (3 minute read) Apple has rolled out Tap to Pay on iPhone to eight additional European countries, including Germany, Italy, and the Netherlands. The feature allows merchants to accept contactless payments directly on iPhones without additional hardware. Apple is partnering with major payment platforms like Adyen, Revolut, and Stripe to support the expansion. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports How AI is reshaping accounting (38 minute podcast) This podcast episode explores AI in accounting and how automation is reshaping the finance function, from ERP integrations and auditability to real-time data for CFOs. It discusses why accounting is more complex than it seems, what makes it uniquely suited for AI, and how founders are building defensible products in a competitive space. Cash flow underwriting: The key to small banks' small business lending ambitions (10 minute read) Community banks and credit unions have been losing ground to online lenders due to slower processes and reliance on traditional credit scores. Online lenders win business by providing faster decisions and funds, which is crucial for small businesses facing cash flow gaps, even if it means accepting less favorable terms. Community banks can regain market share by leveraging open banking and cash flow underwriting, allowing them to quickly and accurately assess risk using real-time bank data rather than just credit scores. Bankers view: Stablecoins, deposits, and the future of payments (10 minute read) While stablecoins present novel tech, the notion that they will supplant established retail banking relationships is a bunch of “hooky”. Big banks aren't just watching from the sidelines - they are best positioned to integrate this new rail, much like they've absorbed countless payment innovations before. From the bank's perspective, stablecoin functionality looks like just another payment type that can be integrated into their existing secure infrastructure. 💻 Launches & Products Adyen enables Tap-to-Pay on iPhone in seven new European markets (2 minute read) Adyen has officially enabled Tap to Pay on iPhone in seven new markets: Belgium, Croatia, Cyprus, Denmark, Iceland, Luxembourg, and Malta. The functionality allows businesses to enable iPhones to accept contactless payments without the need to purchase or manage additional hardware. Visa wants to let AI agents pay without showing a checkout page (2 minute read) Visa's CEO said enabling AI agents to complete purchases autonomously is now the company's top strategic priority. The idea: users will give spending instructions to AI agents, who will transact using pre-authorized “tokens,” eliminating traditional checkout steps. This shift could redefine e-commerce by making payments invisible, faster, and agent-driven. Tink and Adyen bring Pay-by-Bank to Vodafone customers in Germany (2 minute read) Tink, a Visa Solution, and Adyen, a global financial technology platform of choice for leading businesses, have announced a partnership with Vodafone, a leading telecoms company, to offer their customers in Germany the option to pay their prepaid tariff and outstanding postpaid balances using Pay by Bank. 🎁 Miscellaneous European Commission looking into Visa and Mastercard fees (2 minute read) The European Commission is reportedly looking into fees charged by Visa and Mastercard in a move that could lead to a formal probe. The competition regulator sent requests for information to market participants last week, asking about fees they impose on financial institutions that provide services to retailers using the Visa and Mastercard networks. Neobank comparison - who's leading and why (5 minute read) The neobank race is heating up. Based on the latest metrics - customers, revenue, profitability, and valuation - each player is taking a distinct path. Nubank leads in customers (114M), Cash App posts the highest revenue ($16.2B), and Revolut has scaled fast (50M users, $4B revenue). Apple stopped over $2 billion in potentially fraudulent transactions in 2024 (2 minute read) Since launching in 2008, the App Store has been a safe and trusted place for users and a vibrant marketplace for developers to grow their businesses around the world. ⚡️ Quick Links JPMorgan overhauls wealth unit, merges U.S. and international businesses (3 minute read) JPMorgan Chase is merging its U.S. and international wealth management divisions under Kristin Lemkau to unify strategy and drive growth across geographies. CFPB's Move to Rescind Rule 1033 Creates Uncertainty in Open Banking (5 minute read) CFPB's potential reversal could reshape open banking and data-sharing practices. Offer expires 6/15 ↗️ 📱 News & Trends Circle files for IPO (6 minute read) Stablecoin giant Circle has officially filed to go public, aiming to raise up to $624 million at a valuation near $6.7 billion when fully diluted. The fintech plans to trade under the ticker “CRCL” on the NYSE, joining a wave of public offerings that includes eToro and Chime. Backed by interest from Ark Invest and reportedly BlackRock, the offering positions Circle as a foundational player in the emerging digital dollar infrastructure. Global Payments sells payroll and HR unit to Acrisure (3 minute read) Global Payments has agreed to sell its payroll and human capital management business to insurance and fintech firm Acrisure for an undisclosed sum. The move reflects Global Payments' strategy to streamline operations and focus on its core payments business. The divested unit had generated about $100 million in annual revenue. Stripe debuts expansion of money movement capabilities (3 minute read) Stripe has launched new enhancements to its money movement tools, enabling platforms to more easily send, receive, and store funds globally. The updates support use cases like instant payouts, global disbursements, and embedded wallets—aimed at helping platforms scale financial services without becoming regulated entities. Stripe says the new capabilities reduce complexity and regulatory friction for businesses building on its infrastructure. Chime plans IPO launch at ~$11B valuation as soon as Monday (3 minute read) Chime is preparing to go public as early as Monday with a projected valuation of about $11 billion, down from its $25 billion peak in 2021. The fintech disclosed Q1 2025 revenue of $518.7 million and net income of $12.9 million, supported by banking partnerships with Bancorp and Stride Bank. The IPO will be led by Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan, with Chime planning to list under the ticker CHYM on the Nasdaq. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Agent payments stack: AI agents will need wallets, not checkouts (4 minute read) As AI agents begin transacting autonomously, traditional checkouts will be replaced by embedded agent wallets that authorize purchases with set constraints. a16z envisions a future where developers provide "intent" and constraints (like budget or merchant type), and AI agents handle fulfillment through APIs or delegated authority. This shift will require a new payments stack—secure, programmable, and agent-native—comprising wallet infrastructure, agent-level authorization, and developer frameworks. Trust me, I'm an algorithm: How fintech is rebuilding customer confidence in the age of AI (6 minute read) Fintechs are being pushed to prove that AI-driven personalization is not just smart, but clearly beneficial and easy to understand. The industry is shifting toward transparent systems that show users why a recommendation is made—rebuilding trust one clear explanation at a time. Instead of replacing human connection, AI is being used to deepen it by making every interaction feel more timely, relevant, and tailored. CB Insights unveils stablecoin market map highlighting 172 high-growth companies (3 minute read) CB Insights, in partnership with Stablecon, has released a comprehensive market map that identifies 172 high-momentum companies driving the stablecoin ecosystem. The map categorizes these firms across various segments, providing insights into the rapidly evolving stablecoin landscape. Stablecoins are a new platform (20 minute read) People still see stablecoins as a new payments rail, when they should be seeing it as a layer above all the others, and, eventually, we'll go fully stablecoin-native. This article talks about why stablecoins are the infrastructure layer (not just a new rail), the stablecoin gold rush and regulatory unlock, and use cases across the stack. 💻 Launches & Products Plaid expands business banking data coverage in US market (4 minute read) Plaid is widening access to business banking data across thousands of U.S. institutions, aiming to better support fintechs and enterprise partners that serve small and medium-sized businesses. The expansion includes broader coverage for commercial checking, savings, and credit accounts, plus enhanced capabilities to support underwriting, cash flow insights, and payments. This move positions Plaid to become a foundational layer for the next wave of SMB-focused financial services. Hopper Technology Solutions and Virgin Australia lead airline retail revolution with Artificial Intelligence-powered fintech (4 minute read) Virgin Australia is introducing a suite of AI-enabled fintech features designed to give travelers more control over disruptions and cancellations as part of a broader shift toward retail-style airline experiences. Built in partnership with Hopper Technology Solutions, the tools—like Cancel for Any Reason and Flight Disruption Guarantee—reflect a growing industry push to blend flexibility with revenue innovation. As airlines search for new ways to drive loyalty and monetize beyond seats, fintech is quickly becoming a core pillar of digital strategy. 🎁 Miscellaneous As fintech funding falls to seven-year low, founders debate bootstrapping vs VC (3 minute read) Global fintech funding dropped to its lowest level since 2017, sparking renewed debate among founders about whether to raise venture capital or bootstrap. Some argue that bootstrapping ensures discipline, while others believe that venture backing can accelerate product-market fit if deployed wisely. There has been a significant pullback in early-stage VC deals, suggesting startups will need to focus more on profitability and resilience. Banks, fintechs halt mortgage lending in California wildfire zones (4 minute read) Some of the country's largest mortgage lenders are pausing originations in parts of California due to rising wildfire risks and escalating insurance costs. Lenders like Rocket Mortgage and Wells Fargo are pulling back from high-risk zones, while fintechs are increasingly using climate data to assess long-term property risk. This shift is leaving homebuyers in fire-prone areas with fewer financing options, underscoring a growing divide between insurable and uninsurable markets in climate-vulnerable regions. Fintech groups oppose state BNPL rules (3 minute read) Major fintech industry groups are pushing back against new state-level regulations on buy now, pay later (BNPL) services, arguing they create inconsistent standards and could stifle innovation. Groups like the Financial Technology Association warn that treating BNPL like traditional credit may burden consumers with unnecessary disclosures. They advocate for a federal framework instead, highlighting the popularity and accessibility of BNPL offerings. ⚡️ Quick Links Paypal Ventures appoints Ian Cox Moya as new Managing Partner (3 minute read) PayPal has named Ian Cox Moya as managing partner of its venture arm, replacing James Loftus, who departed to join Velocity Global. Brian Armstrong on Coinbase's internal venture model (2 minute read) Coinbase incubated BASE by treating it like a startup within Coinbase—similar to a YC-style team. FICO scores come under scrutiny and lenders eye new ways to assess risk (4 minute read) Traditional credit scoring models, like those based on payment histories relied upon by FICO, create credit access gaps for millions of consumers, including thin-file individuals who lack sufficient credit history. text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-05 Robinhood shares hit all time high 🚀, PayPal launches physical card 💳, Revolut rolls out ATMs in Spain 💰 Are You Still Just Asking ChatGPT to Write Emails? (Sponsor) AI is reshaping functions across top firms—from finance to operations. But for all the hype, most organizations are just beginning to unlock its real business value. You'll demystify today's most important AI tools and learn just enough Python to streamline workflows, automate tasks, and make smarter decisions. No coding experience needed—you'll get step-by-step guidance from Columbia Business School faculty. 👉 Save $300 with code TLDR + $200 off for early enrollment before June 16. Save Your Seat 📱 News & Trends Robinhood shares hit all-time high amid crypto boom (3 minute read) Robinhood's stock hit a new record of $71.72, surpassing its post-IPO high, as Bitcoin and broader crypto markets surged. Analysts credit the rally to strong execution and growth in crypto offerings, including acquisitions of WonderFi and Bitstamp. Robinhood is also exploring new products like prediction markets and is positioned to scale its crypto business alongside its existing equities and options platforms. PayPal launches physical credit card for in-store BNPL (3 minute read) PayPal has launched a new physical credit card in partnership with Synchrony and Mastercard that allows U.S. customers to use PayPal Credit for in-store purchases. This expands PayPal Credit's reach beyond online use, making it available wherever Mastercard is accepted. The rollout is part of PayPal's broader push to offer omnichannel financial services and compete more directly with traditional banks. Payoneer sees U.S.–China tariff truce as boost for global trade (3 minute read) Payoneer CEO John Caplan called the 90-day tariff truce between the U.S. and China a win for global trade, especially small U.S. businesses and Chinese exporters. Despite pulling earnings guidance due to earlier trade tensions, Caplan defended the decision and noted early Q2 results remain strong. Payoneer is now targeting acquisitions under $100 million to grow its B2B segment, the company's fastest-growing line of business. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports F-Prime's fintech bets eye IPOs, with Stripe, Klarna, Chime in the mix (3 minute read) Venture firm F-Prime Capital is poised to see major liquidity events as portfolio companies Stripe, Klarna, and Chime prepare for potential IPOs. These three fintechs alone have collectively raised over $25 billion and could significantly boost returns if they go public. F-Prime, which has focused heavily on fintech, healthcare, and enterprise software, may benefit from a resurgence in the IPO market after a two-year lull. Fintech's future: scaled winners and emerging disruptors (6 minute read) BCG's 2025 report shows that just 5% of fintechs account for over 50% of the sector's revenue, with winners pulling ahead on scale, monetization, and profitability. Meanwhile, early-stage disruptors are gaining momentum in B2B SaaS, infrastructure, and AI-native fintech, particularly in underserved verticals like SMBs and cross-border finance. To succeed, founders are urged to focus on operational leverage, product monetization, and partnerships with incumbents. Taste is a compound advantage (4 minute read) Taste isn't just design polish—it's a philosophy encoded in 10,000 small decisions. Stripe, Notion, and Spotify succeed not by shipping more, but by caring more. In a world where AI can build anything, Taste is what makes a product feel right—and what turns teams into movements. 💻 Launches & Products Visa announces key partnerships with Klarna, Zilch to launch flexible debit cards (2 minute read) Visa announced plans to launch a pilot for FDIC-insured debit card with Klarna and roll out Zilch's first physical card at Money20/20 Europe in Amsterdam. The card will allow users to make a purchase as usual with a card at checkout and it will be integrated into the Klarna ecosystem. Users will be allowed to choose their payment options, such as Pay in 4 and Pay Later. Revolut rolls out ATMs in Spain (2 minute read) Revolut is launching its own branded ATMs in Spain, marking a significant step toward expanding its physical presence in Europe. The machines allow users to withdraw cash, view balances, and access account details via a QR code, without needing a card. This move signals a deeper commitment to local infrastructure and could pave the way for similar deployments across other European markets. Venmo introduces new debit card benefits and payment options as rival Cash App struggles (4 minute read) Venmo is expanding beyond its roots in peer-to-peer payments by enhancing its debit card with early direct deposit, fee-free ATM withdrawals, and new bill-splitting features. The overhaul also adds retailer rewards and deeper money management tools, signaling a push to become a more full-service financial platform. These updates arrive as Cash App faces headwinds from layoffs, regulatory pressure, and slowing user growth. 🎁 Miscellaneous Banking is getting easier. Is it riskier? (6 minute read) New research suggests that the convenience of digital banking is directly increasing the speed and severity of bank runs. Using account-level data from a top-30 U.S. bank, Chicago Booth's Juliane Begenau and Erik Stafford found that customers with mobile app access were significantly quicker to withdraw funds during periods of stress — particularly wealthier clients with more assets. This implies that as banks lean into digital convenience, they may unintentionally heighten fragility, making fast withdrawals a systemic vulnerability in times of panic. Consumers are financing their groceries. What does it say about the economy? (7 minute read) A growing number of Americans are using buy now, pay later (BNPL) services like Klarna and Affirm to purchase groceries, signaling a shift from occasional splurges to financing basic survival needs. Major retailers like Walmart and Instacart are seeing BNPL usage rise sharply, with some customers splitting bills under $50 and doing so weekly, effectively turning these apps into paycheck advance tools. Experts warn this trend reflects deeper financial fragility and could trap low-income shoppers in a cycle of debt, as short-term installment plans mask the long-term cost of living paycheck to paycheck. Remittance tax proposal sparks backlash over equity and safety risks (3 minute read) A proposed 3.5% tax on remittances sent by non-U.S. citizens could raise $22B over a decade but faces sharp criticism from fintechs and advocates. Opponents warn it will inflate costs for 50M+ people, drive fund flows into informal or crypto channels, and disproportionately harm small businesses that offer remittance services. Groups like IAMTN and the American FinTech Council argue the measure risks financial exclusion and could reverse progress on safe, regulated money transfers. Google launches agentic shopping experience (3 minute read) Google is introducing a shopping assistant that can automatically complete purchases when an item meets a user's criteria, including price thresholds. Users can describe what they want—like a dress for a fall wedding—and set preferences such as style, fit, or budget, and the AI handles product discovery and monitoring until it finds the right match. This shift toward autonomous shopping marks a step closer to true agentic commerce, where AI doesn't just recommend—it acts. ⚡️ Quick Links Wells Fargo escapes Fed-imposed asset cap after seven years (3 minute read) The Federal Reserve has lifted its 2018 asset cap on WELLS FARGO, freeing the bank to grow its balance sheet, take on deposits, and expand lending and Wall Street operations. Verizon and Trustly unlock pay by bank at retail stores (2 minute read) Verizon and Trustly have partnered to extend pay-by-bank options to Verizon's brick-and-mortar retail stores, allowing customers to use their bank accounts directly to purchase devices, upgrades, and accessories. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-09
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-09 Circle stocks triples after IPO 🚀, Pricing agenetic models 💸, Intuit launches agentic AI experiences 🤖 📱 News & Trends Uber considers using stablecoins for cross-border money transfers (3 minute read) Uber is exploring the use of stablecoins to pay drivers and partners across borders more efficiently, aiming to reduce costs and delays tied to traditional remittance channels. The company has not finalized any plans but is actively evaluating blockchain-based options to streamline global payments. If implemented, this would mark a major step toward mainstream corporate adoption of stablecoins for real-world financial operations. Circle stock triples after IPO, signaling surging interest in crypto markets (3 minute read) Circle shares surged 205% above its IPO price, hitting $94.50 on Friday and lifting its fully diluted market cap to roughly $25 billion. Ark Investment bought over 4.5 million shares across its funds, betting on growing institutional interest in stablecoins like USDC. With pending U.S. legislation expected to regulate the sector, Circle's blockbuster debut signals rising confidence in crypto-financial infrastructure. UK's Wise to move primary listing to US in latest blow for London (4 minute read) Wise is planning to shift its primary stock market listing from London to the U.S., as it looks to tap into deeper liquidity and a more tech-savvy investor base. The fintech firm cited improved analyst coverage and stronger market dynamics in the U.S. as key reasons for the move. The decision follows a broader trend of UK-based tech companies seeking better valuations and visibility across the Atlantic. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Fintech's next chapter (15 minute read) After a decade of breakneck growth, fintech is entering a more disciplined era centered on profitability, infrastructure, and regulation-driven innovation. With capital harder to come by and competition intensifying, many companies are moving away from direct-to-consumer models toward B2B services, embedded finance, and infrastructure plays. Despite a global field of 37,000 fintechs, fewer than 100 account for nearly 60% of the sector's $378 billion in 2024 revenue—more than half of which came from payments. Fintech still represents just 3% of the $12.7 trillion global financial services market, but its evolution signals a shift from hypergrowth to strategic endurance, where scale, specialization, and ecosystem integration increasingly set the winners apart. Fintech is still 3% Done (10 minute read) In 2024, Fintech companies grew revenue 3x faster than incumbents at 21% YoY and grew EBITDA by 25%, but still represent only 3% of the financial services market cap. We're not at the end of the fintech revolution - we're barely past the opening credits. The data from 2024 shows an industry hitting its stride just as the next wave of disruption begins. As Chime, Circle, and Klarna prepare for IPOs that could shift that market cap percentage slightly, a new generation of AI-native companies is coming up, changing the rules entirely. From isolation to integration: how financial firms are building the infrastructure for crypto's real-world usability (6 minute read) A new wave of crypto adoption is being driven not by ideology, but by utility — and traditional financial players are building the pipes to make it work. PayPal now embeds stablecoins directly into payments, Coinbase is helping financial institutions tap into blockchain rails through a hybrid model of on-chain backends and centralized interfaces, and Crypto.com is partnering with Green Dot to integrate crypto with mainstream banking tools. The shift isn't about displacing banks — it's about reimagining finance through integration, where partnerships and infrastructure take priority over decentralization. Pricing Agentic: Economic Models for a New Kind of Demand (10 minute read) Agentic demand fundamentally alters the economic calculus compared to conventional e-commerce marketing funnels and a radical remaking of Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC). Pricing models could include a publishing model, platform pricing of purchase orders, and demand-side auctions. There are many other options, but a discussion of the trade offs here are useful to get a view on stakeholder reactions, and potential for disruptive value (and traction). 💻 Launches & Products AI for Business & Finance Certificate Program: Early Enrollment Savings Ends June 16 (Sponsor) Unlock the real value of AI in business and finance in this online certificate program from Columbia Business School Executive Education + Wall Street Prep . You'll explore real use cases from top firms like BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, and more—plus, learn to evaluate AI tools with confidence, and use beginner-friendly Python to automate tasks and drive smarter decisions—no coding experience required. 👉 Save $300 with code TLDR + $200 off for early enrollment before June 16. Apollo teams up with JPMorgan, Goldman to trade private credit (5 minute read) Apollo is launching a new private credit trading platform called Atlas, partnering with JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs to build secondary markets for private loans. The move aims to bring more liquidity and transparency to the $1.7 trillion private credit market, which has traditionally been opaque and illiquid. If successful, Atlas could redefine how institutional investors access and manage private debt assets. Intuit launches agentic AI experiences for 100M+ users via GenOS (3 minute read) Intuit announced a major upgrade to GenOS, its proprietary AI operating system, to power “agentic” AI experiences across its product suite including TurboTax, QuickBooks, Credit Karma, and Mailchimp. These AI agents aim to handle tasks automatically on behalf of users—like filing taxes or optimizing cash flow—rather than just providing suggestions. The initiative supports Intuit's broader mission to build a network of interconnected AI-powered financial assistants for over 100 million consumers and small businesses. Palantir partners with former fintech darling Bolt for AI checkout (3 minute read) Palantir is teaming up with Bolt to launch a new AI-powered checkout system aimed at creating faster, more personalized online shopping experiences. Bolt will use its trove of shopper identity and transaction data to build customized checkout models with Palantir, embedding them directly into retailers' websites and apps. The move follows a wave of AI-driven commerce announcements, including Google's unveiling of an AI-centric shopping experience and Perplexity AI's plan to let users check out via PayPal or Venmo within its AI chat tool. 🎁 Miscellaneous Deutsche Bank explores stablecoins and tokenized deposits (3 minute read) Deutsche Bank is exploring stablecoin issuance and tokenized deposits as it deepens its digital asset strategy. The bank may issue its own token or join a consortium, while also developing blockchain-based deposit solutions for payments. It joins peers like Santander and JPMorgan in pursuing blockchain-enabled efficiencies, supported by evolving EU and US regulations. UK targets rogue finfluencers in joint action with global regulators (3 minute read) The UK's Financial Conduct Authority is cracking down on misleading online financial promotions, teaming up with regulators in the US and Australia to monitor cross-border "finfluencer" activity. Officials say social media personalities are increasingly promoting high-risk or fraudulent investments to global audiences. The initiative aims to enforce clearer guidelines and remove harmful content faster across platforms like TikTok and Instagram. Bankrupt fintech Synapse seeks liquidation after failed asset sale (2 minute read) Synapse Financial Technologies is moving to liquidate its assets after failing to find a buyer during its bankruptcy proceedings. The fintech shut down operations in May 2024, leaving millions in customer funds frozen across partner apps like Yotta, Juno, and Yieldstreet. With no funds for a second auction or data custody, the estate says reorganization is no longer feasible. ⚡️ Quick Links Monzo surpasses £1bn revenue milestone (2 minute read) UK fintech Monzo has recorded a sharp rise in profitability and posted revenue in excess of £1bn for the first time. Walmart's Flipkart wins RBI lending licence, paving way for direct consumer and seller loans (3 minute read) Walmart-backed Flipkart has secured a lending license, allowing it to offer loans directly to consumers and sellers. Trump Boosts Crypto in 401(k)s by Axing Biden-Era Guidance (5 minute read) The Trump campaign has pledged to reverse Biden-era retirement plan guidance that discouraged including crypto in 401(k)s. text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-12 Stripe acquires crypto wallet startup Privy 🤝, Chime set to launch IPO on Thursday 📈, Klarna launches debit card 💳 Ask Luca! “Help me reconcile invoices and payments” (Sponsor) Meet Luca from Leapfin, your AI copilot for revenue data exploration and reporting – built for Finance teams who want more control, faster answers, and fewer bottlenecks.
