Make reconciliation faster and easier with the help of AI. Trusted by Canva, Reddit, and Poshmark. See how Luca works! 📱 News & Trends Klarna takes on banks with its own debit card (3 minute read) Klarna has launched a debit card in the U.S., marking a major push beyond buy now, pay later into core banking services. The card allows users to spend directly from their Klarna balance and earn rewards, putting the fintech in more direct competition with traditional banks. Klarna says it's aiming to be a full-service financial provider, not just a payments company. Stripe acquires crypto wallet startup Privy to expand crypto offerings (3 minute read) Stripe has agreed to acquire Privy, a startup that enables businesses to embed crypto wallets into their user experience, removing the need for external wallets like MetaMask. The deal follows Stripe's $1.1B acquisition of Bridge and reflects its growing push into stablecoin infrastructure and crypto-native financial services. Privy, last valued at $230M in March 2025, will remain an independent product, supporting Stripe's aim to serve companies launching crypto products. Walmart is using its own fintech One to provide credit cards after dumping Capital One (4 minute read) Walmart is cutting ties with Capital One and rolling out its own credit cards through a new partnership with Synchrony, integrated into its expanding fintech app, One. This move marks a major step in Walmart's push to build a full-service financial ecosystem for its customers, starting with payment and credit offerings. The launch positions One as a central hub for money management and signals Walmart's deeper ambitions in consumer finance. Chime set to launch IPO on Thursday, aiming for $11 billion valuation (3 minute read) Chime is moving forward with its long-awaited IPO today, targeting a valuation of around $11 billion. The digital banking platform has hired Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley to underwrite the offering, which could make it one of the largest fintech IPOs of the year. Chime previously delayed its public debut in 2022 during a market downturn but now appears to be capitalizing on renewed investor interest in consumer-facing fintechs. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports The rise of systems of consolidation (5 minute read) Modern fintechs are evolving from point solutions into "systems of consolidation"—platforms that centralize fragmented financial workflows across users, tools, and data. These systems are designed not only to improve efficiency but to reshape how finance teams operate and make decisions. As consolidation deepens, these platforms gain power by becoming the default context in which financial operations happen. Fintech's next chapter: scaled winners, AI, and stablecoins (5 minute read) Fintech revenues grew 21% in 2024. Nearly 70% of public fintechs are now profitable. The sector is seeing major tailwinds from agentic AI and stablecoin adoption in high-inflation markets like Turkey. Scaled players must double down on profitability and AI, while emerging disruptors have whitespace to tackle B2B, infra, and lending. Paying Agents: The first bots you should let in (10 minute read) Historically, GUIs were for humans and APIs for machines - but AI agents are now using both, forcing a rethink of interaction models and access policies. With platforms like ChatGPT handling end-to-end user journeys, websites must open up to agents or risk becoming irrelevant. The digital wallet revolution: How it's reshaping consumer behaviour and payment infrastructure (8 minute read) Fintech is no longer just a novelty - the digital wallet is a cornerstone of the new digital economy, driving shifts in consumer behavior, altering merchant ecosystems, and compelling banks and PSPs to rethink their roles. This is not an incremental change. It's a systemic realignment of how value is exchanged and how financial identity is managed. 💻 Launches & Products Get AI Certified from Columbia Business School Executive Education (Sponsor) Build AI skills that accelerate your career. In just 8 weeks, you'll demystify the buzzwords, move beyond basic prompts, and learn how to apply AI to real business challenges —with step-by-step guidance from Columbia Business School faculty and leaders from BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, and more. No tech background needed. Get the program brochure to learn more about the curriculum. P.S. Save $300 if you enroll this week using code TLDR. PayPal to bring stablecoin to Stellar Network (2 minute read) Already available on Ethereum and Solana, PYUSD will come to Stellar pending regulatory approval by the New York State Department of Financial Services. The move would give the stablecoin access to Stellar's vast network of on- and off-ramps, providing additional access through digital wallets, and connect to local payment systems and cash networks. Société Générale launches dollar-pegged stablecoin (2 minute read) Société Générale has introduced a dollar-backed stablecoin called EURCV on Ethereum, becoming one of the first major banks to offer such a product. The bank says the move aims to support digital asset markets and improve settlement efficiency. This builds on its 2023 launch of a euro-denominated stablecoin and reflects a growing institutional interest in blockchain-based finance. Why Bolt is embracing Palantir for its new checkout tech (3 minute read) Bolt has launched Checkout 2.0, an AI-powered online checkout experience built in partnership with Palantir, aiming to personalize UX based on shopper behavior, browser use, transaction history, and financing preferences. Bolt CEO Ryan Breslow emphasized that all data used in the system is “user‑consented” first-party information, addressing privacy concerns tied to Palantir's government-linked reputation. The tool represents Bolt's push to drive intelligent, data-driven checkout flows while offering transparency and opt-in controls for consumers. 🎁 Miscellaneous Why a Wall Street analyst-turned-AI CEO thinks stock pickers won't go extinct (4 minute read) Former Morgan Stanley analyst and current Toggle AI CEO Jan Szilagyi argues that human stock pickers still have an edge, even in an AI-driven era. While AI can analyze vast datasets and surface insights instantly, he believes intuition, narrative understanding, and risk management remain fundamentally human. Szilagyi says the future is not man versus machine—but man with machine. Barclays rolls out Microsoft 365 Copilot to 100,000 colleagues (3 minute read) Barclays has become one of the first major banks to deploy Microsoft 365 Copilot at scale, providing 100,000 employees with access to AI productivity tools. The rollout follows an internal pilot that showed measurable time savings and improved efficiency in tasks like summarizing meetings and drafting documents. Barclays sees generative AI as key to streamlining work while maintaining strong governance and data security. Neobanks Pressure-Test Gen Z Strategies With Traditional Banks to Win Crucial Market Share (8 minute read) Neobanks account for 40% of consumers worldwide and will represent 31% of the workforce by 2030. With current purchasing power standing at $450 billion and expected to reach $12 trillion within five years, Generation Z — the demographic born between 1997 and 2012 — is reshaping how banks and FinTechs attract and retain customers. ⚡️ Quick Links Worldpay and Visa partner to boost authorisations (2 minute read) Worldpay has expanded its partnership with Visa to enhance the payments leader's 3D Secure (3DS) solution, which can help merchants reduce fraud while reducing consumer friction and boosting authorizations. Plug and Play closes $50M fintech, AI fund targeting global innovation and corporate collaboration (4 minute read) Plug and Play is backing early-stage startups at the intersection of AI and fintech with a new $50 million fund. text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-16 Ramps raises $200M at $16B valuation 💸, Chime surged 37% in its public debut 🚀, Shopify enables USDC payments 🪙 Reusable identities myths and realities — broken down by product leaders from Ally, KeyBank, and Persona (Sponsor) Nobody likes proving they're real over and over again. In this on-demand webinar , fraud experts from Ally Financial, KeyBank, and Persona explain: → What reusable credentials and trust networks are → How they help platforms detect fraud faster → How product teams can reduce friction for trusted users during onboarding and re-verification The panel also dispels common myths about reusable credentials and their impact on fraud, and cover some practical considerations for implementation. ↗️ Watch the recording ↗️ 📱 News & Trends Chime rises 37% as the IPO market opens up (4 minute read) Chime surged 37% on its public debut, signaling renewed investor appetite for fintech IPOs after a long dry spell. The neobank priced its shares at $38, above the expected range, pushing its valuation to over $20 billion. With Klarna and Stripe waiting in the wings, Chime's strong showing could help unlock a broader wave of fintech listings in 2025. Amex leans into B2B payments (3 minute read) American Express is accelerating its push into business-to-business payments by encouraging corporate clients to use their Amex cards more frequently for work-related transactions and expanding its expense management platform, Center, into the Blueprint offering. The firm aims to build a global multi-rail B2B network—integrating digital tools and virtual cards—to streamline payments, enhance cash flow transparency, and automate accounts payable/receivable processes. This strategy bolsters its corporate payments volume and positions Amex as a comprehensive fintech partner in uncertain economic times. Ramp hits $16 billion valuation after new funding led by Founders Fund (2 minute read) Fintech startup Ramp has raised $200 million in new funding at a $16 billion valuation, led by Founders Fund. The company was previously valued at $13 billion in a secondary sale earlier this year and $5.8 billion in 2023. It provides corporate cards and financial tools for businesses. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports ) 💻 Launches & Products Marqeta and Klarna extend partnership to roll out the Klarna Card in the US (3 minute read) Marqeta is powering Klarna's expansion in the U.S. by enabling the launch of its physical Klarna Card for in-store payments. The card allows users to “pay in four” anywhere Visa is accepted, blending buy-now-pay-later flexibility with traditional card utility. Klarna already has over a million cardholders in Europe. This U.S. rollout signals a major push into everyday, omnichannel spending. Plaid launches Protect: next-gen fraud model detects 40% more first-party fraud (1 minute read) Plaid Protect is a new fraud detection system that starts with just a device fingerprint and can pull from over 10,000 data attributes across its network. The model claims to catch 40% of first-party fraud missed by older systems and predict 50% of future account suspensions at onboarding. It has a natural language interface that lets analysts run complex queries like identifying users who linked multiple accounts with short phone tenure. Shopify enables USDC payments via Coinbase and Stripe (3 minute read) Shopify is partnering with Coinbase and Stripe to allow merchants to accept USDC stablecoin payments on the Base network using Shopify Payments, no additional integrations required, offering faster, borderless transactions with lower fees and a seamless checkout experience. Merchants will be able to receive payouts in local currency or hold USDC directly. Eligible merchants can earn up to 0.5% cashback. This initiative signals a major step toward mainstream stablecoin adoption. 🎁 Miscellaneous Adyen opts to build in-house rather than buy (3 minute read) Adyen is prioritizing organic growth by building its own payment systems and POS hardware instead of acquiring competitors as it scales in the U.S. Its direct integration with merchants allows it to leverage proprietary data for higher authorization rates and faster issue resolution. This strategy distinguishes Adyen from peers relying on acquisitions and underscores its focus on tight client relationships and custom solutions. How Airwallex founder rejected Stripe buyout before building $775 million fortune (6 minute read) Airwallex co-founder Jack Zhang turned down a $1.2 billion acquisition offer from Stripe seven years ago. The company has since grown into a $6.2 billion global banking and payments platform. New York legislature votes to protect cash payments (2 minute read) New York's legislature has passed a bill that would prohibit most food and retail establishments from refusing to accept cash, imposing fines of up to $1,000 for first violations and $1,500 for repeat offenses. The legislation, now heading to Governor Hochul, aims to protect low-income, elderly, undocumented, and unbanked consumers who rely on cash for daily purchases. If signed into law, New York would join several cities and states that have enacted similar protections to ensure cash remains a valid payment option. ⚡️ Quick Links CFPB's enforcement veteran steps down amid agency shifts (5 minute read) CFPB veteran enforcement official Cara Petersen is leaving the agency after nearly two decades - the bureau is reportedly scaling back investigations and dismissing ongoing cases. Citing research showing that half of Americans believe switching phone plans is too difficult, Klarna says its users can transfer their existing number, or get a new one, and activate their phone plan in a few taps within the Klarna app, without any phone calls, paperwork, or store visits. Apple just rewired digital KYC (10 minute read) Apple is implementing a new Digital ID feature in iOS 26 that builds on integration for Driver's Licenses in the Wallet app. Starting this fall, Apple Wallet will allow iPhone users to add a U.S. passport. Apple is also adding a “Verify with Wallet on the Web” option in iOS 26 that can use a state-issued license or Digital ID to verify age and identity in a private and secure way. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Stablecoins – A New Model of Trust Enabled by Technology? (10 minute read) The defining innovation of stablecoins is not the technology itself, but the trust architecture they enable. While today's business architecture will NOT be turned upside down, the stablecoin frame does enable new models for managing legal contracts, systems interaction, and operational governance, all within a new regulatory superstructure. Small Businesses Don't Need More Cash. They Need Better Cash Flow Management (10 minute read) It's universally acknowledged that running a small business is difficult. What's less well-understood is why. Listening to the often-grim discourse around entrepreneurship and seeing stats like “one quarter of small businesses fail within the first year,” you'd be tempted to think it's simply a liquidity problem: small business owners don't have enough money to stay afloat. Yet, according to a new report, the key realization is that most SMBs don't have a cash problem. They have a cash flow management problem. Why closed-loop payments are ideal for boosting loyalty (5 minute read) Loyalty fails when it's disconnected from how people pay. Traditional loyalty programs are often built as afterthoughts. A separate sign-up. A separate app. A separate step. However, it doesn't have to be like that, because it's a disconnected experience for your customers, and that often leads to hesitation and confusion. What if loyalty can be tied to the payment? Closed-loop payments make that possible. They don't just process a sale. They embed loyalty directly into the act of paying. That means no extra cards and no extra friction for your customers. Just the value that is delivered at the moment it matters most. 💻 Launches & Products Robinhood adds advanced trading tools to retain active users (2 minute read) Robinhood Markets has upgraded its mobile app with new features, such as options trade preview simulations and enhanced real-time charting. With roughly 63% of Q1 revenue coming from customer trades, these enhancements are critical as CEO Vlad Tenev pursues a vision to transform Robinhood into a full-service global financial platform. The move aims to deepen engagement with millennials and Gen Z traders while laying the groundwork for upcoming offerings like bank accounts, credit cards, and wealth-management services. Alipay embeds payments into AR glasses (3 minute read) Alipay has integrated QR code payments into augmented reality glasses from Chinese outfit Rockid to enable users to pay for products in-store by looking at the label. Revolut will soon launch its AI financial assistant, UK CEO says (3 minute read) Revolut is gearing up to debut an AI-powered financial assistant for its users in the UK, aiming to help customers manage money more proactively. The tool will analyze spending habits, send smart notifications, and recommend tailored financial actions. This move signals a broader push by the neobank to deepen user engagement and sharpen its edge against traditional banks and fintech rivals. 🎁 Miscellaneous Aspora raises $50M to improve remittances for the Indian diaspora (3 minute read) Aspora has secured $50 million in Series B funding to build faster and more affordable remittance products targeting the Indian diaspora. It aims to compete with players like Wise and Remitly by offering seamless cross-border payments integrated with local wallets and bank accounts. Plaid at Money20/20 Europe 2025: Driving open banking adoption across Europe (5 minute video) Plaid President Jen Taylor unpacked the evolving role of open banking in the U.S. at Money20/20 Europe, spotlighting its growing use in lending and credit decisions. She noted that financial institutions are increasingly turning to cash flow data over traditional scores, while lending itself is becoming more decentralized—now embedded directly at checkout. Despite regulatory uncertainty at the CFPB, Taylor affirmed Plaid's long-term commitment to partnering with policymakers and advancing open banking innovation. Planning for 100-year lives: how fintech can reshape wealth for the longevity economy (5 minute read) As global life expectancy rises, financial systems built around a fixed retirement age are increasingly mismatched with modern realities. Today, one in six people worldwide is over 60—up from one in 11 just two decades ago—yet most wealth management tools still cater to midlife priorities. Fintech is stepping in with robo-advisors, behavioral nudges like auto-enrollment and gamified savings goals, and retirement income tools such as annuity ladders and dynamic withdrawal strategies, offering more adaptive, data-driven ways to manage money across longer lifespans. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech's biggest hurdle isn't product—it's marketing (2 minute read) Fintech's core challenge isn't product-market fit, but product-marketing fit. Juniper Square raises $130M Series D led by Ribbit Capital to power JunieAI for private markets (3 minute read) Juniper Square has secured $130 million in a Series D round led by Ribbit Capital at a $1.1 billion valuation to build out JunieAI, its enterprise-grade AI platform tailored for private market general partners. Nubank introduces recurring bill payments on Pix (2 minute read) Nubank has announced the launch of Automated Pix, a new option to pay recurring bills with convenience and security - it will be available to customers starting June 16. text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-23 Revolut exploring launch of stablecoin 🪙, JPM launches JPMD token 🏦, X exploring Payments Cards 💳 How real-time data is reshaping fintech—one login at a time (Sponsor) Financial services apps collect tons of user data—but the real breakthrough comes from using it instantly. ⚡ Picture this: a customer who normally logs in from NYC on an iPhone suddenly appears in Brazil on a Windows laptop. That's the kind of red flag 🚩 banks want to catch the moment it happens. With real-time data, financial institutions personalize experiences, spot fraud early, and deliver lightning-fast app performance— all at once. 👀 Want the full story on how Redis powers this? Dive into the blog 📖, or learn more at redis.io . 📱 News & Trends Coinbase is quietly taking on Visa and Mastercard (4 minute read) Coinbase's new “Base Pay” initiative lets merchants accept USDC on the Base network and settle in fiat, bypassing the card networks' ~2% interchange fees. By pairing on-chain payments with Coinbase Commerce's fraud tools and Stripe-style APIs, the exchange is positioning itself as a lower-cost alternative to Visa and Mastercard for online transactions. If merchant adoption grows, Coinbase could capture both payment volume and stablecoin float, eroding the card duopoly's grip on e-commerce. Revolut reportedly exploring launch of its own stablecoin (2 minute read) Revolut is considering launching its own stablecoin as part of a broader push into digital assets. The UK-based neobank has reportedly discussed the idea with regulators and is exploring a product that would be pegged to fiat currencies like the U.S. dollar or British pound. With over 50 million users, a live crypto exchange, and banking licenses across 30+ countries, Revolut's built-in distribution gives it a significant edge in going to market. JPMorgan breaks new ground with JPMD deposit token launch (2 minute read) JPMorgan Chase is making a landmark move into public blockchain infrastructure with the launch of JPMD, a deposit token representing a significant evolution in how traditional banks approach digital assets. The world's largest bank plans to pilot this token on Coinbase's Base network, marking the first time a major commercial bank has deployed deposit-based products on a public blockchain. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Pay by bank has chargebacks now (10 minute read) Pay by Bank now has chargebacks. On the surface, this looks like Visa co-opting yet another fintech innovation. However, zoom out and something more important is happening: everyone's racing to become the trust anchor for AI-driven finance. Pay by bank was quietly building momentum, but missing a “crossing the chasm” moment, and a better user experience could be the thing that does it. A fintech travelogue (4 minute read) This essay follows Alex Johnson's week-long swing through industry conferences in Amsterdam, Berlin, and London, highlighting how regional context shapes fintech priorities. European founders stressed embedded finance and open-banking monetization, while U.S. visitors talked about AI agents and stablecoins. Both camps agreed regulatory clarity will determine who scales fastest. Johnson argues that successful players must blend local compliance savvy with global distribution ambition as fintech enters its next maturity phase. Americans will put money in Amazon And Walmart stablecoins (8 minute read) Stablecoins were once a crypto curiosity. With the passing of the GENIUS Act, they're now a legitimate threat or opportunity depending on your perspective. JPMorgan has already filed a trademark for a digital deposit token, possibly signaling its intent to create a bank-issued stablecoin product. For other banks, the Act raises urgent questions: 1) Should it support the regulation? 2) How will stablecoins impact deposits, operations, and financial risk? and 3) Should it issue its own stablecoin? 💻 Launches & Products X exploring Payments Cards with trading function coming ‘soon' (2 minute read) X chief executive Linda Yaccarino has said that users will “soon” be able to make investments or trades on the social media platform, as she outlined a push into financial services in owner Elon Musk's quest to build an “everything app”. She added that the company was also exploring the introduction of an X credit or debit card, which could come as soon as this year. JPMorgan and Amex unveil next-gen premium cards (3 minute read) JPMorgan Chase is refreshing its top-tier Sapphire line with a metal “Sapphire Reserve LED” card that lights up when tapped, while American Express is piloting a similarly illuminated Platinum prototype to keep its product feeling exclusive and tech-forward. Both issuers aim to wow affluent millennials who value design and experiential perks as much as points. The flashy form factors signal a broader push to differentiate high-end cards in an increasingly crowded premium market. 🎁 Miscellaneous From pivot to IPO: lessons from Marqeta's Jason Gardner (3 minute read) Marqeta founder Jason Gardner recalls how a radical pivot from a floundering consumer-card play to a modern card-issuing API—anchored by a single, game-changing customer—set the company on a path to its 2021 IPO. He warns that over-reliance on one client is a mistake he has made twice, urging founders to diversify early. Looking ahead, he flags the Middle East's rapidly evolving payments rails as fintech's next big frontier. Cantaloupe expands into stadium self-checkout with Cheq deal (3 minute read) Cantaloupe has partnered with Cheq to bring its payment hardware and software to stadium kiosks and concessions, extending beyond traditional vending machines. The integration enables cashless, self-service checkout at live events, streamlining purchases for fans. It's a strategic move to tap into the fast-growing unattended retail and embedded payments space. ⚡️ Quick Links Federal Reserve and OCC crack down on payments fraud, especially checks (3 minute read) The Federal Reserve and Office of the Comptroller of the Currency are urging banks to bolster defenses against a surge in check fraud and synthetic identity fraud. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-26
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-26 Wealthfront files for IPO 🚀, Xero to acquire Melio 🤝, Circle hits $74B enterprise value 💰 📱 News & Trends Polymarket reportedly raising $200M at $1B valuation (1 minute read) Crypto prediction market Polymarket is reportedly in talks to raise $200 million at a $1 billion valuation, signaling growing investor interest following its surge in trading volume around political and pop culture events. The raise would mark a major milestone for the once-niche platform navigating regulatory scrutiny. Wealthfront confidentially files to go public in the U.S. (2 minute read) Automated investment platform Wealthfront has confidentially submitted paperwork for a U.S. IPO, signaling a move to tap public markets after years of private growth. The filing comes roughly two years after UBS' planned acquisition of the company fell through, prompting Wealthfront to refocus on independent expansion. If approved, the offering could mark one of the largest fintech public listings of the year. Xero to acquire Melio for $2.5B to compete with QuickBooks in AP/AR (2 minute read) Xero is acquiring U.S.-based Melio for $2.5 billion to strengthen its accounts receivable/payable offering and better compete with QuickBooks, which launched its own native bill pay feature in 2023. Melio brings 80,000 U.S. customers, $30 billion in TPV, and $153 million in FY25 revenue—roughly $2,000 per customer, or 6x Xero's current average. The deal aims to drive U.S. growth and cross-sell, with a projected $70 million in incremental revenue by FY28, though international expansion remains uncertain. Navan files for IPO, signaling a potential wave of B2B listings (2 minute read) Travel and expense platform Navan has confidentially filed for an IPO, potentially becoming the first major B2B SaaS listing in years. With over $300 million in ARR and a focus on profitability, Navan's move could reopen public markets for enterprise software companies. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports a16z outlines playbook for building enterprise AI companies (3 minute read) Andreessen Horowitz shares a framework for enterprise AI builders, stressing the importance of embedding into core workflows, leveraging proprietary data, and driving clear business value. The post draws on SaaS history to suggest that successful AI companies will focus on real adoption and durable use cases over hype. Bill.com CEO shares lessons on building for the Fortune 5 million (46 minute podcast) BILL founder and CEO René Lacerte sat down with fintech investor Rex Salisbury to discuss how the company scaled from startup to public while serving small businesses at scale. The conversation covers BILL's approach to payments, M&A, AI innovation, and navigating competitive markets. Lacerte also shares advice for founders aiming to build enduring, category-defining companies. Shopify × Stripe × Coinbase: stablecoin rails reshape payment economics (2 minute read) USDC checkout matches card take rates but eliminates FX fees and settlement delays, offering meaningful savings for Shopify's 14% cross-border merchants. Coinbase charges ~1% (likely 0.5% for Shopify), while Stripe's cut remains steady—keeping net margins roughly unchanged for now. However, with stablecoin rails reducing costs and boosting efficiency, pressure is building on incumbents like Stripe to respond post-Bridge. 💻 Launches & Products 👉 Fintech engineers, this one's for you (Sponsor) fintech_devcon is a developer-first conference happening this August 4-6 in Denver, featuring builder-led technical sessions by engineers at companies like Amazon and Plaid. Love architecture, code, and real-world lessons? Check out the action-packed agenda. 🔥 🎟️ Use code TLDR25 to save $195 on your ticket. Visa and Spreedly Combat Failed Payments Via Just-in-Time Card Updates (2 minute read) Spreedly, a leading global open payments platform, today announced a strategic enhancement to its platform through the integration of Just-In-Time Card Updates for Visa Cards powered by Visa Account Updater (VAU). Outdated card details are a key driver of failed payments, particularly in recurring billing or subscription models. Spreedly's Just-In-Time Card Updates integration addresses this challenge by providing merchants with up-to-date card credentials precisely when they're needed. Frollo launches Open Banking portal for mortgage brokers (3 minute read) Open Banking platform Frollo has rolled out a new portal designed to help mortgage brokers access real-time financial data directly from clients. The tool enables brokers to retrieve customer income, expenses, liabilities, and asset information via the Consumer Data Right (CDR) without relying on outdated documents like PDFs or screenshots. Frollo aims to speed up loan assessments and improve compliance across the mortgage industry by reducing paperwork and increasing data accuracy. Mastercard to open Account-to-Account instant payments sandbox to UK banks (2 minute read) Starting later this year, Mastercard will provide access to its fifth-generation A2A instant payments technology via a sandbox. Offering an environment for UK banks and financial institutions to experiment, validate, and collaborate across new ways to pay and modernize the UK's A2A payment ecosystem using Mastercard's innovative payment technology. 🎁 Miscellaneous Circle hits $74B enterprise value, surpassing Robinhood and Block (1 minute read) Circle has reached a ~$74B enterprise value, surpassing the valuations of Robinhood ($68B), Nubank ($59B), and Block ($38B). It now trails Coinbase ($78B) by just $4B. The company trades at 32x revenue, 80x gross profit, 152x EBITDA, and 285x earnings. With USDC's market cap at $60B and growing 90% year-over-year, Q2 2025 financials may soon be estimated from onchain supply data. Walmart-backed PhonePe seeking to raise $1.5 billion in India IPO (2 minute read) PhonePe is reportedly targeting a $1.5 billion fundraise through an IPO in India, aiming for a valuation of up to $20 billion. The digital payments giant, majority-owned by Walmart, has already shifted its headquarters from Singapore to India to pave the way for a domestic listing. If successful, this would mark one of India's largest tech IPOs and further cement Walmart's growing stake in the South Asian fintech space. Revolut founder could receive $1B+ if company hits ambitious targets (2 minute read) Revolut founder Nik Storonsky may earn a windfall worth over $1 billion through a new pay package tied to aggressive company milestones, echoing Elon Musk's 2018 Tesla compensation structure. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-30
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-06-30 Pine Labs files $1B India IPO 🇮🇳SEA Limited builds fintech empire from games 🎮💰 Intuit turns QuickBooks into AI CFO 🤖📊 📱 News & Trends Paypal-Backed Pine Labs files India IPO riding on fintech boom (3 minute read) Indian fintech firm Pine Labs has officially filed for an IPO in Mumbai, aiming to raise as much as $1 billion as it capitalizes on the country's expanding digital payments market. Pine Labs provides point-of-sale and pay-later solutions to merchants across Asia, helping retailers offer flexible financing and streamline in-store transactions. The company, backed by investors like PayPal and Temasek, had previously shelved a U.S. IPO in 2022 but now sees strong domestic investor appetite and improving profitability as reasons to go local. Intuit's AI-Powered QuickBooks Marks Strategic Pivot to Dominate SME Fintech via Multi-Agent Automation (4 minute read) Intuit is evolving QuickBooks into an AI-native platform, integrating multi-agent systems to help small businesses automate complex financial workflows. This shift goes beyond basic bookkeeping, enabling QuickBooks to act more like an autonomous financial assistant that coordinates tasks like payroll, tax filing, and invoicing. By embedding automation across its ecosystem, Intuit aims to secure its leadership in SME fintech and counter emerging competitors leveraging LLMs. Sea Limited's fintech playbook: from gaming to digital finance (3 minute read) SEA Limited started as a gaming company but used its Shopee e-commerce success to fuel growth in digital financial services across Southeast Asia. Its fintech arm, SeaMoney, now reaches over 60 million users through payments, credit, and wallet products, positioning SEA as a rare example of a tech firm that built fintech as a second act, not the first. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports An exhaustive review of the history and nascent culture of the CFPB (10 minute read) This article reviews the history and evolving culture of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), examining why it has become a central target for political attacks and efforts to reduce its power, especially under recent Republican administrations. While some of the CFPB's vulnerability is due to broad anti-bureaucratic agendas, unique aspects of its founding, mission, and leadership style also made it especially susceptible to being singled out for aggressive rollback and restructuring. Stablecoins are a platform - new financial products (10 minute read) Banking as a service turned bank charters into APIs - what once required years and millions became weeks and tens of thousands. Stablecoins let anyone build financial products with new licensed entities (stablecoin issuers) and with instant settlement. When you remove fundamental constraints, once-impossible business models become inevitable. This rewrites the economics of building financial products Why Kalshi is worth $2B—despite Polymarket's dominance (2 minute read) Polymarket may lead in volume and mindshare, but it's geo-blocked in the U.S., so it is banned from the very market driving its buzz. Kalshi, by contrast, is CFTC-regulated and legally accessible to U.S. users, giving it a powerful edge over institutional investors like Paradigm. Polymarket represents financial nihilism, while Kalshi is being positioned as infrastructure for “conditional finance”—a platform for programmable, outcome-based contracts far beyond betting. 💻 Launches & Products Intuit launches AI agents and fintech platform (2 minute read) These agents will automate workflows, and when combined with human experts, will deliver real-time insights and improve cash flow for businesses. The new AI agents, embedded in the Intuit platform, create a more powerful QuickBooks that provides businesses with a virtual team working on their behalf while the customer is always in control. Bolt launches support for stablecoin payments (2 minute read) Bolt, the checkout, identity, and payments platform, today announced Bolt Connect, a new product designed to help marketplaces onboard merchants faster, streamline operations, and scale with fewer resources. Digits Adds AI Agents to Accounting Platform (3 minute read) Startup accounting platform Digits has introduced autonomous AI agents to handle forecasting, expense categorization, and anomaly detection in real time. In early testing, the AI agent delivered 97.8% accuracy—outperforming human accountants at 79.1%—while being 8,500 times faster and operating at 24 times lower cost. The update is part of Digits' broader vision to transform accounting into a dynamic, always-on experience integrated directly into tools like Slack. 🎁 Miscellaneous How venture capital compensation breaks down by fund size (1 minute read) Deedy Das shared 2024 VC compensation data across 500+ responses, detailing base, bonus, and carry by fund size. Median total cash comp rises meaningfully with fund size, but carry—the long-term profit share—typically vests over 8 years and varies widely by title and internal structure. Many firms label employees as “partners” externally, but actual economics depend heavily on internal leveling. Senate shrinks proposed CFPB cuts in ‘megabill' after Trump calls for ‘one big beautiful bill' (4 minute read) Senate Republicans have scaled back their plans to slash the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's funding, reducing proposed cuts from 75% to 6%. The move follows pressure from Donald Trump and key GOP figures to pass a single, unified legislative package rather than piecemeal reforms. While the revised bill still faces Democratic resistance, the shift signals a tactical retreat aimed at preserving broader political consensus ahead of the 2024 elections. Coatue outlines AI-driven “Great Separation” in new report (2 minute read) Coatue's latest report argues that AI is creating a widening gap between fast-growing, AI-native companies and slower-moving incumbents, calling 2025 the “Year of Offense” for high-performers. Vertical AI, massive private capital raises, and accelerating IPOs are reshaping the enterprise landscape, with AI projected to drive over 75% of future market cap. ⚡️ Quick Links Startup vs. incumbent comes down to distribution vs. innovation (1 minute read) Startups move fast and innovate early, but incumbents have scale, customers, and inertia. Hebbia doubles down on vertical AI with finance and legal focus (3 minute read) Hebbia focuses on high-value verticals like finance and law with a premium, agent-based AI platform that goes beyond search to execute full workflows, distinguishing itself from lower-cost horizontal tools like Glean. Regulator orders Fannie, Freddie to consider crypto holdings in loan assessments (3 minute read) The U.S. Federal Housing Finance Agency has directed Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to begin factoring in applicants' digital asset holdings when assessing creditworthiness. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-03
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-03 Thiel, Luckey launch bank 🏦🔥, Klarna rebrands ahead of IPO 💳🚀, Robinhood to list OpenAI, SpaceX tokens in EU 💹🛰️ 📱 News & Trends Peter Thiel and Palmer Luckey back new SVB rival bank (2 minute read) A group of tech billionaires led by Palmer Luckey and Peter Thiel is reportedly launching a new U.S. bank aimed at serving startups and venture firms, positioning it as a rival to the now-defunct Silicon Valley Bank. The move reflects ongoing gaps in founder-friendly banking infrastructure following SVB's collapse. Klarna steps up push to become digital bank ahead of US IPO (6 minute read) Klarna is accelerating its shift from buy-now-pay-later giant to full-fledged digital bank, rolling out new products like a US debit card with Visa and mobile plans on AT&T as it prepares for another shot at a US IPO. The Swedish fintech is rebranding itself as a neobank and expanding beyond short-term credit to build resilience amid rising customer losses and tightening regulation. With Chime's IPO success and renewed investor interest in digital banks, Klarna's diversification play could help it regain momentum. Circle applies for U.S. trust bank license after bumper IPO (3 minute read) Circle is seeking a U.S. trust bank charter just days after its IPO, signaling a strategic push toward deeper regulatory alignment. The move would allow the USDC stablecoin issuer to operate under federal oversight and potentially access the Federal Reserve system directly. If approved, Circle could become one of the first crypto-native firms to secure a national trust bank license, setting a regulatory precedent for the sector. Robinhood to offer trading in OpenAI and SpaceX pre-IPO tokens in Europe (2 minute read) Robinhood plans to let users trade tokenized shares of private companies like OpenAI and SpaceX in Europe through a partnership with startup Xpansiv. The move aims to democratize access to pre-IPO equity, though questions remain about liquidity, pricing transparency, and regulatory oversight. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Fintech adoption and consumer risk: a configurational analysis of value and risk factors (12 minute read) An in-depth academic analysis has mapped out the full spectrum of consumer risks associated with mobile fintech, revealing that while utility remains the strongest driver of adoption, issues like performance risk and trust still present major hurdles. The researchers used two complementary methods—one focused on finding patterns in survey data, the other on identifying combinations of factors that influence behavior—to examine ten value and risk drivers. They found that social value has a strong influence, while fun and financial worries matter far less than expected, suggesting that fintech adoption hinges more on practicality and peer influence than on fear or entertainment. Reactors, Planners, Gen Z: Spending Habits Driving Fintech Innovation (4 minute read) Fintech startups are designing products around three emerging consumer personas—Reactors, Planners, and Experiencers—each defined by distinct spending behaviors and emotional relationships with money. While Planners seek control and budgeting tools, Reactors respond emotionally to financial stress, and Experiencers prioritize flexibility and freedom. This segmentation is pushing fintechs to move beyond generic money management and toward hyper-personalized, psychology-driven experiences. Ramp shares framework for making big bets in business (3 minute read) Ramp outlines how it evaluates and executes high-risk, high-reward strategic initiatives—from launching new products to entering new markets. The company emphasizes structured conviction, clear ownership, and staged validation to de-risk bold decisions while still moving fast. 💻 Launches & Products Balance partners with Alibaba to offer B2B BNPL (1 minute read) B2B payments platform Balance is teaming up with Alibaba to provide buy now, pay later options for U.S. business buyers on the e-commerce giant's platform. The partnership aims to simplify cross-border trade by offering instant credit decisions and flexible payment terms to SMBs. Affirm expands into gaming and digital goods (1 minute read) Affirm is moving into the video game and digital content space, aiming to extend its pay-over-time offerings to a younger, online-native audience. The expansion includes partnerships with platforms that sell games, in-game currencies, and digital media. Revolut partners with NBA to grow U.S. presence (1 minute read) Revolut has signed a multi-year partnership with the NBA, becoming the league's official financial app partner in the US. The deal is part of Revolut's broader push to expand its American footprint and connect with younger, digitally native users. 🎁 Miscellaneous Brex execs on saying no, staying sharp, and fintech's next chapter (1 hour video) Former Brex COO Michael Tannenbaum and current CBO Arthur Levy reflect on the challenges of sustained success, the discipline of saying no, and how great operators go beyond finance. While bullish on blockchain's role in financial services, they view enterprise AI as still experimental—useful, but not yet foundational. Clio to acquire vLex in $1B legaltech mega-deal (2 minute read) Clio is acquiring legal research giant vLex for $1 billion, combining practice management and legal data into one of the largest AI-powered legaltech platforms. The deal expands Clio's reach to over 1 million legal professionals globally, positioning it to compete more aggressively in the enterprise legal software market. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech Bolt progresses its turnaround by landing Klarna as a partner (4 minute read) Checkout startup Bolt has secured Klarna as a major new partner, signaling continued momentum in its efforts to rebound from a rocky past. Mastercard Steps Up Fraud Prevention Efforts With Launch of Account Intelligence Reissuance Service in EEMEA (3 minute read) Mastercard is introducing a new Account Intelligence Reissuance service across Eastern Europe, the Middle East, and Africa to help banks proactively replace compromised cards before fraud occurs. New York finalizes new BNPL regulations (1 minute read) New York has approved new rules for buy now, pay later providers, requiring clear disclosures, licensing, and stronger consumer protections. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-07
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-07 Klar raises $190M to expand in Mexico 🇲🇽, Ripple applies for US banking license 🏦, Wise seeks Fed access with bank charter move 🚀 📱 News & Trends Ripple seeks a U.S. banking license, adding to list of crypto companies (4 minute read) Ripple has filed for a national banking license as it aims to bring its stablecoin RLUSD under federal regulation and expand into broader financial services. The application would shift oversight from New York's DFS to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, giving Ripple dual regulatory coverage and potentially setting new transparency standards in the stablecoin market. In parallel, Ripple's subsidiary has applied for a Federal Reserve master account—an early step in crypto's deeper integration with traditional financial infrastructure ahead of likely federal legislation. Payments firm Wise seeks U.S. National Trust bank charter to access Fed payments (5 minute read) Wise Plc has filed an application to be directly regulated by the US Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to create a national trust bank in the US. The national trust bank charter would allow Wise to connect directly to the payment rails of the Federal Reserve to clear and settle US dollar-based payments. Klar raises $190M Series C to expand in Mexico (3 minute read) Mexican fintech Klar raised $190 million in a Series C round led by General Atlantic that valued the company at over $800 million. With $170 million in equity and $20 million in venture debt, Klar plans to accelerate product development, pursue a banking license, and expand into B2B payments and financing after acquiring assets from Tribal. The company, nearing $300 million in annual revenue, is targeting a $500 million run rate by Q3 2025 as it eyes a potential IPO. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports How Coupang missed its fintech moment (3 minute read) Despite its dominance in Korean e-commerce, Coupang failed to capitalize on fintech opportunities like payments, lending, and wallets that peers in Southeast Asia have embraced. Regulatory hurdles, leadership focus, and an aversion to non-core bets left room for competitors to build financial ecosystems around commerce. Robinhood's CEO on the Plan to Tokenize Everything (56 minute podcast) Robinhood, the company known for first introducing commission-free trading, has now become a behemoth with all kinds of different business lines, including credit cards, savings vehicles, crypto, and wealth management. This week, it announced further expansion with news that it's launching its own chain as well as tokenized stock trading (that for now is only available in the EU). In this podcast, Robinhood's founder and CEO, Vlad Tenev, talks about its new endeavors, as well as the legacy of the 2021 meme stock mania, the evolution of the YOLO traders, the changing regulatory environment, and when we can expect to have 24/7 on-chain stock trading in the US. Why Robinhood and OpenAI Clashed over Onchain Stock Tokens (8 minute read) Robinhood's new Arbitrum-based chain and its synthetic OpenAI equity token exemplify the neobroker race to dominate onchain capital markets, paralleling the rise of DeFi-native perpetuals and memecoins. While many current offerings are financial wrappers tied to offchain assets, players like Superstate and Securitize aim for native tokenization, where ownership, custody, and governance are all encoded onchain. The market's future hinges not on regulatory delay or wrapper complexity, but on whether onchain systems can replace legacy financial architecture entirely. 💻 Launches & Products FICO's new formula will include buy now, pay later loans (4 minute read) FICO is revamping how it calculates credit scores by factoring in buy now, pay later (BNPL) loans for the first time. This shift means installment plans from providers like Affirm and Klarna could now impact your creditworthiness, whether you pay on time or not. The move reflects BNPL's rise from niche tool to mainstream financial product, with millions of Americans relying on it for everyday purchases. Slice opens India's first UPI ATM Branch (3 minute read) Fintech company Slice has rolled out Slice UPI credit card and UPI-powered physical bank branch and ATM, which accepts cash in Koramangala, Bengaluru. The Slice UPI credit card charges no joining or annual fee. It delivers an effortless credit experience that feels as natural as any UPI transaction. Instacart launches debit card for shoppers with cashback perks (2 minute read) Instacart has introduced a debit card designed for its contract shoppers that offers cashback rewards, faster payments, and reduced transaction fees. The move aims to strengthen loyalty among gig workers and provide financial tools tailored to their needs. 🎁 Miscellaneous Stripe's first employee, the founder of fintech Increase, sort of bought a bank (2 minute read) Fintech startup Increase, led by Stripe's first hire, Valar Afshar, has acquired the balance sheet and tech stack of Lead Bank to create a new infrastructure bank. Rather than becoming a traditional bank, Increase will use Lead's capabilities to deepen its position as a B2B banking platform, offering direct access to Fed payment rails without intermediaries. The move signals a broader shift among fintechs toward owning core infrastructure rather than renting it. Airwallex builds stablecoin team to power global payments (2 minute read) Airwallex is hiring engineers to build stablecoin infrastructure to enable customers to buy, hold, send, and settle tokenized currencies worldwide. The move signals a growing trend among cross-border fintechs—like Wise, Nium, and Thunes— to leverage stablecoins for faster settlements, reduced FX risk, and new payout options as the fintech–crypto divide continues to blur. They're tokenizing the stocks (6 minute read) Robinhood is gearing up to offer tokenized stock trading in the EU, allowing users to trade blockchain-based versions of public and private equities like SpaceX and OpenAI. The move mirrors similar efforts by Kraken and Bybit, and promises features like 24/7 trading, global accessibility, and DeFi-enabled margin loans. While the promise is global liquidity and interoperability, practical hurdles, like price spreads, liquidity risk during market closures, and legal ambiguity around unapproved private stock derivatives. ⚡️ Quick Links Payments processors turn to AI to fight fraud (2 minute read) Major payments processors are increasingly deploying AI and machine learning to detect and prevent fraud in real time, as transaction volumes and attack sophistication grow. Student loan delinquencies and defaults are rising in the US (4 minute read) Delinquency rates on U.S. federal student loans have surged, with over 8 million borrowers missing payments since repayment requirements resumed. Coinbase Ventures portfolio valued at $361M after $145M write-offs (2 minute read) Coinbase Ventures has invested $522 million to date, including $320 million in 2021, but has taken $145 million in cumulative impairments and acquired two portfolio companies (Bison Trails and ORDAM). BitChat: Jack Dorsey's Offline Payments App (4 minute read) Block CEO Jack Dorsey has quietly launched BitChat, a peer-to-peer chat and payments app designed to work even when users are offline or off-grid. The app uses Bluetooth and local mesh networking to allow communication without the need for cellular or internet access. It's currently in beta and aligns with Dorsey's broader push toward decentralized, resilient financial infrastructure. 🎁 Miscellaneous Dext and Airwallex collaborate to launch Dext Payments (3 minute read) Accounting software provider Dext has teamed up with Airwallex to launch a new embedded payments product aimed at streamlining how small businesses settle invoices. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-14
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-14 Revolut eyes $1B raise 💸, JPMorgan data access fees 📊, Ant Group to integrate Circle’s USDC 🌐 Don't let your fraud team miss the signals that matter most (Sponsor) Fraud teams rely on risk signals, the data points that can help someone distinguish fraudsters from legitimate users. But connecting siloed data is difficult. So is figuring out which signals will help you fight various types of fraud. This new guide from Persona offers practical frameworks to rethink your fraud strategy from the signal up. Find out which signals can help you detect AI-powered fraud, such as deepfakes and synthetic faces. Learn how to use signals to improve automation during onboarding or account recovery. Get a handy reference for 50+ risk signals you should collect , from behavioral patterns to device fingerprinting. 📥 Get the guide 📱 News & Trends JPMorgan to charge fintechs for customer data access (3 minute read) JPMorgan plans to impose new fees—potentially totaling hundreds of millions—on fintechs accessing customer bank data, shaking up models for firms like Venmo, Robinhood, and Coinbase. Critics call it an attempt to stifle competition amid regulatory uncertainty, while JPMorgan argues third parties should pay for secure, authorized access to protect consumers. Revolut eyes $1B raise at $65B valuation in new funding round (2 minute read) Revolut is reportedly seeking $1B at a $65 billion valuation in its latest fundraising, just a year after securing a $45 billion deal. The UK neobank continues its global expansion and product diversification as it positions itself alongside fintech giants like Stripe and Ant Group. Ant Group to adopt Circle's USDC stablecoin for global blockchain platform (3 minute read) Jack Ma-backed Ant Group is partnering with Circle to integrate USDC into its blockchain platform, supporting treasury management and cross-border payments. As Ant seeks stablecoin licenses in Singapore, Hong Kong, and Luxembourg, the move aligns with its push to grow Ant International after regulatory crackdowns in China. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports How banks can reclaim low-value cross-border payments (3 minute read) McKinsey outlines how banks can regain share in low-value cross-border payments, a segment increasingly dominated by fintechs. Strategies include investing in real-time payment rails, improving user experiences, and leveraging data insights to offer more competitive pricing and transparent services. Community banks view embedded finance as key to longevity (4 minute read) Smaller banks are increasingly betting on embedded finance to stay relevant, with 88% of surveyed community financial institutions saying they view it as vital for long-term survival. There is growing interest in offering banking services within nonbank platforms, as many institutions are choosing to buy rather than build. As competition from fintechs and big banks intensifies, embedded finance is emerging as a strategic lever, not just an upgrade. Chase's “Punitive” Fee Proposal Could Cripple Open Banking (10 minute read) JPMorgan Chase has quietly introduced a controversial fee schedule for data aggregators, potentially charging as much as 1,000% more than the revenue generated per transaction for some fintechs. The proposed pricing, aimed especially at payments-related API calls, could undermine the economics of open banking use cases like instant ACH, cashflow underwriting, and overdraft avoidance. If adopted, these fees could force fintechs to raise prices, reduce services to lower-income users, or revert to insecure methods like screen scraping — all while giving incumbent banks a stronger grip on customer data. 💻 Launches & Products Ramp introduces AI agents suited for controllers (3 minute read) Designed to automate policy enforcement and prevent fraud, Ramp's new AI agents use context-aware reasoning to manage expense approvals and detect suspicious transactions. Unlike traditional automation, the agents operate independently, offer rationale for decisions, and adapt based on company policy and user feedback. Early customers reported 99% accuracy, with each action logged in an audit-ready trail and escalations triggered only when needed. Goldman Sachs pilots Cognition's AI coder Devin to create hybrid workforce (3 minute read) Goldman Sachs is deploying Devin, the autonomous AI software engineer from Cognition Labs, to augment its 12,000 human developers with plans to scale into hundreds or thousands of AI agents. Backed by Peter Thiel's Founders Fund, Cognition's $4B-valued Devin signals a shift to agentic AI capable of executing multi-step tasks like app development, with Goldman expecting a 3–4x productivity boost as humans and AI form a “hybrid workforce.” How the $1,000 ‘Trump Accounts' for Newborns Will Work (4 minute read) A new policy will give every U.S. baby born between 2025 and 2028 a $1,000 government-funded investment account designed to jumpstart long-term savings. The accounts function like a hybrid IRA, with additional contributions allowed up to $5,000 per year and funds restricted to low-cost stock index investments. Withdrawals are generally locked until age 18, after which the accounts transition into traditional IRAs with standard tax rules and early-use exceptions for things like education or a first home. 🎁 Miscellaneous Fed study flags BNPL risks to housing and personal finance (2 minute read) A new Federal Reserve study warns that buy now, pay later (BNPL) usage may strain consumers' ability to pay for housing, with some users prioritizing BNPL payments over rent or mortgages. The report highlights concerns about rising debt burdens and calls for closer monitoring of BNPL's impact on household financial stability. Bundling Debit with BNPL Is the Next Big Thing in Cards (4 minute read) Klarna is adopting Visa's Flexible Credential framework, enabling a single debit card to act like a hybrid of debit, credit, BNPL, pay-in-four, and even rewards-based payments. Visa CFO Christopher Suh described the setup as giving users the ability to “toggle between funding sources,” offering real-time control over how purchases are paid. ⚡️ Quick Links London Stock Exchange to launch private market this year (3 minute read) Pisces will go live by the end of 2025 to help startups and pre-IPO companies raise capital and manage equity more efficiently. Lloyds in talks to buy fintech firm Curve for £138m, Sky says (3 minute read) The 259-year-old Lloyds Banking Group is reportedly in advanced discussions to acquire British fintech Curve for around £138 million (US$186 million). title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-17
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-17 Bilt raises $250M 💸, Mastercard's higher tier rewards card 💳, Anthropic's Financial Analysis Solution 🧠 How Blackstone earned $60k in rewards with one simple change (Sponsor) Blackstone Griddles unlock new summer grilling possibilities. But how did they unlock $60K in rewards with just one change? Watch their video case study to see how Blackstone uses BILL Spend & Expense to massively streamline their spend management and earn big time rewards at the same time. Then claim your exclusive summer offer: take a demo of BILL and get a 28" Blackstone Omnivore Griddle .**Terms and conditions apply. See offer page for details. 📱 News & Trends Jamie Dimon says JPMorgan Chase will enter stablecoins as fintech threat grows (3 minute read) JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon said the bank will engage with stablecoins despite his skepticism, citing the need to stay competitive as fintech players encroach on payments and banking services. JPMorgan recently launched a limited “deposit coin” for clients and is exploring broader stablecoin initiatives. Citigroup and Bank of America are also weighing stablecoin strategies, potentially collaborating like they did with Zelle to counter fintech rivals such as PayPal and Cash App. Bilt raises $250M at $10.75B valuation, plans card relaunch (3 minute read) Bilt has closed a $250M round led by General Catalyst and GID, with a $100M strategic investment from UWM. The fintech now covers 1 in 4 U.S. apartment buildings and projects $1B+ in revenue by Q1 2026 and $100B in annual housing transactions by year-end. It is expanding into condos, HOAs, and student housing with partners like Douglas Elliman and Blackstone. A new “Bilt Card 2.0”, co-developed with Cardless, will be launched in February 2026 with three tiers ($0, $95, and $495 annual fees). Mastercard adds Top-Tier Card for banks wooing big spenders (3 minute read) Mastercard is introducing a higher-yet tier of rewards card, the World Legend category, to help banks challenge offerings from other companies. The company plans to debut the World Legend category with one of its largest US partners before the end of September, according to Bunita Sawhney, Mastercard's chief consumer product officer. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports 20VC: Robinhood's comeback: Vlad Tenev on scaling to $3B revenue and 4x'ing the stock (57 minute podcast) In a wide-ranging interview, Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev details the company's transformation. Over 18 months, Robinhood grew net revenue 58% to nearly $3B, flipped a $500M loss into $1.1B profit, and 4x'd its market cap to over $80B. Highlights include AI writing 50% of new code, a secret ChatGPT-style support tool, a tokenized private shares product, and a bold vision for “Capital as a Service” to reinvent startup fundraising. Tenev also reflects on Robinhood's 2020–2022 culture crisis and teases new ventures like cash delivery trucks. The rise of service-as-a-software: software that works for you (6 minute read) Service-as-a-software is redefining enterprise tech by turning AI into autonomous “workers” that deliver outcomes, not just tools. Unlike SaaS, these solutions directly perform tasks, making them especially transformative for labor-heavy industries like construction, logistics, healthcare, and financial services. With outcome-based pricing and access to labor budgets, this model enables startups to scale faster and build defensible, data-rich platforms that replace manual processes with always-on AI agents. Can you make agents pay? (6 minute read) Cloudflare is launching a new marketplace called Pay per Crawl, allowing website owners to charge AI crawlers micropayments every time their content is scraped. The system aims to balance the scales for publishers, who currently get minimal traffic in return for having their content vacuumed up by LLM models. While crypto isn't part of the beta, Cloudflare has hinted at stablecoins and agentic paywalls as being part of a broader vision — one with major parallels to open banking's looming data battles. 💻 Launches & Products Citi rolls out Cognition's agentic AI to 40,000 developers (3 minute read) Citi is deploying Cognition's AI agent Devin to automate repetitive developer tasks like software patches, upgrades, and code rewrites, aiming for 2x–20x productivity gains on routine work. While human developers remain in charge, the rollout marks a shift toward a “hybrid workforce” where AI handles boilerplate tasks at scale, freeing engineers for higher-value projects. Claude for financial services (5 minute read) Anthropic launched a Financial Analysis Solution that integrates market data and internal platforms like Snowflake and Databricks into one interface for finance professionals. It features Claude 4 models, pre-built connectors for providers like FactSet, PitchBook, and S&P Global, and partnerships with Deloitte, KPMG, and others for implementation. Early adopters like Bridgewater and NBIM report significant productivity gains. The solution is now available via AWS Marketplace, with Google Cloud support coming soon. 🎁 Miscellaneous BlockFi co-founder joins SoFi to lead digital assets push (2 minute read) BlockFi co-founder Flori Marquez Gilroy has joined SoFi as head of digital assets, signaling the fintech's renewed focus on crypto products. Gilroy will oversee strategy and product development as SoFi looks to expand its offerings amid growing regulatory clarity in the digital asset space. Fintech chains are the new App Store (2 minute read) Robinhood is joining Coinbase, Kraken, and OKX in launching its own blockchain, with Stripe potentially next. Unlike decentralization-focused projects, these fintechs are building platforms similar to Apple's App Store—bringing users on-chain and letting others build for them. With rollup-as-a-service providers like Gelato and Conduit, launching Layer 2s on Optimism or Arbitrum is now trivial. The goal: own the rails, unlock new services and revenue streams, and avoid renting blockspace from general-purpose chains. UniCredit picks Wise to handle FX payments in latest retail push (3 minute read) UniCredit will integrate Wise's payments platform to offer international currency transfers across Europe, aiming to reach all retail clients by 2027. The move follows a review of UniCredit's mobile payments that revealed “shortcomings,” with the bank targeting €400 million in additional fee revenue. This makes UniCredit the largest European bank to adopt Wise's Platform product, joining Morgan Stanley and Standard Chartered in leveraging Wise's global payments network. ⚡️ Quick Links Rex Salisbury's Cambrian Ventures raises new fund, bucking fintech slowdown (4 minute read) Cambrian Ventures, led by former Andreessen Horowitz fintech partner Rex Salisbury, has closed a new $20 million fund to back pre-seed fintech startups. AI now produces half the code of $88bn fintech Robinhood (2 minute read) Robinhood's CEO revealed on the 20VC podcast that AI now writes around half of all new code at the brokerage, signaling a major shift in how the $88 billion fintech approaches software development. PNC Considers Charging Fintechs for Access to Customer Data (4 minute read) PNC is the latest major bank to explore charging fintechs for access to customer data, following a path first taken by JPMorgan. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-21
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-21 OpenAI's payment checkout system 💲, Block to join S&P 500 📈, Agentic commerce reshapes fintech and commerce 💻 Invoiced: Get Paid 30-50% Faster with AR Automation (Sponsor) What do MassMutual, Covetrus, Valsoft, Trimble, and ezCater have in common?They all turned to Invoiced to automate accounts receivable, slash DSO, and improve cash flow—without adding extra headcount. Here's how Invoiced helps you get paid faster: → Automates invoicing & collections – payment reminders and follow-ups run on autopilot→ Accepts online payments & tracks status in real time→ Gives you clear cash flow insights to stay on top of performance→ Integrates seamlessly with your ERP or CRM—no heavy IT work required Start your free demo and see how fast you can get paid 📱 News & Trends OpenAI working on payment checkout system within ChatGPT (2 minute read) OpenAI is reportedly building a native checkout experience inside ChatGPT that would let users make purchases from third-party vendors directly within the chatbot. The move could position ChatGPT as a transactional platform, not just a conversational one, enabling a more seamless blend of shopping and AI assistance. This could create new revenue opportunities for developers and OpenAI alike. Jack Dorsey's Block is set to join S&P 500 index, replacing Hess (2 minute read) Digital payments firm Block will be added to the S&P 500 index, replacing energy company Hess as of July 24. The shakeup follows Chevron's acquisition of Hess, which is expected to close soon. Block's inclusion marks a symbolic win for fintech, signaling growing acceptance of newer tech-driven firms in traditional financial benchmarks. Stripe acquires real-time payments startup Orum (3 minute read) Stripe has acquired Orum, a New York-based fintech startup that specializes in fast, intelligent payment routing using machine learning APIs. Orum's platform helps move money across various payment rails—ACH, RTP, credit cards, and potentially blockchain—reducing settlement times to under 24 hours. The deal follows Stripe's broader M&A momentum, including its recent buys of Privy and Bridge, as it sharpens its edge in the real-time payments space. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports The rise of vertical fintech: Tailwinds, opportunities, and AI-driven acceleration (6 minute read) Vertical fintech is entering its breakout phase as industries demand tailored financial products and workflows. Companies like Cedar, WeTravel, and Cloover are expanding from niche offerings into full financial operating systems by embedding payments, credit, and software tightly within vertical-specific workflows. AI is driving faster product expansion and unlocking underserved markets such as construction, logistics, and healthcare, where complex high-value financial flows create strong moats for vertical players. Apps are dying. Agents are buying (20 minute read) Agentic commerce—where AI agents handle discovery, purchase, and payments—will reshape fintech and commerce. Apps dissolve into “liquid software,” checkouts become invisible, and merchants face discovery challenges as SEO shifts to AEO (agent experience optimization). This post explores Visa's intelligent commerce, MCP servers (like Ogment AI), security risks, and whether this is the Nokia WAP era or the iPhone moment for AI-driven commerce. The bull case: a platform shift 10x bigger than mobile. GENIUS Passed Into Law. Now What? (15 minute read) President Trump capped off “Crypto Week” on Friday by signing the GENIUS Act into law, describing it as an “exciting new frontier.” The measure creates a dual federal and state licensing and oversight framework for “payment stablecoins.” Trump's signature on GENIUS passes it into law, kicking off an implementation period that will take 18 months or until 120 days after the date on which primary federal stablecoin regulators issue any final implementation regulations, whichever comes first. It is expected that GENIUS will become effective around November 2026. 💻 Launches & Products Coinbase Wallet is now Base app, a crypto 'everything app' (3 minute read) Coinbase has transformed its Wallet into the new Base app, combining crypto trading, payments, messaging, and social features into a single platform. Announced at its “A New Day One” event in LA, the app also introduces support for mini apps running on Coinbase's Ethereum Layer 2 network. The move reflects Coinbase's broader ambition to create a crypto-native super app that acts as the central gateway to the onchain world. Hive Wealth brings digital family office to masses with AI (4 minute read) Hive Wealth is launching a digital platform aimed at democratizing the “family office” experience using AI-powered tools. The app helps underserved communities—especially women and people of color—plan generational wealth by tracking income, spending, net worth, and community benchmarks. Yvette Sadler Butler, former president of Silicon Valley Bank Private Banking & Wealth Management, is leading the company's push into tech-enabled financial empowerment. Adyen pushes into niche golf payments market (3 minute read) Adyen partnered with Worldwide Golf to deploy its payment terminals across 100 U.S. stores, aiming to unify in-store and digital transactions. The Dutch payments giant faces stiff competition from niche players like GolfTranz, foreUp, and Golf Pro Payments, which offer full-stack solutions tailored to golf businesses. Adyen says its platform flexibility and experience with retailers like Dick's Sporting Goods position it to win more in the golf space despite the vertical's complexity. 🎁 Miscellaneous JPMorgan found yet another revenue stream - your bank data (4 minute read) Banking giant JPMorgan Chase just shook the entire financial technology landscape by implementing fees for FinTech companies seeking access to customer bank account data. This marks the end of an era where such access has traditionally been provided at no cost, fundamentally altering the economic foundation upon which many FinTech services operate. Bank of America touts AI gains amid industrywide adoption push (4 minute read) Bank of America is reporting significant productivity gains from artificial intelligence, with 98% of its employees now using in-house AI tools. The bank has deployed more than 700 AI applications and is leaning on the technology to enhance internal operations, fraud detection, and client services. FDIC proposes inflationary adjustments (4 minute read) The FDIC is planning to raise several monetary thresholds tied to inflation in an effort to modernize outdated regulatory benchmarks. The move is designed to keep compliance requirements proportional to a bank's size and complexity, offering targeted relief to smaller institutions. The FDIC estimates this proposal could potentially lighten regulatory burdens for over 1,500 community banks—especially important given that, over the past 20 years, the total number of banks has dropped by 50%, with banks under $500 million in assets declining by 27.6% while larger banks have grown by over 75%. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech multiples soar as markets eye all-time highs (2 minute read) Robinhood ($HOOD) trades at 26.1x revenue and 72x earnings ($96B market cap), Coinbase ($COIN) at 13.1x revenue and 50.7x earnings ($106B), and Circle ($CRCL) at 24.6x revenue and 219x earnings ($56B). The death of the Amex lounge: Why the upper middle class isn't special anymore (5 minute read) With millions of new millionaires crowding housing, resorts, and Amex lounges, the upper middle class is facing an existential crisis as their once-exclusive lifestyle loses its scarcity and appeal. AI continues to push consumer fintech funding out of favor (4 minute read) Investor attention is drifting further from consumer fintech.
