title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-24
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-24 PayPal World 🌍, Agentic AI for Intuit Enterprise Suite 🧑‍💻, UK competition authority vs Apple ❌ 🔐 AI Risk Summit at the Ritz-Carlton, Half Moon Bay (Sponsor) AI Risk Summit 2025 brings together security executives, researchers, and policymakers to tackle the evolving risks of artificial intelligence. Join SecurityWeek August 19–20 at the stunning Ritz-Carlton in Half Moon Bay, CA for two days of expert-led sessions on generative AI risks, LLM security, adversarial threats, and regulatory readiness. Hear from leaders at American Express, Block, Discover Financial, Google, Oracle, Walmart, OpenAI, NVIDIA and more as they explore real-world strategies for safe and responsible AI adoption in the enterprise. This intimate in-person event is designed for decision-makers who want to stay ahead of the curve. The full agenda is now live . 👉 Register now 📱 News & Trends PayPal launches “PayPal World” to connect global wallets and payment systems (3 minute read) PayPal World is a platform that links major payment systems and wallets, starting with PayPal and Venmo interoperability. Launch partners include Mercado Pago, UPI (NPCI), Tenpay Global, and Venmo. Representing nearly 2B users, the platform allows consumers to pay internationally using their domestic wallets and enables businesses to accept new payment options without added integrations. PayPal World goes live this fall, with plans for expanded wallet partnerships and future support for AI shopping agents and stablecoins. Goldman Sachs and BNY Mellon launch tokenized money market funds for institutional investors (3 minute read) Goldman Sachs and BNY Mellon unveiled a platform enabling institutional clients to invest in tokenized money market funds, with ownership recorded on Goldman's blockchain. Backed by firms like BlackRock, Fidelity, and Federated Hermes, the initiative targets the $7.1T money market industry, promising faster settlement, 24/7 trading, and greater efficiency. Unlike stablecoins, tokenized funds offer yield and could serve as collateral across trades, reducing frictions in traditional markets. BNY will maintain dual records during the transition to support adoption. UK competition authority takes on Apple over digital wallets (3 minute read) The move comes as the CMA confirms that it is planning to designate both Apple and Google with ‘strategic market status' in their mobile platforms, including operating systems, app stores, and mobile browsers. For Apple, this includes addressing its longstanding restrictions that prevent rivals from competing with its wallet. The CMA says it will focus on interoperability to take on the restrictions on functionality to enable UK fintechs to bring innovative digital wallets to market. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Penrose AccountingBench reveals frontier AI models fail real-world “closing the books” tasks (8 minute read) Penrose's AccountingBench tests LLMs on accrual accounting with real SaaS financial data. While models like Grok 4 and Claude 4 initially match CPA baselines within 1 percent, they degrade over time, accumulating compounding errors and even hacking validation checks to progress. GPT and Gemini models fail to close a single month. The benchmark exposes how current AI struggles with multi-step, high-stakes tasks where errors cascade across periods. Why Software Platforms Still Struggle to Turn Payments Into Profits (8 minute read) Software companies should evaluate their payments maturity across three stages: enablement, customer value creation, and monetization. Moving from customer value creation to monetization requires specialized payments expertise, strategic integration, and data intelligence. Companies can generate revenue by participating in card economics, engaging directly in funds flow, and monetizing their platform's network and payment speed as value-added services. The fintech comeback: Sheel Mohnot on why everything is fintech and what's getting funded in 2025 (20 minute podcast) After a brutal two-year slump, early-stage fintech investing is rebounding—and Sheel Mohnot is at the center of it. As co-founder of Better Tomorrow Ventures, Mohnot shares how his fund is doubling down on infrastructure, embedded finance, and AI startups solving real workflow pain, backing over 50 companies that reflect the “everything is fintech” thesis. 💻 Launches & Products Zero-fuss invoice → cash with Invoiced AR automation (Sponsor) Managing accounts receivable shouldn't be a struggle. Invoiced simplifies the entire invoice-to-cash process — eliminating complexity, automating collections, and accelerating payments. Optimize your AR with automation, AI-powered reconciliation, and accurate forecasting. Start with a demo Intuit Launches New Agentic AI Experiences for Intuit Enterprise Suite (3 minute read) Intuit has announced significant updates to Intuit Enterprise Suite, an all-in-one solution designed to help mid-market businesses scale with the addition of new proactive AI agents, enhanced automation, business intelligence, and financial management capabilities that increase productivity and profitability. These enhancements deliver an integrated solution built for enterprise-level operations, all in one place on the Intuit platform. Verifone and PagBrasil Launch Exclusive International Pix Payment Solution for Brazilian Shoppers in the U.S. (3 minute read) PagBrasil and Verifone's International Pix enables Brazilian consumers to pay in Brazilian Reais (BRL) via Pix while shopping in the US. This innovative solution offers a frictionless, real-time payment experience, saving users conversion fees and eliminating reliance on international credit cards. 🎁 Miscellaneous Stablecoins emerge as alternative: Amex CEO (4 minute read) American Express CEO Steve Squeri said stablecoins are a “good proxy” for ACH and Swift payments, though unlikely to fully replace them. Following the Genius Act's passage, Amex is evaluating stablecoins' role, especially for cross-border payments, despite them being a minor revenue driver today. Amex recently partnered with Coinbase for a crypto rewards card, joining Visa and Mastercard in exploring digital assets. Q2 revenue rose 9% year-over-year to $17.86B, while net income fell 4% to $2.89B. Analysts remain bullish, citing Gen Z/millennial engagement. Block agrees to $12.5M settlement over Cash App spam texts in Washington (3 minute read) Block, parent of Cash App, agreed to a $12.5M settlement in a class action alleging its “Invite Friends” feature violated Washington state's anti-spam law by enabling unsolicited text messages. The case, filed in 2023, claimed Cash App users sent referral texts without recipients' consent. Block denies wrongdoing but settled to avoid further litigation. This follows earlier Cash App-related payouts, including $255M to regulators in January and a $40M NYDFS fine in April. The settlement applies only to Washington residents and awaits court approval. Sam Altman Says AI Has ‘Defeated' Bank Voice ID Protections (5 minute read) OpenAI's CEO says the banking sector is facing a “significant impending fraud crisis” because artificial intelligence has advanced to the point that it can impersonate someone's voice to get around security protocols and move funds. ⚡️ Quick Links Payroc to acquire LedgerPay, expanding cloud-based payment capabilities (3 minute read) Payroc WorldAccess announced plans to acquire LedgerPay, a cloud-based payments platform operated under the PayiQ brand. Quavo raises $300M from Spectrum Equity to scale AI-powered dispute management (2 minute read) Quavo secured a $300M growth investment from Spectrum Equity to expand its AI-driven fraud and dispute automation platform. Fiserv acquires part of TD's Canadian merchant business (2 minute read) Under a multi-year strategic management services programme, TD Merchant Solutions will use Fiserv technology, including the Clover point-of-sale system, for its business. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-28
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-07-28 Ramp may raise at $21B valuation 💸, Mastercard's AI card design platform 🪪, How Pix was built 🌍 📱 News & Trends Mastercard launches AI card design platform (2 minute read) Already live in North America, Europe, and Australia, the Mastercard AI Card Design Studio promises to bring speed, affordability, and advanced customisation to issuers. Users can upload logos and product details, use AI-assisted design features, customise as needed, and instantly download brand-compliant designs in minutes. Ramp in talks to raise at $21B valuation, up 30% from June (1 minute read) Ramp is reportedly in discussions to raise new funding at a $21 billion valuation—up 30% from its June 2025 financing round. The corporate card and spend management company continues to scale rapidly, capitalizing on AI-driven automation and expanding product offerings. OpenAI's Sam Altman didn't expect banks to adopt AI so soon (3 minute read) Sam Altman shared his surprise at the banking sector's rapid embrace of generative AI during an on-stage chat with Federal Reserve Vice Chair Michael Barr. Major players like JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley are already weaving AI into customer service, compliance, and internal operations. According to bank executives surveyed by KPMG in April, generative AI could handle up to 40% of daily tasks by year's end. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports How the Central Bank of Brazil built Pix, powering 6 billion monthly transactions (40 minute podcast) Pix was created by the Central Bank of Brazil with strong public-private collaboration, backed by regulatory authority and supported by a co-creation model with stakeholders. Key to its adoption were a low-cost centralized infrastructure, clear branding, mandatory participation by large banks, and a robust national communication strategy. Globally, Pix is seen as a leading example of fast payment system deployment, driven by the central bank acting as a neutral coordinator and scheme owner. TAMs lie during platform shifts (2 minute read) Traditional TAM logic breaks down when platforms shift. AI tools like Claude Code don't just boost developer productivity, they unlock entirely new users and behaviors. The best startups don't chase existing markets, they create and expand them in ways TAM never predicts. Congress Accidentally Created a Federal Payments Charter with Stablecoins (20 minute read) For decades, fintech companies begged Congress for a federal payments charter. State regulators killed it every time. Last month, Congress accidentally gave them something better. We just lived through the most consequential change to financial services regulation in two decades or more with the passing of the GENIUS Act. The result will be the biggest shift in US domestic payments in a generation. For the first time, we have a third way to regulate payments. The FBO account, the MTL, and now, stablecoins. This parallel financial system exists on top of, and around the existing one. Like the internet does over Telcos. 💻 Launches & Products Samsung Wallet adds in-store instalment payments (2 minute read) Samsung has teamed up with Splitit to let users of its mobile wallet use their existing credit to split in-store payments into instalments. Claude builds a full DCF in under 2 minutes (2 minute read) Claude's new AI agent can build a complete, source-linked DCF model live on stage—pulling from EDGAR, FactSet, PitchBook, and more. It doesn't stop there: it writes full investment memos in firm-specific templates. For bankers and analysts, it's both thrilling and threatening—what once took hours now happens in seconds. Ally makes AI platform available companywide (6 minute read) Ally Bank is rolling out an in-house AI platform designed to help developers embed real-time intelligence into every customer interaction. The system has been tested with 2,200 employees across marketing, audit, and technology teams over the past 18 months, combining structured and unstructured data to generate dynamic insights. CIO Sathish Muthukrishnan says this is part of a long-term vision to build proprietary AI capabilities instead of relying solely on third-party models. Coinbase brings leverage to the masses with perpetual futures (5 minute read) Coinbase is making it easier for retail investors to trade perpetual futures—highly leveraged crypto contracts that don't expire. The offering, available through its Coinbase Advanced platform, mirrors what's already common on offshore exchanges like Binance and Bybit. While the move could boost trading volumes and revenue, it also marks a shift toward riskier products at a time when regulators remain cautious about retail access to complex derivatives. 🎁 Miscellaneous Cleo has hit $250m ARR and is looking at an IPO! (1 minute read) UK-based AI money app Cleo is reportedly preparing for a public listing, eyeing the U.S. market as its key growth driver. The startup, which helps Gen Z users build credit and better financial habits through an AI chatbot, has surpassed 700K users and $250M ARR. Fintech coalition demands regulatory action: JPMorgan data fees exceed 75% of Plaid revenue (4 minute read) A group of fintechs is sounding the alarm on what they call "data monopolization," urging the CFPB to step in as JPMorgan's data access fees have surged to more than $60 million—over 75% of Plaid's U.S. revenue. The coalition argues these fees are anti-competitive and threaten innovation across consumer finance. The push comes ahead of the CFPB's upcoming final rule under Section 1033 of Dodd-Frank, which is expected to clarify data access rights. ⚡️ Quick Links Credit card APRs hit historic highs (1 minute read) Average U.S. credit card interest rates have surged from 14.56% to 21.16% in just 3.5 years—a record-breaking jump. While not finalized, this move would deepen ties between two of the most influential U.S. companies and give JPMorgan a powerful new distribution channel for financial products. CFPB to rewrite open banking rule amid industry backlash (2 minute read) The CFPB will “substantially” revise the 2024 open banking rule following a lawsuit from banking groups and growing fintech pressure. Congress Accidentally Created a Federal Payments Charter (12 minute read) The GENIUS Act quietly introduced a seismic shift in U.S. financial regulation by creating a federal framework for stablecoin issuers, offering a third path for nonbanks to move money alongside FBO accounts and state-by-state MTLs. This new category, the Permitted Payment Stablecoin Issuer (PPSI), enforces strict redemption, audit, and oversight standards while enabling programmable, blockchain-based payments that can bypass legacy infrastructure. With faster setup, lower costs, and 24/7 settlement potential, stablecoin-backed accounts are emerging as a viable alternative—laying the groundwork for a parallel financial system that could ultimately outcompete traditional rails on economics, efficiency, and scale. 💻 Launches & Products Bilt revamps credit card lineup, drops Wells Fargo for Cardless (3 minute read) Bilt is ending its partnership with Wells Fargo and launching three new credit cards with Cardless in early 2026, including two with annual fees of $95 and $495. The move comes after Wells reportedly lost up to $10 million per month on the no-fee Bilt card due to unprofitable user behavior. The revamped cards expand beyond rent to include mortgage, student housing, and HOA payments, as Bilt aims to drive broader usage and long-term profitability. FIS partners with Circle to enable USDC payments for banks (2 minute read) FIS has partnered with CIRCLE to allow U.S. financial institutions to offer domestic and cross-border payments using USDC, the largest regulated stablecoin. The integration begins with FIS' Money Movement Hub and follows recent U.S. stablecoin legislation, signaling growing convergence between blockchain infrastructure and traditional finance. The partnership aims to reduce payment complexity and costs while unlocking real-time, compliant stablecoin settlement at scale. Cash App launches group payment “pools” feature (2 minute read) Cash App has launched pools, a new peer-to-peer payment feature that lets users collect and manage group payments for events, gifts, and shared expenses. The tool supports Apple Pay and Google Pay, allows organizers to set goals, track progress, and withdraw directly to their Cash App balance. Initially available to a limited group, pools aim to simplify group money logistics for Cash App's 57 million+ monthly users, deepening social and financial engagement across the platform. 🎁 Miscellaneous Salient raises $60M to expand AI-powered loan servicing (2 minute read) Salient has raised $60M in Series B funding led by Omers Ventures to scale its AI-powered loan servicing platform. Salient plans to expand its team and build new AI tools to reduce servicing costs and improve borrower experience. Fiserv sued over Clover migration and misleading growth claims (2 minute read) Fiserv is facing a federal securities lawsuit alleging it misled investors by forcibly migrating 200,000 merchants from its legacy Payeezy system to Clover, while masking slowing organic growth. The suit claims Fiserv overstated Clover's performance and failed to disclose merchant churn to competitors like Square and Toast. Filed by the City of Hollywood Police Officers' Retirement System, the class action targets Fiserv's leadership and follows a Q2 earnings miss that sent the stock tumbling. How AI is finally tackling America's unclaimed duty crisis (6 minute read) AI is beginning to unlock the $70 billion in unclaimed U.S. customs duties left behind by small and mid-sized importers. These businesses often miss out on duty drawbacks due to complex paperwork and a lack of awareness, forfeiting billions each year. ⚡️ Quick Links JPMorgan and Coinbase strike deal to link bank accounts and crypto wallets (3 minute read) JPMorgan and Coinbase are partnering to let users link traditional bank accounts with their crypto wallets, aiming to streamline fiat-to-crypto transfers. Visa brings Google Pay integration into fleet cards (2 minute read) With the addition of Google Pay tokenization and push-to-wallet, Visa addresses the long-standing challenge of fleet data tags being tied solely to the plastic card chip. Wallet Wars 2025: Super Apps vs. Specialized Fintechs (5 minute read) In the rapidly shifting landscape of digital finance, 2025 is shaping up to be a defining year for digital wallets. text: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-07 Mastercard backs open banking 🤝, Ramp raised $500M 💸, Coinbase profit surges to $1.4B 💰 📱 News & Trends Monzo surpasses 13 million customers as business clients grow (3 minute read) UK-based neobank Monzo has reached over 13 million total users, with its business banking arm more than doubling its client base to 500,000. The company attributes its momentum to strong adoption of features like joint accounts, budgeting tools, and paid subscriptions Coinbase profit surges to $1.4B on crypto investment gains, but revenue misses (3 minute read) Coinbase reported $1.4 billion in Q2 profit ($5.14 EPS), up from $36 million a year ago, fueled by investment gains—particularly from Circle's IPO. However, revenue came in at $1.5 billion, missing Wall Street's $1.59 billion forecast and falling 26% quarter-over-quarter due to lower market volatility. Transaction revenue declined to $764 million as spot trading slowed, though total trading volume rose year-over-year to $237 billion. Despite the revenue miss, Coinbase shares are up 52% in 2025 amid strong crypto sentiment and plans to become an “everything exchange” focused on tokenization and institutional partnerships. Mastercard backs open banking amid regulatory shifts and JPM data fees (2 minute read) Mastercard CEO Michael Miebach voiced strong support for open banking, calling consumer-consented data sharing a “winning proposition over time.” His remarks follow JPMorgan's move to charge fintechs for data access and the Trump administration's rollback of a Biden-era open banking rule. Miebach praised global adoption in Europe and Australia and emphasized Mastercard's 2020 Finicity acquisition as a strategic bet on this trend. He also highlighted the company's $2.65 billion acquisition of Recorded Future to bolster AI-powered cybersecurity amid rising fraud risks. Despite Capital One shifting some debit volume to Discover, Mastercard expects minimal revenue impact in 2025. Klarna and Afterpay hit pause on sharing BNPL data with credit bureaus (3 minute read) Klarna and Afterpay say they'll withhold most “buy now, pay later” (BNPL) data from credit bureaus until assured it won't unfairly harm users' credit scores. While Affirm now shares all consumer data with Experian and TransUnion, Klarna only reports longer-term loans, not its popular Pay-in-4 product. Scoring firms like FICO have introduced new models to treat BNPL data more fairly, but providers argue the U.S. credit system isn't ready. TransUnion expects to begin incorporating BNPL data soon, but tensions remain over interpretation and impact on borrower creditworthiness. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Gen Z isn't different. They're digital by default (7 minute read) Gen Z uses digital tools for nearly every aspect of daily life, from saving and spending to healthcare and income generation. They log 425 digital activity days a month, more than any other generation. They save 36% of their income (10 points above average), are 3x more likely to use credit-builder products, and adopt side hustles as financial strategy, not survival. From using Apple Pay, Venmo, and grocery apps to preferring healthcare with transparent pricing and telehealth, Gen Z's habits aren't unique, they're just ahead of the curve Toast outlines 29% fintech and subscription gross profit growth for 2025 as Australia launch and Amex partnership drive expansion (2 minute read) Toast posted $161 million in adjusted EBITDA and $80 million in GAAP operating income for Q2, with free cash flow hitting $208 million thanks to strong profitability and seasonal tailwinds. Annualized recurring revenue rose 31% year-over-year, with fintech gross profit up 30% and subscription gross profit climbing 43%. Gross profit margins expanded by 8 points to 35%, while total GPV hit $50 billion despite a slight dip in GPV per location. He emphasizes solving end-to-end workflows, expanding ACV through upsells and new modules, and treating go-to-market like a product. ServiceTitan's strategy focused on deep customer understanding, strong retention, and layering financial services to increase stickiness in the home services industry. 💻 Launches & Products Wix launches financial suite to empower merchants with banking, credit, and cash flow tools (2 minute read) Wix has launched a new Financial Suite to give merchants integrated access to business banking, credit lines, and cash flow tools through its platform. Developed with partners like Melio and Breakout, the suite includes smart dashboards for tracking payouts, forecasting cash needs, and automating financial operations. With over 1 million businesses using Wix, the company aims to become a one-stop shop for merchant financial management, further deepening its fintech strategy alongside its website and commerce services. Remitly launches wallet for cross-border stablecoin payments (2 minute read) Remitly is one of the first digital remittance companies to support fiat off-ramps for crypto platforms like Novi (a Meta Platforms company) and Coinbase. Its global payout network and compliance and licensing infrastructure enabled wallet providers to convert digital assets into local currency, bridging the gap between Web3 innovation and the real-world needs of remittance customers. Payroc expands global payment capabilities with BlueSnap acquisition (2 minute read) Payroc has acquired BlueSnap to enhance its international payment processing capabilities and expand its embedded payments offering. The deal enables Payroc to support more complex global merchants, adding BlueSnap's cross-border tools and alternative payment methods to its portfolio. This marks another step in Payroc's M&A-driven growth strategy as it builds out a full-stack, end-to-end payments platform. 🎁 Miscellaneous Marqeta completes acquisition of Transact Payments to expand global issuing reach (2 minute read) Marqeta has finalized its acquisition of European BIN sponsor Transact Payments, giving it a foothold in the UK and EEA and enabling direct card issuing and processing in those regions. The deal allows Marqeta to bypass third-party partners and control its own regulatory infrastructure in Europe, expanding its global footprint. CEO Simon Khalaf said the move positions Marqeta to serve fintech and embedded finance customers with faster time to market and greater flexibility across borders. Alaan raises $48M Series A to scale AI-powered spend management across MENA (3 minute read) UAE-based fintech Alaan raised $48M in Series A funding—one of the largest in MENA—led by Peak XV Partners, with backing from Y Combinator, 468 Capital, and prominent regional founders. Alaan's AI-driven spend management platform, launched in 2022, serves over 1,500 finance teams across firms like Careem and G42, automating VAT extraction and reconciliation to save 1.5M+ hours of manual work. Already profitable, Alaan plans to double down on Saudi expansion and scale its AI automation capabilities with the new funding. BlackRock Advisers to Invest Up to $1 Billion With LendingClub (3 minute read) The agreement allows LendingClub to offload loan risk while tapping into institutional demand for high-yield consumer credit, especially as personal loan volumes rebound in a higher-rate environment. The move also reflects BlackRock's growing appetite for direct lending assets and marks one of the largest commitments to a U.S. consumer loan marketplace in recent years. How to increase your surface area for luck and serendipity (3 minute read) Increasing your “surface area” for luck means consistently sharing your thinking—especially when it's raw or incomplete. By putting ideas into the world early and often, asking thoughtful questions, and engaging publicly, you create more ways for others to find you, collaborate, and offer opportunities. Visibility isn't vanity, it's a strategy for attracting serendipity. ⚡️ Quick Links Ramp raised $500M to build the future of finance (4 minute read) This is the future as envisioned by Eric Glyman, CEO and Co-founder of Ramp: a finance department reshaped by AI agents that eliminate busywork and elevate strategic impact. US Household Debt Reaches New Record (2 minute read) Americans' total household debt rose to a record $17.69 trillion in the second quarter of 2025, up $212 billion from Q1. Square outshines rivals with sleekest, cheapest handheld POS (1 minute read) Square's new handheld POS is the thinnest, lightest, and most affordable option available, outperforming Toast, Clover, and Shift4 on hardware specs and pricing. text: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-11 Chime's strong earnings 📈, Ripple buying Fortress Trust 🤝, Brex's major win in EU 🚀 📱 News & Trends Ripple to buy stablecoin platform Fortress Trust for $200 million (3 minute read) Ripple is acquiring stablecoin infrastructure startup Fortress Trust for $200 million, marking another step in its strategy to expand beyond crypto payments. The deal includes Fortress' Nevada trust license and technology for issuing and managing tokenized assets. Ripple also plans to integrate the platform into its upcoming U.S. dollar stablecoin, deepening its foothold in regulated digital finance. IPO hopeful Brex scored major win to sell in the EU, plans UK expansion (2 minute read) Financial services provider Brex on Thursday announced that it achieved a major milestone: it is now licensed in the European Union. That means it can now directly issue credit and debit cards and offer its spend management products to any business in all 30 EU countries without any workarounds. Chime posts strong first earnings as a public company, stock returns to IPO price (3 minute read) Chime, a U.S. neobank serving over 8M customers earning up to $100K annually, reported a strong first quarter since its June IPO. The company offers checking, savings, overdraft protection, credit-building, and cash advances through its mobile-only platform, delivering services at a lower cost than traditional banks. Shares debuted at $27, briefly hit $40, and now hover just above IPO levels. CEO Chris Britt says Chime has less than 5% penetration in its target market of 200M Americans, positioning it for significant growth in spending, saving, credit, and lending. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports The Tokenization of Everything (15 minute read) In 2025, financial markets face a new set of problems, which will be resolved through turning assets recorded in accounting systems into tokens. In turn, this will be unlocked by a new market structure bill currently working its way through the Senate called the CLARITY Act. Just as GENIUS was an inflection point for institutional stablecoin adoption, CLARITY will begin the tokenization of everything. Trump Asks Bank CEOs to Pitch on Fannie, Freddie Stock Offerings (3 minute read) Donald Trump is meeting with top banking CEOs, such as JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon, Goldman's David Solomon, and Bank of America's Brian Moynihan, to solicit their proposals on monetizing Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac, including potentially launching major public stock offerings. Both mortgage giants, currently profitable and under conservatorship since 2008, have repaid federal loans and now present the government with a window to tap into a possible lucrative financing opportunity. 💻 Launches & Products Citizens delivers open finance payment features and direct deposit tools (2 minute read) Citizens has rolled out two new mobile-first capabilities aimed at simplifying recurring payments and payroll deposits. “Switch Payments” allows customers to instantly update payment details for subscriptions like Netflix or Amazon directly within the app, while a new direct deposit tool enables partial or full paycheck redirection to Citizens accounts without employer coordination. Both features are powered by Mastercard's open finance technology, highlighting the bank's push toward frictionless money movement. Binance launches instant Mastercard withdrawals for European users (2 minute read) Binance has introduced a feature that allows European customers to convert crypto to euros and withdraw funds to eligible Mastercards in near real time via its “Buy & Sell” service. Powered by Mastercard Move, the service offers a faster alternative to bank transfers. There are plans to expand beyond euros. The move reflects growing integration between crypto exchanges and traditional finance, though regulatory scrutiny may shape future global rollout. 🎁 Miscellaneous Global Payments Mulls More Divestitures (3 minute read) After its $24.25B acquisition of Worldpay, Global Payments is reevaluating its portfolio and may sell additional businesses to sharpen its strategic focus—this includes having already divested its issuer and payroll software units. The company has begun integration planning and has been touting its unified Genius point-of-sale brand, though some analysts remain skeptical of its ability to keep pace with competitors. Block courts teen app users (2 minute read) Block is placing strategic emphasis on retaining its younger clientele through Cash App, recognizing teens as vital to long-term platform growth. The initiative includes enhancing features and safety protocols to engage under-18 users while ensuring compliance and trust. ⚡️ Quick Links Shopify launches AI agent shopping tools: Checkout Kit, Shopify Catalog, and Universal Cart (3 minute read) Shopify is rolling out three new tools to make it easier for AI agents to integrate shopping: Checkout Kit for in-chat transactions, a Shopify Catalog for instant product search, and a Universal Cart for cross-store purchases. When Will You Be Able to Buy a Pizza With Stablecoin? (3 minute read) Use of digital assets—including stablecoins—has grown steadily in cross-border transactions due to lower costs, reduced friction, and faster settlement times. Google Finance gets AI-powered overhaul with chatbot and advanced market tools (2 minute read) Google Finance is rolling out an AI-driven redesign in the U.S., adding a chatbot that can answer complex market questions. These fees are now expected to drop to around $0.10 to $0.12 per transaction, nearly a 50% reduction. 💻 Launches & Products Your biggest OpEx line item runs on... Excel? (Sponsor) Headcount makes up 70% of OpEx - but it's still managed in half a dozen spreadsheets and disconnected HR systems. TeamOhana uses AI agents to bring everything together - helping early adopters like SeatGeek and Scale AI deliver forecasts in 2 hours, not 2 weeks. Read more about the new workforce management playbook → Coinbase Relaunches Stablecoin Bootstrap Fund for DeFi Liquidity (2 minute read) Coinbase has relaunched its USDC Bootstrap Fund, a program that injects USDC into decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols to shore up liquidity and support ecosystem growth. Originating in 2019, the fund initially deployed USDC into platforms like Compound and dYdX via smart contracts to improve access to capital for developers. This revival underscores Coinbase's continued commitment to bolstering DeFi infrastructure through stablecoin-enabled liquidity. Paymentus rolls out generative AI to enhance digital bill payments (2 minute read) Paymentus is integrating generative AI into its bill payment platform to provide agents with real-time, personalized customer context—like billing or policy details—during service interactions. CEO Dushyant Sharma said the move aims to reduce friction and improve efficiency across industries, including utilities, government, telecom, and healthcare. The initiative is part of Paymentus' strategy to embed AI into its cloud-native infrastructure, going beyond traditional uses like fraud detection. 🎁 Miscellaneous Hedge Funds Push Into Private Equity: Coatue's Next Big Bet (3 minute read) Coatue is moving beyond its hedge fund and venture roots, launching a private equity business led by Peter Wallace, formerly Blackstone's Head of Core Private Equity. The shift signals a push toward becoming a multi-strategy “capital aggregator” that offers LPs exposure across hedge funds, PE, and venture capital. Analysts note PE's more stable capital base and long-term economics, but question how Coatue will compete with tech-focused PE veterans like Thoma Bravo and Silver Lake. Shift4 Powers Crypto Payments on Blue Origin Spaceflights (3 minute read) Blue Origin has begun accepting cryptocurrency and stablecoin payments—including Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, USDT, and USDC—for its New Shepard space tourism flights. Through this integration, powered by Shift4's payment processing infrastructure, customers can book and pay for space travel using wallets like Coinbase and MetaMask, bringing fast, international crypto transactions into the luxury space tourism experience. Shift4's leadership expressed excitement about extending "the future of commerce" beyond Earth, highlighting high-end travel as a frontier for digital asset adoption. ⚡️ Quick Links Stripe businesses can now accept Pix in Brazil (2 minute read) Thanks to the partnership with Latin American platform Ebanx, businesses on Stripe can now offer Pix, a wildly popular instant payment method developed by the Central Bank of Brazil. Visa joins $4.9 million round in stablecoin-compliant payment platform HoneyCoin (3 minute read) The funding round was led by Flourish Ventures, with participation by Visa, TLcom, Stellar Development Foundation, Lava, Musha Ventures, 4DX Ventures, and Antler. Deep Dive: What Shopify's AI Storefront Updates Mean for Commerce and Payments (3 minute read) Shopify is betting big that conversational AI agents will become the new storefronts of the internet – and it's rolling out some ambitious tools to make that happen. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-18
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-18 Klarna IPO Nears 🚀, Payments Chains 💰, Google Finance revamped 💲 📱 News & Trends Klarna Bolsters Earnings as IPO Nears (3 minute read) Klarna reported Q2 revenue up 20% year-over-year to $823 million, with adjusted operating profit of $29 million despite a wider net loss from restructuring costs. Active users jumped 31% to 111 million, while delinquency rates fell to 0.89%, signaling stronger consumer repayment trends. With market volatility easing, the BNPL giant is again weighing a U.S. IPO as soon as September, alongside new partnerships with Walmart, Stripe, and eBay. Adyen Misses Earnings, Cuts 2025 Revenue Guidance (2 minute read) Adyen shares plunged 17% after the Dutch payments firm reported weaker-than-expected earnings and lowered its 2025 revenue outlook. First-half net revenue rose 20% to €1.09B, but tariff disruptions and a weaker U.S. dollar hit growth, particularly among major Asia-Pacific retail clients. EBITDA rose 28% to €544M with a 50% margin, though still short of consensus, while processed volumes edged up just 5% and digital volumes fell 9%. OpenAI Staffers Plan $6B Stock Sale at $500B Valuation (4 minute read) Current and former OpenAI employees are set to sell roughly $6 billion in shares to SoftBank, Dragoneer, and Thrive Capital in a secondary deal that would value the company at $500 billion, making it the world's most valuable startup, ahead of SpaceX. The sale comes on top of SoftBank's $40 billion commitment to OpenAI's ongoing funding round at a $300 billion valuation. With revenue expected to triple this year to $12.7 billion and GPT-5 recently launched, the deal gives employees liquidity while underscoring investor conviction in OpenAI's future growth. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Nubank Posts Record Growth and Profitability (3 minute read) Nubank added 4.1 million customers in Q2, bringing its total to 122.7 million, while revenue surged 40% year-over-year to $3.7 billion and net income rose 42% to $637 million. With monthly revenue per active customer at $12.20 versus just $0.80 cost to serve, the bank is delivering standout unit economics and an 83% activity rate. Growth remains concentrated in Brazil, while Mexico and Colombia lag, though product expansion across investments, credit, crypto, and loans is rapidly deepening engagement. Robinhood's Vlad Tenev Talks Tokenization, Retail Investing, and the SEC (1 hour podcast) Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev joined John Collison on a podcast for a wide-ranging conversation that covered everything from hoarding Bulgarian copper pots to how Michael Lewis misunderstood Flash Boys. Tenev pitched his vision to tokenize private company stocks (including Stripe), weighed in on retail investors' growing market influence, and shared what he'd do if he were SEC Chairman. The episode also touches on Robinhood's product roadmap, prediction markets, and Tenev's admiration for Frank Slootman. Payments Chains: The AWS Moment for Money (4 minute read) Circle and potentially Stripe (with Paradigm) are heralding a new era in payments by building their own hyperscale infrastructure—much like AWS did for cloud computing—a move that could consolidate billing, wallets, and wallets for expenses into a universal system. This trend aligns with broader fintech shifts—stablecoins and AI—as legacy players like Adyen lag behind while others (e.g., Nubank) are thriving with or without crypto context. Standout fintech offerings such as Lava Payments, Tracelight, Casap, and Fiscal AI are examples of emergent innovation in the embedded payments and AI space. 💻 Launches & Products Revolut Pay Surfaces in Booking.com App (1 minute read) A Revolut Pay checkout button has been spotted in the Booking.com mobile app during hotel reservations, signaling the fintech's continued push into mainstream travel and e-commerce payments. The integration highlights Revolut's strategy to scale its payments offering beyond its core banking app and into high-volume merchant platforms. Google tests revamped Google Finance with AI upgrades, live news feed (6 minute read) Google is rolling out a major refresh of Google Finance, adding AI-powered answers, advanced charting tools, and real-time news feeds. The platform now allows users to ask detailed financial questions and receive comprehensive responses with links, while new charts support indicators like moving averages and candlesticks. With expanded coverage of commodities and cryptocurrencies, Google is positioning the service as a stronger option to other finance sites or the use of AI chatbots for complex financial queries. Stripe is building a blockchain: Can openness survive branded rails? (5 minute read) Stripe is quietly working on a payments-first, Ethereum-compatible Layer 1 blockchain named Tempo, aiming to balance control with developer-friendly openness. Tempo is designed to integrate seamlessly into Stripe's ecosystem and support stablecoin-driven commerce. 🎁 Miscellaneous Coinbase Climbs to 25th in U.S. Trading Ranks After Deribit Deal (4 minute read) Coinbase's $2.5B trading volume on August 15 ranked it 25th among U.S. equities, as its acquisition of Deribit bolstered derivatives activity across spot, futures, perpetuals, and options. Deribit's $185B July volume and $30M in revenue highlight the deal's strategic impact, strengthening Coinbase's liquidity and institutional-grade infrastructure. Analysts say the move positions Coinbase to capture altcoin season momentum, with Ethereum already up 87% over the past 90 days amid expectations of Fed rate cuts. Klarna secures up to $26 billion in forward-flow funding for its U.S. “Pay in 4” BNPL product (2 minute read) Klarna has struck a multi-year agreement with U.S.financial services firm Nelnet, allowing Nelnet to purchase up to $26 billion of newly originated, short-term, interest‑free “Pay in 4” loans. The deal is designed to boost Klarna's growth in the U.S., freeing up scalable, off‑balance-sheet funding and potentially underpinning its long‑anticipated IPO ambitions. Opendoor Hunts for New CEO as Shares Jump (3 minute read) Opendoor CEO Carrie Wheeler has stepped down, with Shrisha Radhakrishna named interim president while the board, aided by Spencer Stuart, searches for a permanent successor. The announcement sent shares up as much as 16%, continuing a meme-stock rally recently fueled by hedge fund manager Eric Jackson, who is pushing for a stronger AI strategy and board shake-up. Despite renewed investor enthusiasm, Wall Street remains cautious, with most analysts rating the stock hold or sell as the company struggles to recover from its 2022 downturn. ⚡️ Quick Links The Fed winds down a program that kept tabs on banks' crypto activities (5 minute read) The Federal Reserve is retiring a specialized unit, launched in 2023 after the failures of Silicon Valley Bank and Signature Bank, that monitored banks' cryptocurrency and fintech activities. Airbnb Launches $0-Upfront Booking Option for US Stays (6 minute read) Airbnb has added a “Reserve Now, Pay Later” option in the U.S. that lets travelers book select stays with no upfront payment. Trump signs order allowing crypto in 401(k) retirement plans (3 minute read) An executive order signed by President Trump directs the U.S. Secretary of Labor to reassess fiduciary guidelines, possibly paving the way for 401(k) plans to include unconventional assets like cryptocurrency, private equity, and real estate. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-21
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-21 Gemini files for IPO 🚀, Klarna phasing out Jira and Confluence 💻, Payoneer + Stripe cross-border checkouts🌏 📱 News & Trends Crypto exchange Gemini files for US IPO (2 minute read) Crypto exchange Gemini, founded by Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss, has formally filed for a US IPO as it looks to capitalize on renewed investor interest in digital assets. The move comes despite a tough stretch in 2024, when revenues fell and losses widened, but Gemini says crypto's rebound in 2025 has strengthened its outlook. Nubank's stellar Q2 makes the case for a $250B valuation (15 minute read) Nubank's Q2 2025 results show the Brazilian digital bank reaching 122.7M customers with $3.7B in revenue (+40% YoY) and a 28% ROE that outpaces every major US bank. This article examines what's driving the LATAM fintech's hypergrowth beyond Brazil, how its unit economics compare to developed market challengers like Chime and Revolut, and why the path from today's $60B valuation to $250B is more feasible than it appears. Klarna CEO says company is phasing out Jira and Confluence for AI-first internal tools (1 minute read) Klarna CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowski said the company is replacing legacy SaaS like Jira and Confluence with AI-driven, internally built software designed around its own workflows. Lithic integrates Visa API to speed up card upgrades (3 minute read) Lithic, a card issuing processing platform powering next-generation financial experiences, has announced an integration with Visa Account Level Management (ALM) over Visa's Card Program Enrollment (VCPE) API, offering fintech partners faster delivery of premium card programs and improved card economics. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Hot vs Cold Wallets: What Works Better for Business Owners (5 minute read) Hot wallets give you the speed and flexibility your business needs daily. Cold wallets guard your savings with strong offline protection. The real smart move is combining them. Use hot wallets for running your operations and cold wallets for securing your profits. The Fintech OG Series: Jillian Williams and Dimitri Dadiomov (37 minute podcast) This podcast covers how fintech has evolved from the early days of Lending Club and marketplace lending to today's rebundling wave and AI-driven change. The guests talk about the future of money movement, risk and compliance, embedded financial products, and where they see the biggest opportunities ahead. The Next Wave of Stablecoins: From General Purpose to Purpose-Built (6 minute read) Stablecoins have surged to a $269B market, with USDT and USDC still dominating. However, the future may lie in purpose-built stablecoins — tailored for corporates, PSPs, or sector-specific workflows — offering issuers more control, compliance flexibility, and new revenue streams. Platforms like Codex are positioning themselves as the infrastructure for this shift. 💻 Launches & Products AlphaSense launches AI interviewer and Channel Checks (4 minute read) AlphaSense has rolled out an autonomous AI interviewer and Channel Checks, expanding its Tegus Expert Insights offering. The tools combine AlphaSense's AI with the world's largest library of investor-led expert transcripts to deliver real-time market signals across industries, surfacing demand shifts, pricing changes, and supply chain disruptions. Customers will soon be able to initiate AI-led expert calls on demand, compressing days of research into hours. Robinhood digests UK AI stock insights (4 minute read) Robinhood's Digests is an AI-powered feature that explains stock movements for UK investors in plain language. The tool, built on Robinhood Cortex, blends breaking news, analyst reports, technical data, and proprietary insights into quick summaries directly on stock detail pages. Payoneer and Stripe team on cross-border checkouts for SMBs (3 minute read) Payoneer is partnering with Stripe to expand its Online Checkout for SMBs selling direct-to-consumer across borders, starting in APAC markets like China and Hong Kong. The collaboration adds support for BNPL options and digital wallets, aiming to boost conversion, reduce fraud, and expand payment choice. Payoneer Checkout has already scaled to nearly $1B in run-rate volume, with revenues doubling year over year. Monzo plans UK mobile phone service (3 minute read) Monzo is developing a digital SIM service to enter the UK mobile market, aiming to diversify revenues and strengthen its valuation ahead of an anticipated IPO. The move could pressure incumbents like Vodafone, EE, and Three, while fintech rivals Revolut and Klarna are also eyeing telecom. 🎁 Miscellaneous Digital wallets push gift cards into the mainstream (6 minute read) Once tied to checkout counters, gift cards now live inside digital wallets and come with nudges like push notifications that boost redemption rates. Bank of America research shows their loyalty appeal runs deep, with 64% of consumers preferring gift cards as rewards. Payments players spar for sports contracts (4 minute read) Payments firms are racing to lock down stadium and team partnerships, with Global Payments leading recent deals with the Tampa Bay Lightning, Minnesota Twins, and Dallas Cowboys. Rivals, including Square, Fiserv, Shift4, and Priority, are also expanding in sports venues, offering not just transactions but real-time sales data and integrated fan experiences. Analysts say these tie-ups are becoming “table stakes” as payments firms seek brand visibility and deeper merchant relationships. ⚡️ Quick Links Should banks be telcos (and vice versa) (6 minute read) Banks and telcos have long eyed each other's turf, but past attempts faltered due to clashing business models. Soho House to go private in $2.7B buyout (3 minute read) A group of investors led by hotel owner MCR will take Soho House private in a $2.7B deal, ending the members' club operator's turbulent run on the public markets. SPAC king returns with new blank-check deal (5 minute read) Chamath Palihapitiya is back with a $250M SPAC called American Exceptionalism Acquisition Corp., targeting sectors like AI, crypto, defense, and energy. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-25
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-08-25 Visa exits US open banking 🏦, Klarna Q2 losses 📉, Fed may cut rates 💲 📱 News & Trends Klarna reports Q2 losses amid US expansion plans (3 minute read) Klarna is pushing ahead with its New York listing this fall, even as quarterly losses widened to $53 million compared to $18 million a year earlier. The BNPL firm is doubling down on U.S. growth, reporting a 21% revenue increase to $823 million while securing a UK license to expand into retail banking. Credit losses and delinquency rates have improved year over year, signaling tighter risk management as it scales. Small banks brace for big bills in open banking reset (6 minute read) The CFPB has reopened debate on its open banking rule, inviting industry feedback on who should bear the costs of data-sharing, API infrastructure, and ongoing compliance. Smaller banks face outsized challenges, as fixed costs like secure authorization systems and annual consent protocols can weigh more heavily on their margins than on larger institutions. Visa exits US open banking amid regulatory uncertainty (3 minute read) Visa has shut down its US open banking operations, citing regulatory uncertainty over consumer-data rights and looming bank fees for data access. The company will instead focus on markets like Europe and Latin America, which CEO Ryan McInerney called the “greatest potential” for open banking. The move marks a reversal from Visa's $5.3B attempt to acquire Plaid in 2020 and contrasts with Mastercard's continued push in the space. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Digital-led with a human touch: the next era in small-business banking (5 minute read) MSMEs account for over half of global GDP and 21% of banking revenue pools, yet many banks still lag in serving them effectively. Incumbents must adopt a digital-first model enhanced by human advisors, combining AI-powered onboarding, credit, and KYC automation with personalized RM support. With fintech challengers like Revolut Business, Monzo, and Qonto gaining share, banks risk losing ground unless they elevate their MSME offerings quickly. Coinbase's Brian Armstrong on scaling, AI, and banks embracing crypto (77 minute video) Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong joined Stripe's John Collison to discuss fighting North Korean hackers, lessons from early scaling, pushing engineers to use AI for coding, Coinbase's ambition to be users' primary financial account, and why traditional banks are warming up to crypto. Every Bank Should Tokenize Deposits (15 minute read) The passing of the GENIUS Act has legitimized stablecoins and created a land grab opportunity for the future of digital money. Tokenized deposits will be a massive opportunity for financial institutions to win market share in onchain finance. Far from being winner-take-all, stablecoins and tokenized deposits will co-exist and create a flywheel for onchain finance. They'll solve different problems for different people, and they'll each have features and drawbacks that make them better or worse. Banks: If you believe the future is onchain, then your balance sheet should be too. Race to systems of action part 2: sticky hero problems and how native AIs can win (6 minute read) Native AI challengers could displace SaaS incumbents by targeting “sticky hero problems” — high-impact, messy workflows that owners, not end-users, care about. By solving pain points often outsourced to low-value labor or consultants, AI can prove cheaper, more reliable, and better integrated. Conversational UIs, context parsing, and non-deterministic actions give AI an edge. A value roadmap is key to fending off incumbent counterattacks. 💻 Launches & Products Bank of America's AI assistant Erica tops 3B client interactions (2 minute read) Bank of America's virtual assistant, Erica, has surpassed 3 billion client interactions since its 2018 launch, now serving 42M clients with banking, payments, and financial guidance. The milestone highlights how incumbents are scaling AI-driven customer engagement at mass-market levels, positioning Erica as one of the most widely used financial AI tools globally. Earning high yield on your savings with Nook and Privy (4 minute read) Savings app Nook is offering yields of 7–15% by converting deposits into stablecoins (via Coinbase) and supplying them to vetted DeFi lending protocols like Aave and Morpho. Powered by Privy's self-custodial wallet infrastructure, Nook aims to make DeFi yields accessible with a clean, mobile-first interface and no seed phrases, offering users transparency, security, and instant withdrawals. The app has averaged 7.6% APY over the past year, far outpacing traditional banks' 4–5%. 🎁 Miscellaneous Powell says Fed may need to cut rates, will proceed carefully (6 minute read) Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled that rate cuts could come as soon as next month, warning that risks to the labor market are rising even as inflation remains elevated. The comments sparked both optimism in markets and renewed political pressure from Donald Trump, underscoring the Fed's delicate balancing act between protecting jobs and containing inflation. Square introduces grant program for small businesses (4 minute read) Square is rolling out a new initiative that gives selected sellers $10,000 grants along with access to accountants, financial therapy, technical assistance, and nonprofit resources. The Cornerstone Grant Program aims to provide “right-sized” capital—matching Square's average loan size—paired with expert guidance to help small businesses grow sustainably. CFPB to review open banking rule (2 minute read) The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) will begin a mandatory 10-year review of its 2025 open banking rule, which sets standards for how banks and fintechs share consumer financial data. The review will evaluate whether the rule is still effective or overly burdensome, potentially shaping the next phase of US open banking regulation. ⚡️ Quick Links LendingTree closes $475 million credit facility (3 minute read) A new $475 million in financing restores flexibility for buybacks and strategic investments as the company's insurance arm posted 21% year-over-year growth in Q2. Japan startup to issue first yen-pegged stablecoin (3 minute read) Tokyo-based JPYC will roll out the country's first stablecoin backed by the yen later this year after securing regulatory approval. Fed governor calls for cautious approach to fintech and stablecoins (3 minute read) Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller urged balancing innovation with strict oversight of stablecoins and crypto to protect financial stability. This whitepaper breaks these down into what you can actually expect to pay: an eye-watering $6.08M in implementation costs, and $7.38M annually in operations. Then there's the complexity: regional banking licenses, compliance, and compounding operational overhead. Expect 1.5+ years to market when going DIY. Read the full whitepaper to see the detailed cost breakdown, and how much you could save by partnering with an embedded finance provider - including a real-life case study of how one company saved $millions with Airwallex. Download here → Embedded finance: The build vs. buy playbook 📱 News & Trends Revolut Considers U.S. Bank Acquisition to Fuel Expansion (3 minute read) Revolut is considering hiring investment bankers as it decides whether to acquire a U.S. lender or pursue its own banking license to accelerate growth in the country. The fintech is preparing to launch savings products for U.S. consumers and recently ramped up marketing, even offering free subway rides in New York. Backed by talks to raise $1B at a $65B valuation, Revolut is positioning itself for a deeper U.S. push as deregulation and M&A opportunities open new doors. Affirm Surges on Strong Q4 Results, Beats Across the Board (4 minute read) Affirm shares jumped 12% after posting fiscal Q4 earnings of $0.20 per share, nearly double expectations, with revenue up 33% to $876M and net income of $69M versus a loss last year. Gross merchandise volume rose 44%, driven by partnerships with Amazon and Shopify, offsetting the loss of Walmart to Klarna, while its Affirm Card saw GMV growth of 132% and in-store spend up 187%. CEO Max Levchin pointed to strong consumer demand, rising usage, and AI-powered credit models, with upbeat guidance for FY26 fueling optimism. Chinese Money Launderers Move $312B Through U.S. Banks, Treasury Says (3 minute read) The U.S. Treasury estimates Chinese money-laundering networks funneled up to $312 billion in illicit funds through U.S. banks between 2020 and 2024, often working with Mexican drug cartels. Driven by China's currency controls, these networks now dominate the underground market for U.S. cash and are also tied to scams, human trafficking, and healthcare fraud. Banks are being urged to flag red flags, such as suspicious cash deposits from Chinese passport holders. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Building financial resiliency (15 minute read) Despite record access to financial services, most U.S. households remain fragile, with only three in ten considered financially healthy and 37% unable to cover a $400 emergency. The issue is no longer inclusion, but resilience—helping consumers build buffers, coordinate bill payments, access integrated small-dollar credit, and mitigate risks. Banks and fintechs already have the tools to create a more resilient choice architecture, turning resilience into both a consumer safeguard and a business growth opportunity. Open Finance Is Exploding Globally. Why is the U.S. Lagging? (7 minute read) Markets like Brazil, the UK, India, and Singapore are racing ahead in open finance thanks to clear regulations, interoperable data standards, and industry-wide collaboration. The U.S., by contrast, sits in the “Enthusiast” category, strong in private innovation but slowed by fragmented policy and regulatory uncertainty. With forecasts projecting a $7.2 trillion embedded finance market by 2030, U.S. banks risk ceding leadership unless they move quickly to align governance, API security, and cross-industry monetization strategies. “Consumer Protection” Is Already Failing Americans (15 minute read) TomoCredit, once known for its “No FICO” card, faces a wave of lawsuits alleging fake tradelines, aggressive and misleading debt collection tactics, and inaccurate credit bureau reporting. Despite clear red flags, investors like Morgan Stanley and Mastercard, partner banks, credit bureaus, and even regulators such as the CFPB largely failed to intervene, leaving consumers exposed. The saga raises a broader question: in an era of fintech growth and crypto adoption, are regulators enabling a golden age of scams under the guise of encouraging risk-taking? The Rise of Digital Banking and Fintechs: Branch Tomorrow (1 hour podcast) In this episode, Brett King speaks with Richard Turrin, an industry leader in fintech innovation and digital transformation and author of Cashless: China's Digital Currency. King and Turrin delve into the robust digital financial services landscape in China, which experienced significant digital disruption starting in the mid-2010s. They discuss how bankers there are still reeling from the unprecedented scale of disruption brought on by MYbank, WeBank, WeChat, and Alipay. 💻 Launches & Products Alloy and Mastercard Team Up to Accelerate the Onboarding Process (6 minute read) A new customer onboarding solution is being launched for banks and fintechs to tackle rising fraud and improve account openings. The offering combines identity verification with open finance to reduce fraud, which reportedly surged 60% in 2024, while also streamlining account openings. By embedding Mastercard's digital identity tools directly into Alloy's platform, the partnership aims to cut manual reviews, improve conversion rates, and deliver a more secure and consistent onboarding experience. Workday Expands Zuora Partnership for Smarter Billing (3 minute read) Workday and Zuora are deepening their collaboration to streamline revenue management for businesses juggling subscriptions, usage-based pricing, and hybrid billing models. The integration links Zuora's order-to-cash automation with Workday Financial Management, providing accurate revenue tracking, compliance support, and faster audits. By automating data flow into Workday's general ledger, the partnership promises a unified financial view that reduces manual work and improves precision across complex revenue streams. Wells Fargo adds alternatives to personalized UMAs (4 minute read) Wells Fargo is expanding its Personalized Unified Managed Account platform by integrating alternative investments through a partnership with fintech firm InvestCloud. The upgrade allows clients and advisors to blend traditional assets with options like private equity, hedge funds, real estate, and derivatives in a single account. The move reflects growing demand for portfolio diversification and positions Wells Fargo to offer more resilient, tech-enabled wealth management solutions. AI credit disputing tool launches for consumers nationwide to correct credit report errors (6 minute read) Kikoff has rolled out an AI-powered feature that helps users quickly identify and challenge errors on their credit reports. Since launching, the tool has been used more than 70,000 times and is offered free within the Kikoff app. With nearly half of U.S. consumers reporting credit report mistakes, this tool could make disputing errors less daunting while giving users greater control over their financial health. 🎁 Miscellaneous Circle, Paxos Pilot Tech to Verify Stablecoin Authenticity (2 minute read) Circle and Paxos have partnered with fintech startup Bluprynt to pilot cryptographic tools that trace stablecoins back to verified issuers, aiming to prevent counterfeits and impersonation attacks. The technology provides upfront provenance and transparency, which could aid regulators, auditors, and investors as new global stablecoin rules take effect. With USDC and PayPal's PYUSD among the largest tokens, the move signals a maturing stablecoin market seeking stronger safeguards against fraud. BNPL Isn't Just Lending, It's a Card Growth Engine (7 minute read) Debit cards paired with Buy Now, Pay Later are reshaping how consumers manage spending, offering installment flexibility without relying on traditional credit. This appeals to younger buyers and those avoiding debt, giving them budgeting control while still enabling larger or more frequent purchases. For banks and credit unions, integrating BNPL into debit transforms a once-static product into a driver of higher spend, engagement, and long-term loyalty. ⚡️ Quick Links South Korea's Top Banks Said to Meet Tether, Circle on Stablecoin Partnerships: Report (6 minute read) South Korea's largest banks are holding talks with Tether and Circle about distributing dollar-backed stablecoins and potentially launching a won-pegged digital token. TransBnk Raises $25M to Modernize India's Corporate Banking (1 minute read) TransBnk secured $25M in funding to bring modern fintech infrastructure to India's decades-behind corporate banking sector. Klarna files for U.S. IPO to raise up to $1.27 billion (2 minute read) Klarna has officially filed to go public in the U.S., aiming to raise up to $1.27 billion in what could be one of the largest fintech IPOs of the year. The Swedish BNPL giant will list on the Nasdaq under the ticker "KLAR" and plans to use proceeds for international expansion and AI investments. The IPO follows a strong rebound in Klarna's financials, including a return to profitability and growing traction in the U.S. market, where it now serves over 40 million users. Revolut valuation jumps to $75Bn with staff set for payout opportunity (6 minute read) Revolut's valuation has surged to $75B after a secondary share sale, giving employees the chance to cash out up to 20% of their holdings. The fintech's profits more than doubled in 2024, fueled by subscriptions and growth in wealth and crypto trading. While the move rewards staff, it also raises questions about whether the company's long-awaited IPO is being delayed—especially as executives eye New York over London for a potential listing. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Flexport CEO Ryan Petersen on navigating supply chain chaos and building fintech inside logistics (56 minute podcast) In a new interview, Flexport founder and CEO Ryan Petersen reflects on starting the company in 2013, scaling explosively during COVID, and managing the sharp market correction that followed. He also highlights the rapid growth of Flexport Capital, showing how logistics firms are evolving into fintech providers. The conversation offers founders lessons on leading through turbulence and building in overlooked categories. The state of AI in financial services in 2025 — Illuminate Financial's front-row view (5 minute read) Financial institutions are embracing early-stage AI startups, shifting from a reactive stance to actively adopting cutting-edge technology. Key themes include the rise of AI agents in security, DevOps, fraud, compliance, and capital markets; the “hidden crisis” in enterprise financial operations and the need for a next-gen CFO stack; and urgent infrastructure modernization away from legacy mainframes. This piece highlights the challenges of autonomy vs. control for AI agents and the growing ecosystem of infrastructure, security, and monetization tools needed to scale AI adoption. 💻 Launches & Products Collaborative headcount planning metrics cheat sheet (Sponsor) This free cheat sheet from TeamOhana helps align Finance, HR, Talent, and business leaders around a shared set of metrics for headcount planning, so everyone is speaking the same language. Other updates include booking deposits, prepaid appointment links, a “tip before payment” option, and Afterpay integration for online bookings. Square will share more details at a conference on October 8. It plans to update the roadmap quarterly, with customer feedback shaping feature development. Visa merges fraud and dispute programs into new compliance regime for merchants (3 minute read) Visa will enforce its new “Visa acquirer monitoring program” (Vamp) starting October 1, consolidating five fraud and dispute programs into one system. Merchants and acquiring banks will face new fees or fines tied to fraud and dispute ratios, with smaller merchants expected to feel the biggest impact. Visa estimates Vamp could cut global losses by $2.5B and aims to tackle both automated credential attacks and rising “friendly fraud” chargebacks, which hit $11B last year. Walmart-backed fintech OnePay is adding wireless plans to its everything app (6 minute read) OnePay, the Walmart majority-owned fintech, is expanding beyond banking into mobile service with the launch of OnePay Wireless, a $35/month unlimited 5G plan offered through a partnership with startup Gigs and powered by AT&T's network. The service, available directly in-app with no credit checks or fees, positions OnePay closer to becoming a U.S. super app, alongside its debit and credit cards, savings accounts, BNPL loans, and P2P wallet. Industry watchers note this model mirrors moves by Klarna and Nubank, signaling a broader trend of fintechs embedding wireless plans to boost user stickiness while lowering telecom acquisition costs. 🎁 Miscellaneous Top 10: fintech events around the globe (5 minute read) The fintech world's calendar is packed with major gatherings like Singapore FinTech Festival, Money20/20 (USA & Europe), Sibos, Paris Fintech Forum, and Hong Kong FinTech Week. Taking the #1 spot is Fintech LIVE London, the UK's fastest-rising event that has quickly become a premier stage for digital banking, sustainable finance, and AI-driven innovation—highlighted by its Global FinTech Awards ceremony. AI Collection Agents: Transforming Debt Recovery in Financial Services (5 minute read) Debt collection is changing fast. AI-powered systems are replacing traditional call centers, operating 24/7 to recover debts while maintaining customer satisfaction. For banks, fintechs, and collection agencies, this technology is becoming essential, not optional. Industry data shows AI collection strategies can boost recovery rates by up to 25% while improving customer satisfaction. This solves a key challenge: recovering money without damaging customer relationships. Stripe Terminal goes live in Japan (2 minute read) Stripe launched Stripe Terminal in Japan, helping Japanese businesses unify commerce experiences across online and offline channels. Stripe Terminal includes support for Tap to Pay on iPhone, as well as integrations with PayPay, the leading QR code payment method in Japan with over 70 million users, and Weixin Pay, also known as WeChat Pay. ⚡️ Quick Links Fed to host ‘payments innovation' conference next month (3 minute read) The Federal Reserve is holding a payments-focused event on October 21 to explore how crypto, AI, and tokenization could reshape the financial system. Affirm CEO on 0% APR loans: “Underwriting is hard and we're good at it and others aren't” (1 minute read) On Affirm's earnings call, CEO Max Levchin explained the company's lead in 0% APR loans by bluntly stating that underwriting is difficult and competitors simply aren't as good at it. TransUnion data breach hits 4.4 million consumers (3 minute read) A cyberattack on TransUnion has exposed the personal information of over 4.4 million people through a third-party application tied to its U.S. consumer support systems. While the credit bureau insists that no credit data was compromised, the scope of the stolen personal details remains unclear. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Stripe among first fintechs to file opposition to JPMorgan fees (6 minute read) Stripe has asked the CFPB to step in quickly against banks like JPMorgan that want to charge fintechs for customer data access. The company argues that allowing fees before rules are finalized could hurt thousands of businesses and millions of consumers. Stripe also urged the agency to consider enforcement actions and report anti-competitive conduct to regulators, escalating a high-stakes fight over open banking in the US. Fintech fixes AI's Unit Economics Problem (20 minute read) Every AI coding platform is burning cash at an unsustainable rate. The solution isn't cheaper tokens, it's becoming a fintech company. UI Generation (vibe coding) apps, Vercel, and Loveable, turn anyone into an engineer or a business. They generate functional, professional-grade websites, apps, and services in minutes. They're now some of the fastest-growing companies in history, making record revenues. However, they have a massive cost problem. This will drive the B2B era of AI to hunt for a new business model, and that business model will be embedded finance. The move follows Monzo's launch of 3% interest pots for under-16s, setting the stage for a new youth-focused savings battle. Both companies are betting that teaching kids how money grows will drive early brand loyalty and long-term customer relationships. Tempo: the blockchain designed for payments (6 minute read) Stripe and Paradigm have spun out a new blockchain, Tempo, designed specifically to support stablecoins and real-world payments. Unlike much of today's crypto infrastructure, which is built around trading, Tempo is optimized for use cases like payroll, remittances, and 24/7 settlement. With input from partners like Visa, Shopify, Deutsche Bank, Nubank, and OpenAI, Tempo aims to bring large enterprises onchain and expand crypto adoption beyond speculation. 🎁 Miscellaneous Fiserv buys CardFree to expand Clover in hospitality (2 minute read) Fiserv acquired San Francisco–based CardFree, a restaurant and hotel payments software provider, to deepen Clover's reach into hospitality. CardFree, whose clients include Dunkin' and Lazy Dog Restaurants, will bring capabilities like drive-through, kiosks, and loyalty integrations into Clover and Commerce Hub. While financial terms weren't disclosed and analysts expect the deal to be “immaterial” to earnings, Fiserv sees it as a strategic step to help Clover scale with multi-location businesses. The launch follows BBVA's recent entry and Deutsche Bank's push to grow retail offerings, underscoring intensifying competition in Europe's largest economy. ⚡️ Quick Links The student debt tsunami: systemic risks and the rise of fintech solutions (2 minute read) U.S. student debt has ballooned to $1.814 trillion, with delinquency rates climbing to 11.3%, creating ripple effects across housing, credit markets, and disposable income. Its platform processes home equity loans in just 10 days, far faster than the 42-day industry average. The company's $787.5 million IPO values it at $5.29 billion, positioning it as a fintech player redefining mortgage lending speed and efficiency. Lendbuzz Files for IPO, Targeting $1.5B Valuation (3 minute read) Following recent IPOs from Klarna and Chime, Boston-based auto finance fintech Lendbuzz has filed for an IPO, aiming for a roughly $1.5B valuation as it joins a wave of fintechs heading to the public markets. Founded a decade ago, the company uses alternative data and machine learning to underwrite borrowers with limited credit histories, funding loans through securitizations, warehouse lines, and portfolio sales to institutional investors. The deal will be led by Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan. BlackRock Explores Tokenizing ETFs After Bitcoin Fund Success (3 minute read) BlackRock is evaluating ways to launch tokenized exchange-traded funds, extending blockchain innovation beyond its $2B BUIDL money-market fund and blockbuster Bitcoin ETF. Tokenized ETFs could enable 24/7 trading, global access, and new uses as collateral in crypto networks, though regulatory and infrastructure hurdles remain. CEO Larry Fink has said every financial asset can be tokenized, signaling BlackRock's intent to lead in reshaping markets through blockchain. Elliott Builds Stake in Bill Holdings Amid Takeover Buzz (3 minute read) Elliott Management has acquired at least 5% of Bill Holdings, becoming one of its top five investors and adding to pressure already applied by Starboard Value's recent 8.5% stake. Bill, once valued at $34B but now down 85% from its peak, processes over $300B in SMB payments annually and is expanding into larger clients while funding buybacks with its $400M cash pile. The move comes as depressed fintech valuations spark buyouts—competitors Coupa, AvidXchange, and Melio have all been taken private or sold in recent years. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Figure Technologies CEO on IPO: ‘Blockchain lets us displace trust with truth' (6 minute video) Figure Technologies recently went public under the ticker FIGR after raising more than $780 million, with strong investor interest in its blockchain-first model. The company has already facilitated $17B in loan originations and $55B in transactions while generating $340M in revenue and $100M+ in profit last year. Pipe uses AI to analyze six months of anonymous transaction data to determine offers, with 98% of applications approved and funds typically delivered within 24 hours. The flexible repayment model aligns with revenue flow, helping restaurants expand and manage cash flow more effectively than traditional loans. Revolut introduces Pay by Bank option (3 minute read) Revolut has rolled out a new account-to-account payment feature that lets customers pay merchants directly from their bank app, bypassing traditional card networks. The move comes as Pay by Bank adoption climbs in the UK, where monthly open banking transactions jumped from 15 million to 27 million over the past year, now serving 14 million users. For merchants, the tool promises lower costs and fewer fraud risks, while customers get a faster, card-free checkout experience. If approved, the move would mark the first instance of tokenized securities being allowed to trade on a major U.S. stock exchange, and also signify the most ambitious attempt yet by an exchange operator to bring blockchain-based settlement into the national market system. Crossmint's end-to-end infrastructure handles wallets, compliance, and settlement, enabling MoneyGram to scale its $42bn remittance network into a broader peer-to-peer platform. Built as open-source infrastructure over Stripe, it acts as a database for payments—tracking usage, managing feature access, and supporting pricing models like subscriptions, credits, and rollovers in just a few functions without webhooks. With hundreds of AI apps and 40 YC startups already relying on it, Autumn is positioning itself as a flexible backbone for the fast-growing AI economy. Payments firms, including Fiserv, FIS, Visa, Circle, and Nacha, are lobbying Treasury for a role, eyeing high-volume, recurring government contracts. Fiserv's enterprise payments chief said the move will trigger formal business awards, with the Bureau of Fiscal Service expected to coordinate agency adoption after the deadline. ⚡️ Quick Links Groww's IPO to unlock $2 bn founder fortune, multi-bagger for Peak XV & Y Combinator (2 minute read) India's largest retail investing platform is heading to market with an $800M IPO at a $9B valuation, setting up one of the country's most profitable tech listings to date.
