Walmart-backed fintech OnePay is bringing crypto to its banking app (4 minute read) Walmart-backed fintech OnePay will soon enable users to buy, sell, and hold bitcoin and ether through its mobile app, partnering with startup Zerohash for trading and custody services. The move positions OnePay—already offering savings, cards, BNPL, and P2P payments—as a contender in the emerging US “super app” race. With its app integrated across Walmart's checkout ecosystem and now ranked fifth in Apple's finance category, crypto support could help OnePay compete directly with PayPal, Venmo, and Cash App. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Why Financial Services Are Betting Big on AI-Powered Analytics (8 minute read) Legacy core banking platforms weren't built to talk to modern AI tools, and the regulatory requirements around data handling add another layer of complexity that doesn't exist in other industries. Most AI implementations fail in financial services: not from bad technology, but from underestimating the infrastructure work required before AI can deliver value. The stablecoin duopoly is ending (3 minute read) The dominance of Circle and Tether is waning as stablecoin market dynamics evolve, with issuers likely needing to share yield more broadly. This pressure isn't unique to stablecoins—depositors globally are earning near-zero interest, with $4T of US deposits and most EU savings earning under 0.5%. As competition increases and consumers demand fairer returns, yield distribution could reshape the broader financial landscape. Agentic Checkout: Stripe + OpenAI's new protocol (15 minute read) This blog unpacks 3 key announcements. First, OpenAI launched commerce in ChatGPT, and the checkout is powered by Stripe. This lets users find and actually buy things in ChatGPT and reduces user friction. Second, OpenAI and Stripe open-sourced a protocol for any merchant to sell via AI Agents. Called the Agentic Commerce Protocol, it builds checkouts from SKUs. Third, Stripe also launched “Shared Payment Tokens.” This lets any payment method be collected by the agent, secures it, and sends it to the merchant, who can send it to their payments processor (e.g., ChatGPT could potentially use your card, PIX, or stablecoin balance, although today, it's just Apple Pay, Google Pay, and cards). 💻 Launches & Products Coinbase App Enables Peer-to-Peer Payments with USDC Stablecoin (6 minute read) Coinbase has rolled out a new peer-to-peer payment feature that lets users send its USDC stablecoin instantly and for free to anyone via phone number, email, wallet address, or ENS. The update allows up to 20 free on-chain transfers per month through Base and unlimited free off-chain sends, reflecting Coinbase's push to make sending crypto effortless. This move marks another step in the company's broader shift from being a trading platform to becoming a full-stack financial system. Ooredoo Fintech to join PayPal World, unlocking seamless global commerce (4 minute read) Ooredoo Fintech is partnering with PayPal to join PayPal World, a new platform linking major global payment systems and digital wallets to nearly two billion users. The collaboration will let Ooredoo Wallet users shop internationally, pay in local currency, and send money across borders with less friction. For merchants, the integration expands reach and payment flexibility without extra development work, positioning both companies at the forefront of inclusive global digital commerce. Bank of America rolls out GenAI assistant for Global Payment Solutions business (4 minute read) Bank of America has introduced Ask Global Payments Solutions (AskGPS), an in-house AI assistant designed to help staff handle complex institutional payment queries faster and more accurately. Trained on over 3,200 internal documents, the tool turns static knowledge into real-time insights, cutting response times from an hour to seconds. 🎁 Miscellaneous SEC plan for blockchain-based stocks pits Coinbase and Robinhood against Wall Street giants (5 minute read) The SEC is reportedly preparing to greenlight stock trading “on chain,” a move that would let investors buy tokenized versions of companies like Apple or Netflix directly through platforms such as Coinbase or Robinhood. The plan, aligned with the Trump administration's pro-crypto agenda, would grant temporary exemptions from existing stock trading rules to accelerate adoption. Checkout.com seeks Georgia banking charter (4 minute read) Checkout.com has applied for Georgia's merchant acquirer limited purpose bank charter, aiming to gain direct access to Visa and Mastercard networks and accelerate its US expansion. If approved, it would join Fiserv and Stripe as one of only three firms with this special charter. The move would let Checkout.com bypass bank intermediaries, cut costs, and speed up product innovation, with plans to launch chartered operations by 2026. Carta enters LP portfolio analytics through acquisition of Accelex (2 minute read) Carta has acquired Accelex, a software provider for institutional LPs, to launch LP Portfolio Analytics—its first product built specifically for capital allocators managing alternative assets. The acquisition expands Carta's platform to serve portfolio companies, GPs, and LPs, addressing long-standing data and workflow challenges in private capital management. CEO Henry Ward says the move positions Carta to build a “networked ERP” for the entire private capital ecosystem. ⚡️ Quick Links Payments conferences to keep on your radar for 2026 (6 minute read) The 2026 payments conference circuit will spotlight AI, open banking, and regulatory change as industry leaders look to balance innovation with compliance and fraud prevention. Crypto is a sleeper hit right now (1 minute read) Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong called crypto a “sleeper hit,” signaling renewed optimism in the sector as markets stabilize and talent flocks back to the industry. Government shutdown risks snarling Wealthfront's IPO plan (4 minute read) Wealthfront's push to go public has collided with the US government shutdown, which forced the SEC to furlough over 90% of its staff—halting IPO processing altogether. Made by Intercom, Fin: >> follows your business rules, >> maintains full audit trails, and >> integrates with any helpdesk. Try the #1 agent for financial services, free > 👉 Want to see how leaders from Anthropic, Boston Consulting Group, and Gary Vaynerchuck are implementing AI-first support strategies? Join 400+ executives at Pioneer summit on October 9th in NYC 📱 News & Trends Verizon names former PayPal boss Dan Schulman as new CEO (4 minute read) Verizon has appointed former PayPal CEO Dan Schulman as its new chief executive, replacing Hans Vestberg after seven years at the helm. Schulman, who has served on Verizon's board since 2018, will lead the company through its next growth phase, including the integration of its $20 billion Frontier acquisition. The board cited Schulman's record of “transformative leadership” at PayPal, where he tripled revenue, as key to guiding Verizon's customer and financial turnaround. Fifth Third to acquire Comerica for $10.9b (4 minute read) Fifth Third will buy Comerica in an all-stock deal valued at $10.9b, creating a ~$288b-asset bank expected to close by Q1 2026. Comerica holders get 1.8663 Fifth Third shares per share (about a 20% premium), with pro forma ownership ~73% Fifth Third / ~27% Comerica. The bank targets top-5 share in the Southeast, Texas, and California, No. 1 retail deposits in Michigan, and projects ~9% EPS accretion with no tangible book value dilution. Fintech Ramp launches fraud fighting, bill payment AI agents (4 minute read) Ramp has introduced new AI agents that scan vendor and payment data to catch fraudulent invoices before they're paid. During a 90-day pilot, the agents flagged over $1 million in fake invoices for dozens of corporate clients, including one blockchain firm that narrowly avoided a $50,000 loss. Backed by Founders Fund, Ramp is doubling down on AI automation after two mega funding rounds, positioning its platform to compete with Stripe and other fintechs racing to make bill payments both smarter and safer. Brazil's Nubank applies for US banking charter (5 minute read) Nubank has filed for a US banking charter with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, aiming to expand beyond its 122 million customers across Brazil, Mexico, and Colombia. The fintech plans to eventually offer deposits, credit cards, lending, and digital asset custody in the US. The move underscores the challenges global neobanks face entering the US market, following stalled applications from Bunq, Revolut, and Monzo. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Seizing the bank charter moment: implications for fintechs and banks (8 minute read) Fintechs are racing to secure US bank charters in 2025, with 20 filings already this year—an all-time high. The move marks a strategic shift as mature fintechs seek greater control over deposits, lending, and payments amid a favorable regulatory window. For some, owning a charter promises lower funding costs, improved margins, and freedom from sponsor bank dependence, though it demands heavy compliance investment and cultural change. The winners will likely be scaled fintechs that evolve into full-service banks, accelerating pressure on traditional players to modernize, acquire fintechs, and deliver digital-first, customer-centric experiences before the gap widens further. Winning in vertical AI: the 2 pathways for success (5 minute read) This post outlines two winning approaches for vertical AI startups: selling AI-powered software or services to customers willing to pay for efficiency gains, or using “hidden AI” to compete directly with incumbents by rebuilding operations with AI at the core. Founders' success depends on matching their technical or operational strengths to the right model—copilots and AI agents for augmenting workflows, or AI-native operators quietly redefining legacy industries. Narrow banking and the rise of stablecoins (6 minute read) Banking is quietly splitting in two: deposit-taking and payments on one side, lending on the other. Traditional banks blur the two by funding risky loans with deposits, but the trend toward “narrow banking” imagines deposits backed only by ultra-safe assets like government bills or central bank reserves. Private credit funds are taking over the lending role, while stablecoins are emerging as the new kind of narrow bank issuing digital “deposits” backed entirely by risk-free assets. AI's UI problem is actually a new era of software (6 minute read) AI's value no longer lies in its interface but in the invisible “work layer” that determines how systems reason, retrieve context, and take action. This shift marks the rise of “systems of work,” where differentiation stems from orchestration logic rather than design. As software moves beyond data ownership and UI polish, the next winners will be defined by how effectively their architecture turns AI capability into reliable business automation. 💻 Launches & Products You don't need to sacrifice flexibility for scale. Marqeta combines the scale and reliability of proven payments infrastructure with the flexibility and innovation of a modern platform—so you can move faster, reduce risk, and grow with confidence. From optimizing spend and cash flow to building seamless, rewards-driven customer experiences, Marqeta's solutions have you covered. When it comes to issuing and processing payments , don't sacrifice. Get the best of both worlds with Marqeta, and see what's possible. Learn more > Revolut aims to take on Indian banks and their ‘criminal' forex fees (6 minute read) Revolut has officially launched in India, targeting the $30 billion Indians spend overseas each year and the $600 million they lose to bank fees, which its India CEO calls “criminal.” The fintech aiming for 20 million users by 2030 has secured key regulatory licenses, including a prepaid payment instrument license, to offer UPI-linked wallets, multi-currency Visa cards, and same-day remittances. Snowflake's Cortex AI for financial services (4 minute read) Snowflake has launched Cortex AI for Financial Services, a suite of tools that lets financial institutions securely deploy AI models and agents within strict regulatory and data security frameworks. Developed with partners like FactSet, MSCI, Anthropic, and Salesforce, the platform's core feature is its managed Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server, which connects proprietary data with third-party sources for seamless AI integration. Square introduces integrated bitcoin payments and wallet solution for local businesses (2 minute read) Square Bitcoin, built for local businesses of all sizes, is the first fully integrated bitcoin payments and wallet solution. Consisting of Bitcoin Payments and Bitcoin Conversions, Square Bitcoin enables sellers to accept bitcoin payments with zero processing fees, automatically convert card sales into bitcoin, and manage their bitcoin alongside their other business finances, all within the Square ecosystem. 🎁 Miscellaneous AI RegTech Feedzai bags $75m at $2bn valuation (3 minute read) Portugal-based Feedzai has raised $75 million from investors including Lince Capital, Iberis Capital, and Explorer Investments, pushing its valuation above $2 billion. The company builds AI-powered tools for financial crime prevention, helping institutions combat fraud, scams, and money laundering. Feedzai's platform now protects over $70 billion in annual payments and prevents more than $2 billion in potential losses each year, as it expands products like Feedzai Orchestration and Feedzai IQ. Financial bubbles destroy everything. Industrial bubbles create the future. (6 minute read) Jeff Bezos drew a sharp line between “financial” and “industrial” bubbles during a rare appearance at Italian Tech Week, arguing that while financial manias like 2008 leave destruction, industrial ones—like the internet or biotech booms—leave behind transformative infrastructure. He warned that today's AI surge mirrors those past cycles: valuations may collapse, but the technology's impact will ripple through every industry. Bezos urged founders to learn from well-run companies, stay customer-obsessed, and focus on building long-term value—because when the dust settles, only what's useful endures. ⚡️ Quick Links Checkout.com launches one-click option for global digital payments (2 minute read) Checkout.com, a leading global digital payments company, today announced a series of innovations across its product suite, designed to keep merchants ahead in a rapidly evolving digital economy. Ramp's Geoff Charles to host Reddit AMA on scaling AI and finance automation (1 minute read) Ramp's VP of Product, Geoff Charles, announced that he will host a Reddit AMA on October 9 from 2–4 pm ET at r/ramp. Early pilots using Google's generative media models boosted app engagement by 15% and orders by 50%, with features like dynamic lookbooks and tailored product campaigns. The collaboration also includes AI-driven fraud detection using graph neural networks, combining creativity and security to reshape Klarna's consumer experience. Affirm backs Google Agent Payments Protocol (2 minute read) Affirm, a payment network that empowers consumers and helps merchants drive growth, today announced its support for Google's Agent Payments Protocol (AP2), an open, payment-agnostic protocol developed with leading payments and technology companies to securely support agent-led payments across platforms. Coinbase and Mastercard in $2 billion bidding race for stablecoin firm BVNK (3 minute read) Coinbase and Mastercard are reportedly competing to acquire London-based stablecoin startup BVNK in a deal valued between $1.5 billion and $2.5 billion. Coinbase is said to be leading the race, which would mark the largest-ever acquisition of a stablecoin firm, surpassing Stripe's $1.1 billion Bridge deal. BVNK provides stablecoin payment infrastructure for clients like Worldpay and Flywire, processing more than $20 billion annually amid surging institutional and regulatory interest in the stablecoin sector. Crypto liquidations near $10 billion after Trump's China tariffs (4 minute read) A historic $9.55 billion in crypto open interest was wiped out after President Trump announced 100% tariffs on Chinese imports, triggering a broad sell-off. Bitcoin and Ethereum led the plunge with $1.37 billion and $1.26 billion in liquidations, respectively, as total market capitalization fell 9% to $3.8 trillion. The event may rank as the largest liquidation in crypto history, with one trader reportedly profiting $190 million from short positions amid the chaos. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports NetSuite reveals its ‘Next' move at SuiteWorld (6 minute read) Oracle NetSuite unveiled NetSuite Next, a major update that infuses conversational AI, agentic workflows, and natural-language search directly into its ERP suite. The new “Ask Oracle” assistant lets users query data, generate dashboards, and take actions, while new AI Canvas and SuiteAgent tools enable collaborative problem-solving and autonomous task execution. NetSuite also announced BILL-powered payments, predictive planning tools, and new subscription analytics for SaaS metrics like MRR, ARR, and CAC payback. The Stablecoin Opportunity That Banks Are Missing (10 minute read) Banks could become wildly more efficient, unlock new revenue streams, serve their customers better, and cement their role in the financial system for the next 50 years. This is what consumers want, what corporates want, what regulators ultimately want, and what the crypto industry wants. The real and important question is whether banks win from tokenization or watch from the sidelines while fintech companies and GSIB banks eat their lunch. Open banking also helps banks: fintechs (5 minute read) Fintech executives argued that open banking benefits banks as much as startups, ahead of the CFPB's October 21 comment deadline for a revised rule. Leaders from MX Technologies, Ambrook, Andreessen Horowitz, and Y Combinator said shared data can help banks modernize and better understand customer needs. The Financial Technology Association is pushing to preserve key provisions of the 2024 rule, while banks' trade groups continue to challenge restrictions on charging data access fees. 💻 Launches & Products Parcel shippers: see how the 2026 FedEx rate increases will impact your costs (Sponsor) FedEx has announced a 5.9% average General Rate Increase (GRI) for 2026. Some services will rise less, but many will be significantly above average. This blog by Sifted gives an overview of what's changed , so you can proactively forecast and mitigate parcel expenses for 2026. Read the blog . Wealthfront launches Nasdaq-100 Direct to bring tax-efficient direct indexing to retail investors (8 minute read) Wealthfront has introduced Nasdaq-100 Direct, a portfolio that lets investors hold individual stocks from the Nasdaq-100 Index® to gain both exposure to large-cap growth companies and the tax benefits of direct indexing. Unlike QQQ or QQQM, it directly holds the stocks that make up the Nasdaq-100 Index, enabling tax-loss harvesting to potentially lower clients' tax bills. Jack Dorsey's Block targets card network fees in latest push (6 minute read) Block is launching a new program called Neighborhoods to help small businesses run branded rewards systems through Cash App while paying just a 1% processing fee, far below Visa and Mastercard's typical 3%. The initiative encourages consumers to pay directly with their Cash App balances, bypassing traditional card networks and keeping more value in Block's ecosystem. It's also a strategic move to reignite user growth on Cash App and strengthen integration with Square's point-of-sale systems, giving local merchants a Starbucks-style loyalty edge without heavy tech investment. Square stirs restaurant battle (4 minute read) Square is rolling out new AI-driven voice ordering and cost control tools for restaurants along with expanded bitcoin payment options and tighter integrations across its ecosystem. The updates aim to help eateries manage costs and attract more customers as competition intensifies with rivals like Toast, Clover, and Shift4. The move marks Square's continued push beyond small businesses toward larger restaurant chains while deepening links between its merchant base and Cash App users through loyalty and marketing programs. AlphaSense launches AI-powered financial data platform (6 minute read) AlphaSense unveiled Financial Data, a next-generation platform that merges quantitative financial datasets with qualitative market intelligence to give finance professionals a unified research workflow. The new suite uses domain-specific AI to deliver structured financials, KPIs, and transactions data alongside broker reports, filings, and expert transcripts, all accessible via natural language prompts. By blending verified data with generative search, AlphaSense aims to make due diligence, deal sourcing, and valuation analysis faster, more transparent, and decision-ready for private equity, venture, and corporate strategy teams. 🎁 Miscellaneous The Payments Journey: From Complexity to Clarity (5 minute read) Two decades ago, reconciliation was seen as a manual slog filled with pain points. Back then, teams spent countless hours buried in spreadsheets, trying to trace transactions, spot mismatches, and make sense of what should be a straightforward process. While technology has moved on, the core issues still linger: payments data is chaotic, fragmented, and often misunderstood. Bank of America fires up AI payments assistant (5 minute read) Bank of America has launched AskGPS, a generative AI-powered assistant for its Global Payments Solutions division, trained on more than 3,200 internal documents. The tool supports 29 languages and provides instant, context-specific answers for over 40,000 business clients, streamlining onboarding and client support. Built entirely in-house, AskGPS reflects the bank's broader push into AI, which already includes its Erica virtual assistant and internal AI tools used by 17,000 developers. Ant Group unveils trillion-parameter AI model Ling-1T (4 minute read) Ant Group, the fintech giant behind the world's largest mobile payment platform Alipay, has introduced Ling-1T, a trillion-parameter large language model designed to excel in software development and mathematical reasoning. This marks Ant's second trillion-scale model and highlights its rapid progress in AI research and infrastructure. With Ling-1T, the company aims to redefine reasoning performance and strengthen China's position in the global race against OpenAI, DeepSeek, and other leading AI developers. ⚡️ Quick Links SoftBank seeks $5 billion margin loan backed by Arm stock (5 minute read) SoftBank is in talks with global lenders to secure a $5 billion margin loan using shares of Arm Holdings as collateral, bringing its total borrowing against Arm stock to $18.5 billion. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-20
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-20 Tempo raises $500M 🚀, Plaid's consumer credit score 💯, Prediction markets 💰 You're using AI to detect fraud - but are you using it to improve CX? (Sponsor) Fintechs pour millions into fraud AI. Customer support still runs on templates and ticket tags. That disconnect costs you users. ⬇️ Download Ubiquity's free e-book to discover how AI is rewriting CX for financial products : 👀 How fintechs like BankMobile achieve 91.85% first-call resolution with AI. 🔮 Anticipating customer needs and personalizing interactions 🔍 Tips for building AI-enabled CX on a budget See how 150+ fintechs scale smarter with AI from Ubiquity. 📱 News & Trends Stripe-backed Tempo blockchain raises $500 million Series A at $5 billion valuation (4 minute read) Tempo, a payments-focused blockchain incubated by Stripe and Paradigm, raised $500 million in a Series A led by Thrive Capital and Greenoaks that valued the company at $5 billion. Investors include Sequoia, Ribbit Capital, and SV Angel. Tempo is an Ethereum-compatible Layer 1 optimized for high-throughput payments and is already working with OpenAI, Visa, Shopify, and Deutsche Bank. The raise marks another step in Stripe's crypto expansion, following its acquisitions of Bridge and Privy and its integration with Coinbase's Base network. Stripe adds stablecoin support for subscription payments (5 minute read) Stripe is rolling out stablecoin-powered subscription payments, letting businesses accept recurring transactions in USDC through the Base and Polygon blockchains. The feature, currently in private preview for US firms, allows users to pre-authorize future payments via smart contracts, solving one of crypto's long-standing hurdles. With support for 400+ wallets and integration into Stripe's full billing suite, the move could help global SaaS and AI companies cut cross-border costs and speed up settlements. Revolut nears close of $3 billion fundraising at $75 billion valuation (5 minute read) Revolut and its investors are finalizing a $3 billion fundraising round that values the London-based fintech at $75 billion, cementing its position as Europe's most valuable startup. The oversubscribed round, managed internally without bankers, combines fresh capital with secondary share sales from employees and early backers. Revolut, which now adds 1 million users every 17 days, plans to invest $13 billion to reach 100 million customers and expand into 30 new markets. The company is also pursuing a full UK banking license to accelerate global growth. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Prediction Markets - The Truth Seeking API (15 minute read) Prediction markets and events contracts are here to stay, and will come to define the future of markets and investment. However, they're also at the epicenter of a troubling trend towards consumer gambling addiction and harm. Yet the value of the information, and to the consumer of contributing information, has never been more needed. This information utility is the durable value of prediction markets. This information is also accurate. In the post-truth, AI-generated slop era, truth-seeking markets tell us things the AI can't. Over time, that will come to define the next era of finance and society. A window into modern loan origination (10 minute read) Home-improvement sellers now close deals by triggering instant point-of-sale financing that's underwritten in minutes by a bank-led, software-driven assembly line. Behind the scenes, facilitators connect specialty banks and private credit via forward-flow agreements. Merchants subsidize rates with a take-rate. Underwriting hinges on credit-file checks and primary-residence status, and terms range from 12-month deferred-interest promos to long fixed plans enforced via judgments that become liens rather than true collateral. The setup scales mid-ticket consumer finance without bloating bank balance sheets, shifting risk to credit funds, and tightening quickly if defaults rise. 💻 Launches & Products The case for demand-side resourcing: Preventing revenue leakage before delivery begins (Sponsor) Where is your revenue really leaking? Is it the hidden flaw in your process: a disconnected sales pipeline and resource planning? Read the strategic case for demand-side resourcing to unify sales and delivery data. Staff projects faster, end reactive guesswork, and stop revenue leakage before delivery even begins. Check out the guide by Certinia MrBeast files trademark to launch his own bank, “MrBeast Financial” (1 minute read) MrBeast has filed a trademark for a new venture called MrBeast Financial, signaling his intent to enter the banking space. The filing was submitted on an intent-to-use basis, which legally confirms plans to bring the project to life. If launched, the creator-led bank could challenge how younger audiences think about saving, spending, and financial trust. Financial data network Plaid launches a consumer credit score (5 minute read) LendScore is a new credit-rating system from Plaid that uses real-time cash-flow data to help lenders better evaluate subprime and near-prime borrowers. Unlike traditional scores that rely on historical credit behavior, LendScore updates dynamically based on income and spending, offering a 1–99 rating scale. While Plaid says the tool is meant to complement FICO, its vast data network gives it a unique edge and potentially sets the stage for a long-awaited shake-up in the credit scoring industry. Airwallex expands US footprint with OpenPay acquisition (4 minute read) Airwallex is deepening its presence in the US following the acquisition of California-based billing startup OpenPay, which provides software for recurring billing and payment orchestration. The deal, whose terms were undisclosed, will help Airwallex build what it calls “the first truly global billing platform” by integrating OpenPay's tools into its payments, card issuing, and expense management suite. OpenPay's co-founder and CEO, Lance Co Ting Keh, has joined Airwallex as general manager, as the company positions itself against rivals like Stripe and Recurly. 🎁 Miscellaneous Stripe's Bridge applies for national trust charter under new stablecoin framework (3 minute read) Bridge, the stablecoin infrastructure firm Stripe acquired earlier this year, has applied to the OCC for a national bank trust charter, joining Circle, Coinbase, Paxos, and Ripple under the new Genius Act. The charter would allow Bridge to issue and manage stablecoins and reserves within a federal framework, though not lend or take insured deposits. Co-founder Zach Abrams said the move aims to “tokenize trillions of dollars.” The OCC has yet to approve any trust charters this year despite growing crypto industry pressure. Basis Theory raises $33 million Series B to power agentic commerce (4 minute read) Basis Theory, an independent payments infrastructure company, raised a $33 million Series B led by Costanoa, with participation from Stage 2 Capital, Moneta VC, and existing investors like Bessemer Venture Partners. The funding will expand its enterprise-grade payment vault and support its leadership in “agentic commerce,” enabling AI agents to safely authorize and manage transactions. Founded by fintech veterans from Yodlee, Dwolla, and Klarna, Basis Theory offers a PCI-compliant, processor-agnostic vault that lets merchants securely control and program their payment data. Michael Saylor and Mike Krieger headline Money20/20 as fintech's biggest show returns to Las Vegas (6 minute read) Money20/20, fintech's largest US conference, is back in Las Vegas in two weeks with its most ambitious lineup yet, featuring over 10,000 attendees, 350 speakers, and the debut of the Money Awards, celebrating global fintech innovation. Michael Saylor will lead a keynote on Bitcoin and digital assets, while Mike Krieger, now Chief Product Officer at Anthropic, will explore how AI is transforming finance. Mexico's Plata raises $250 million at $3.1 billion valuation (4 minute read) Mexican fintech Plata raised $250 million in new equity funding, valuing the company at $3.1 billion as it prepares to launch full banking operations. Investors include Kora, Moore Strategic Ventures, and TelevisaUnivision. Plata received approval for a banking license in late 2024 and is finalizing audits to expand beyond credit cards into payroll and debit products. Founded in 2023, Plata now counts over two million users and 3,000 employees, joining Revolut and Nubank among fintechs transitioning into fully licensed banks in Mexico. ⚡️ Quick Links Morgan Stanley drops restrictions on which wealth clients can own crypto funds (5 minute read) Morgan Stanley is opening crypto access to all of its wealth clients, allowing investments in digital asset funds across any account type starting October 15. Citi rolls out AI prompt training requirement to most staff (5 minute read) Citi is now requiring nearly all of its 180,000 employees with access to AI tools to complete prompt training as part of a company-wide effort to raise AI fluency and productivity. Revolut's UK banking license may be delayed due to risk management concerns (6 minute read) Revolut's push to secure a full UK banking license has hit another snag, as regulators question whether the fintech's risk management systems can keep up with its rapid global expansion. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-23
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-23 OpenAI hires 100+ ex-bankers 🏦, Wayfair expands Affirm partnership 💰, Visa’s Trusted Agent Protocol 🖥️ Report: ecommerce friction is costing SaaS companies billions in lost revenue (Sponsor) Nearly every software company now sells online through PLG signups, self-serve renewals, or hybrid models. Payments and ecommerce are now central to every growth strategy. Cleverbridge's new Friction Report reveals where global revenue consistently goes unrealized and what's driving it: 96% of buyers expect to pay in their local currency, yet fewer than half of sellers consistently localize checkout. 1 in 3 buyers abandons a purchase when their preferred payment method isn't available. Only 6% of software companies say they sell globally “with ease.” See the key findings and learn how SaaS finance leaders are improving conversion yield across every market. 📱 News & Trends Wayfair expands Affirm partnership (2 minute read) This brings Affirm directly to checkout, just in time for Wayfair's Way Day sales event running from October 26-29 and the peak holiday shopping season. Whether buying a new dining table or holiday decor, eligible shoppers can now choose Affirm when checking out online or in-store across the entire Wayfair family of brands — Wayfair, Joss & Main, AllModern, Birch Lane, and Perigold. Amazon resolves major AWS outage that disrupted banks and global websites (4 minute read) Amazon Web Services restored operations late Monday after a widespread outage took down more than 1,000 apps and websites, including banks like Lloyds and Halifax, as well as Snapchat, Reddit, and Fortnite. The failure—linked to DNS issues in AWS's US-EAST-1 region—caused cascading disruptions and more than 11 million user reports. Experts warned that the outage highlights how much critical infrastructure depends on a few dominant cloud providers, with calls growing to diversify away from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google to improve resilience. OpenAI hires 100+ ex-bankers to train AI on financial modeling (4 minute read) OpenAI is running a secret project, Mercury, with more than 100 former bankers from JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, and Morgan Stanley training its AI to build financial models for IPOs, restructurings, and more. Contractors earn $150/hour and submit one Excel model per week, helping the AI learn industry norms like formatting and structure. The initiative highlights OpenAI's push to make its tech useful for professional services as it chases profitability at a $500B valuation. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports ) Amex downplays shutdown impact as premium customers keep spending (4 minute read) American Express executives said the company remains insulated from the economic effects of inflation and the government shutdown, citing continued strong spending from its affluent customer base. CEO Steve Squeri called the economy “bifurcated,” noting that Amex cardholders are spending more on travel and dining—front cabin airfare up 14% and restaurant spending up 9% year-over-year. The company reported $18.43 billion in revenue and $2.9 billion in net income for Q3, up 11% and 16% respectively, with overall card member spending rising 9%. Hidden AI: How startups can “eat” their AI to dominate markets (5 minute read) Equal Ventures partner Rick Zullo outlines the concept of “Hidden AI,” where startups embed AI deeply into operations rather than selling it as a product. Using portfolio companies Stand Insurance and Equal Parts, Zullo explains how targeting the most expensive parts of an industry's cost structure—loss ratios for carriers and OpEx for brokers—can create monopolistic advantages. Rather than chasing lower CAC or higher WTP like first-wave insurtechs, these firms use AI to transform underwriting and operations from within, building compounding moats invisible to customers but decisive in market share. 💻 Launches & Products Meet with Okta and SHI about identity security, get Beats Studio Buds or an espresso machine (Sponsor) Identity threats targeting financial services are getting smarter. SHI and Okta want to show you can go beyond authentication and stop attacks with AI-powered Identity Threat Protection and Identity Security Posture Management. Take a 30-minute meeting - walk away with security insights and your choice of premium gift. Grab your meeting slot Stripe adds SAFE fundraising tools to Atlas platform (3 minute read) Stripe announced that founders can now send, sign, and track SAFEs directly through the Atlas dashboard, expanding its suite of startup financial tools. Product lead Jeff Weinstein said the feature complements Stripe's existing products—Payments for revenue and Capital for loans—by supporting equity fundraising. Weinstein added that Stripe plans to make fundraising “easier, faster, and more fun” for founders worldwide and invited feedback on new features. Plumery launches cashback management capability to help banks deliver personalised rewards (5 minute read) Cashback is one of the most universally recognised loyalty mechanisms, yet many banks still struggle to deliver it due to outdated core systems. Plumery's new Cashback Management capability helps bridge that gap, allowing banks to design and deploy personalised cashback programs in weeks without re-platforming. With AI-driven reward personalisation, pre-built APIs, and full compliance control, Plumery gives banks the tools to boost engagement, retention, and revenue through modern, merchant-funded loyalty experiences. 🎁 Miscellaneous Bill Holdings to cut 6% of workforce amid activist investor pressure (4 minute read) Bill Holdings will lay off about 140 employees, or 6% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring aimed at improving profitability and focus. The move follows an agreement with activist fund Starboard Value, which holds an 8.5% stake and will add two directors to Bill's expanded 13-member board. Another activist, Elliott Management, also recently took a 5% position. CEO René Lacerte said the cuts will improve efficiency and free capital for growth, including new AI investments and embedded payment partnerships with Oracle NetSuite and Paychex. Banks chase AI-fueled efficiencies (6 minute read) Major US banks are betting on AI to streamline operations and cut costs, with Citi, JPMorgan, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs embedding automation and generative tools into everything from code reviews to customer support. Citi, leading the charge, says nearly 180,000 employees now use its in-house AI tools, with over a million automated code reviews freeing 100,000 developer hours weekly. While Citi and JPMorgan tout tangible productivity gains, Wells Fargo's CFO admits its “efficiency agenda” is still a work in progress. ⚡️ Quick Links Nubank's Mexico playbook hints at how it could disrupt US banking (4 minute read) Nubank's explosive growth in Mexico—driven by ultra-low costs, organic acquisition, and disciplined scaling—shows its playbook could upend US banking as it seeks a national charter. Kraken buys derivatives platform for $100M (3 minute read) Kraken has acquired Chicago-based derivatives platform Small Exchange from IG Group for $100 million, paving the way for a US-based suite of exchange-listed crypto derivatives. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-27
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-27 JPMorgan to accept BTC and ETH as loan collateral 🏦, NHL + prediction markets 🪙, Pay by Bank’s Potential 💰 Where 1,300+ institutions trade crypto with confidence (Sponsor) Looking for a reliable partner to provide clarity and structure for crypto trading? Galaxy Trading connects institutions to digital asset markets, with over $20 billion in derivatives volume last year. But Galaxy offers more than just reliable liquidity . → Support for tailored strategies across spot, derivatives, and lending → Currently serving 1,300+ institutional counterparties → Regulated by major authorities, including CFTC, FCA, FinCEN, and the NFA. Crypto markets are anything but quiet. Navigate them with Galaxy . 📱 News & Trends Stablecoin use for payments jumps 70% since US regulation (6 minute read) Stablecoin transactions for real-world purchases and transfers have surged since the US passed its first crypto regulation in July, with more than $10 billion moved in August alone — up from $6 billion in February. Business payments now dominate the market, representing two-thirds of total volume at $6.4 billion monthly, as companies turn to stablecoins to bypass slow international banking systems. The new federal rules under the Genius Act have given stablecoins fresh legitimacy, and even traditional players are moving to use them for cross-border payments. NHL inks deal with prediction markets Kalshi and Polymarket (4 minute read) The NHL signed multiyear agreements with prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket, the first such deals between a major US sports league and prediction markets. The partnerships let both firms use NHL data, logos, and official designations, signaling a shift as prediction markets gain legitimacy alongside traditional sportsbooks. Kalshi and Polymarket allow trading on event outcomes, operating nationwide even where sports betting is illegal. The move drew criticism from the American Gaming Association, which labeled prediction markets “backdoor gambling schemes,” while the NHL said it aims to safeguard game integrity through collaboration. American Fintech Council calls on CFPB to prohibit open banking data access fees (5 minute read) Following the CFPB's reversal of its open banking rule, the American Fintech Council is urging regulators to safeguard consumers' right to access and share their financial data without fees. The group warns that allowing banks to charge for data access would stifle innovation and harm millions who rely on fintech tools for everyday financial management. The move comes amid growing tension between fintechs and major banks, as the US risks falling behind other markets in open banking leadership. JPMorgan to accept Bitcoin and Ether as loan collateral (3 minute read) JPMorgan Chase will let institutional clients use Bitcoin and Ether as collateral for loans by year-end, marking a major step in Wall Street's crypto integration. The program, using a third-party custodian, expands on JPMorgan's earlier acceptance of crypto-linked ETFs as collateral. The move reflects growing comfort among big banks with digital assets amid lighter regulations under the Trump administration and crypto's renewed market strength. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Prediction Markets: Understanding Their Impact and Future (5 minute read) Platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket are redefining how people forecast events by turning collective sentiment into tradable market data. Their contrasting models, Kalshi's regulated approach and Polymarket's decentralized one, show how prediction markets can both complement and challenge existing financial systems. As fintech startups integrate insights from these markets into crypto payments and risk tools, they're uncovering new ways to enhance transparency and inclusion, though regulatory friction still looms large over mainstream adoption. Unlocking Pay by Bank's Potential (5 minute read) Adoption of ‘pay by bank' in the US is currently low (around 6.4 % of consumers report usage), largely because awareness is weak, not because of distrust or preference for cards. Growing infrastructure - faster bank-to-bank rails, open banking enabling smoother flows, and rising consumer interest—suggests the foundation is in place for growth. For merchants, the attraction lies in potentially lower payment processing costs and an alternative to payment-card networks, but replacing cards entirely requires nuance and proper use-case alignment. Fintech partnership and operational strategy guide (8 minute read) A new e-book from Matt Fong, Managing Director at Embedded Finance Treasury & Payments Sales, outlines five pillars of fintech success — from reconciliation discipline and cross-border infrastructure to banking partnerships, build-vs.-partner frameworks, and emerging payment methods like stablecoins and wallets. The guide aims to help scaling fintechs navigate operational blind spots with institutional rigor. Agentic payments memo (10 minute read) This memo outlines how agents will soon move more transaction volume than humans, requiring new payment rails optimized for real-time, machine-to-machine transfers. It explores challenges in identity, fraud, arbitration, and global settlement, and proposes “agentic payments” — a new layer enabling agents to transact autonomously via protocols rather than legacy bank systems. The memo frames this as a once-in-a-generation rebuild of money movement. 💻 Launches & Products Customer Success (CS) Maturity: A Self-Assessment for Services Organizations (Sponsor) Fragmented data across delivery and billing can cripple your CS strategy. Use Certinia's self-assessment to identify your maturity stage, move past silos and firefighting, and learn how to align your systems to move up the maturity curve. Take the self-assessment . Lloyds and Lolly unveil Pay by Bank self-service kiosks (3 minute read) Lloyds has partnered with hospitality tech firm Lolly to introduce self-service kiosks that enable direct bank-to-bank payments through Pay by Bank. Customers can scan a QR code and complete transactions via their mobile banking app, sending funds instantly to the merchant's account. The system promises faster checkout times, lower fees for businesses, and a glimpse into how open banking could reshape in-person payments. Zelle parent pursues crypto for international payments (3 minute read) Early Warning Services, the bank-owned parent of Zelle, plans to enable cross-border payments using stablecoins pegged to fiat currencies. The move would expand Zelle's reach beyond the US, aiming to make international transfers faster and cheaper by bypassing traditional rails. CEO Cameron Fowler cited improved US crypto regulation under the Genius Act as key to moving forward. Zelle processed $1T in payments last year across 151M accounts. Coinbase bitcoin rewards credit card gets full release (3 minute read) Coinbase has officially launched a bitcoin rewards credit card for US users following a long waitlist period. The card, issued by First Electronic Bank and powered by Cardless in partnership with American Express, offers 2–4% back in bitcoin depending on users' Coinbase asset holdings. Early adopters have already spent over $100 million collectively, and Coinbase says cardholders are engaging more deeply with the platform, depositing an additional $200 million in assets. 🎁 Miscellaneous Global AML Systems Spending to Reach $75bn by 2030, Juniper Research Finds (5 minute read) Global spending on anti-money laundering (AML) systems is set to more than double, rising from $33.9 billion in 2025 to over $75 billion by 2030, as institutions race to close compliance and monitoring gaps. Banks are expected to drive much of this surge, accounting for nearly two-thirds of total spending amid tightening regulatory oversight. Trump pardons Binance founder Zhao (3 minute read) Clemency was granted to Changpeng “CZ” Zhao, the former chief of the world's largest crypto exchange, after he admitted to anti–money laundering violations and served a brief prison term. The White House framed the move as correcting an overzealous crackdown on digital assets, while Binance praised the decision as aligned with a push to make the US a global leader in crypto. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech 100: The most promising fintech startups of 2025 (2 minute read) CB Insights unveiled its 8th annual Fintech 100 list, spotlighting the world's most promising fintech startups redefining the industry's infrastructure. Zip US expands partnership with Stripe (2 minute read) Zip, a digital financial services company that offers innovative, people-centered products for everyday Americans, has announced the next evolution of its partnership with Stripe, a programmable financial services business. Consumers increasingly concerned about AI scams (4 minute read) A new survey by Alloy reveals that 85% of US consumers believe AI makes it harder to detect financial scams, with bank impersonations and voice cloning among the most common tactics. Banks risk $170B profit hit from AI shift, says McKinsey (1 minute read) Consumers using AI agents to optimize finances could erode banks' margins by automating account switching and reducing deposits in low-interest accounts, McKinsey warns. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-30
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-10-30 PayPal + OpenAI 💰, Claude AI for finance 🧑‍💻, Citi + Coinbase 🪙 Outsourcing calculator for fintechs: compare nearshore, offshore, in-house (Sponsor) Most fintech companies think they have a handle on operational costs. Then they run the numbers and realize they're overpaying by 30-70%. Ubiquity built a free ROI calculator specifically for fintech operations. It factors in what generic calculators miss: compliance overhead, fraud prevention costs, and 24/7 coverage requirements. Input your current setup (team size, salaries, overhead) and see exactly what you could save with nearshore or offshore outsourcing. The calculator breaks down: Direct salary costs vs. outsourced rates Hidden expenses like office space, hardware, benefits Fintech-specific requirements (compliance, fraud ops, round-the-clock support) See your potential savings (2 minutes) 📱 News & Trends Citi to tie up with Coinbase to boost digital payments for institutional clients (3 minute read) Citi and Coinbase are partnering to develop digital asset payment solutions for Citi's institutional clients, with plans to expand globally. The collaboration will allow clients to deposit and withdraw fiat through Coinbase, bridging traditional finance and crypto. The firms also plan to explore stablecoin conversions as part of the rollout. The move follows the US Genius Act, which established federal stablecoin rules, and comes amid renewed crypto dealmaking encouraged by a more industry-friendly US policy environment. Barclays' $800 million fintech gamble marks a pivot from Wall Street to Main Street (4 minute read) Barclays is acquiring US fintech Best Egg for $800M to expand its consumer lending footprint with a digital personal loans platform. Best Egg, founded in 2013, has originated over $40B in loans and currently services $11B. The deal aligns with CEO CS Venkatakrishnan's strategy to shift focus from investment banking to retail finance and strengthen Barclays' US consumer bank. The acquisition follows recent asset sales and aims to boost returns and diversify income amid a broader push to rebalance risk-weighted assets. PayPal signs deal with OpenAI to become the first payments wallet in ChatGPT (4 minute read) PayPal will integrate its digital wallet into ChatGPT, allowing users to buy and sell directly through the AI platform starting next year. The deal positions PayPal as the first full payments wallet embedded in OpenAI's ecosystem, offering secure checkout, package tracking, and dispute resolution. CEO Alex Chriss said the move brings PayPal's verified merchants and hundreds of millions of users into ChatGPT's growing e-commerce network. The partnership follows OpenAI's recent integrations with Shopify, Etsy, and Walmart, marking a step toward 'agentic commerce'. Sequoia invests in AI tool that could replace junior bankers (4 minute read) Sequoia Capital is leading a new $50–$100M round in Rogo Technologies, valuing the New York AI startup at $750M, more than double its valuation from earlier this year. Founded by former Lazard and JPMorgan bankers, ROGO builds software that automates slide decks, IPO documents, and financial models, positioning itself as an AI “analyst” for investment banking. Customers include Tiger Global, Lazard, and Moelis. The deal underscores growing competition from OpenAI and Anthropic, which are both developing AI tools for finance. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Navigating the Future of Payments: A Guide for Key Players (10 minute read) The global payments industry, a sector that generated an astonishing $2.5 trillion in annual revenue in 2024, is at a turning point. The long-standing pursuit of a single, universally efficient system is giving way to a new era defined by competition among diverse market systems, each with its own rules and technologies. This transformation is not just a minor shift - it's a fundamental reshaping of how money moves between individuals, businesses, and governments. Global Banking Annual Review 2025: Why precision, not heft, defines the future of banking (10 minute read) Global banks have hit record profits, yet their valuations remain stubbornly low, a sign that investors doubt the sustainability of current gains. Traditional scale-driven strategies have peaked. The next growth curve will hinge on precision—targeted, data-informed approaches across technology, capital efficiency, M&A, and customer engagement. In the age of agentic AI and hyperpersonalized finance, banks that embed precision into every decision could not only close the valuation gap, but redefine what profitable growth looks like in modern banking. 💻 Launches & Products Scalable tech or  and tailored solutions (Sponsor) Why pick one? Why pick one? Build a payments solution tailored to your customers' precise needs and to match the scale of your business. Get the best of both worlds with Marqeta : a payments provider who can keep pace with your growth trajectory - and that's trusted by some of the biggest brands in the world. See what's possible Anthropic rolls out Claude AI for finance, integrates with Excel to rival Microsoft Copilot (8 minute read) Anthropic is embedding its Claude AI directly into Microsoft Excel, letting analysts analyze, build, and audit financial models without leaving their spreadsheets. The move expands Claude's presence across Microsoft's enterprise AI ecosystem and gives it direct access to real-time financial data from major providers like Moody's, LSEG, and Morningstar. Armed with new pre-built workflows for valuations, due diligence, and earnings analysis, Anthropic is positioning Claude as a precision finance assistant - a trusted, transparent tool designed to rival Copilot and OpenAI in one of AI's most lucrative battlegrounds. Klarna touts perks to take on banks (4 minute read) Klarna is expanding its monthly membership program with two new tiers, Premium ($17.99/month) and Max ($44.99/month), offering perks like airport lounge access, 1% debit card cash back, and NYT subscriptions. The move deepens Klarna's shift toward becoming a “full-scale digital bank,” following its US debit card launch and recent IPO. Analysts say the model mimics premium credit cards from Amex and JPMorgan but with a lighter monthly commitment and lower consumer burden. Worldpay backs Agentic Commerce Protocol (2 minute read) Worldpay, a global leader in payment technology, today announced its support for the Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP), an open standard for AI commerce that lets AI agents, people, and businesses work together to complete purchases. Venmo and Bilt team on rent and mortgage payments (2 minute read) Venmo today announced at Money20/20 in Las Vegas a partnership with Bilt, the nation's largest housing and neighborhood commerce platform, to expand how people use Venmo for everyday payments. 🎁 Miscellaneous Shoppers to tap BNPL for holidays (3 minute read) A survey of 1,000 US adults found that half of holiday shoppers plan to use buy now, pay later services this season, citing affordability and budget control as key reasons. Gen Z and millennials are leading adoption, with a quarter using BNPL regularly. Merchants offering BNPL see up to 91% higher average order values, though nearly half of users have faced financial hardship, underscoring both the convenience and strain of installment-based spending. Money20/20 USA: AI takes centre stage on opening day (9 minute read) AI dominated the first day of Money20/20 USA, with major players unveiling new tools that highlight how quickly artificial intelligence is transforming financial services. Wells Fargo detailed its expanded partnership with Google Cloud to deploy generative AI across all banking operations, while Starling Bank launched “Scam Intelligence,” an AI tool that detects and blocks fraud in marketplace listings. Incode introduced its Agentic Identity solution to secure AI-driven transactions, and Anthropic debuted Claude for Financial Services V2. Morgan Stanley acquires private company trading platform EquityZen (6 minute read) Morgan Stanley is acquiring EquityZen, a fintech platform that facilitates secondary share trading in private companies, expanding its reach into the growing private markets space. The move strengthens the bank's ability to offer end-to-end solutions, from cap table management to liquidity programs for both private firms and wealth management clients. ⚡️ Quick Links Western Union plots its stablecoin move (7 minute read) One of the most recognized money transfer firms, Western Union, is developing its own stablecoin, USDPT, as part of a broader push to modernize cross-border payments. Revolut eyes bank charter to have ‘seat at the table,' says US CEO (4 minute read) Revolut is pushing to secure a US bank charter to strengthen its regulatory relationships and operate more independently. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-11-03
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-11-03 Coinbase targets $2B BVNK deal 💰, Affirm expands with New York Life 🤝, Wealthsimple hits $10B 🚀 Meet with Okta and SHI about identity security, get Beats Studio Buds or an espresso machine (Sponsor) Identity threats targeting financial services are getting smarter. SHI and Okta want to show you can go beyond authentication and stop attacks with AI-powered Identity Threat Protection and Identity Security Posture Management. Take a 30-minute meeting - walk away with security insights and your choice of premium gift. Grab your meeting slot 📱 News & Trends Fintechs are edging closer to banking's big payments privilege (5 minute read) The Federal Reserve is exploring a plan that could give fintech leaders like Circle and Stripe direct access to its core payment systems through so-called “skinny” master accounts — limited versions of bank accounts that exclude perks like interest and overdraft protection. The proposal would allow qualified fintechs to move money through the Fed without relying on sponsor banks, potentially cutting costs and reducing counterparty risk. If adopted, the move could erode a major revenue stream for sponsor banks and mark a major shift toward treating fintechs more like regulated banks, bringing the US closer to Europe's more open payments model. Affirm gets boost from New York Life with $750 million loan deal (3 minute read) Affirm is expanding its partnership with New York Life Insurance, which will purchase up to $750 million in Affirm loans through 2026, providing off-balance-sheet funding to support about $1.75 billion in annual loan volume. The insurer has already invested nearly $2 billion in Affirm's collateral pools since 2023. The move reflects a broader trend of insurers such as Liberty Mutual and PGIM backing fintech lenders seeking stable, higher-yield assets amid elevated rates. Wealthsimple hits $10-billion valuation in new round backed by Dragoneer, GIC, CPP Investments (7 minute read) Wealthsimple has doubled its valuation to $10 billion following a new Series E round of up to $750 million CAD led by Dragoneer, GIC, and CPP Investments. The Toronto-based fintech now manages over $100 billion in assets and serves three million clients. The fresh capital will fuel expansion across investing, spending, and credit, as well as strategic acquisitions, giving Wealthsimple flexibility to delay an IPO while it continues its push toward a stated $1 trillion AUA goal by 2034. Coinbase targets $2 billion BVNK deal for fresh stablecoin push (3 minute read) Coinbase is in late-stage talks to acquire UK-based stablecoin infrastructure firm BVNK for about $2 billion, aiming to expand beyond trading fees and strengthen its stablecoin business. The move follows new US stablecoin regulations and growing industry adoption by players like Visa and Mastercard. BVNK's payment platform could bolster Coinbase Business, enhancing merchant payments and stablecoin-based banking. BVNK is backed by Citi Ventures, Haun Ventures, and Visa. The deal could close by early 2026. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports On the heels of fund III: fintech's next chapter (5 minute read) 2025 marked fintech's comeback year - IPOs are reopening, M&A is heating up, and growth-stage funding is flowing across every corner of the ecosystem. Giants like Xero, Stripe, and Barclays are back on the acquisition trail, while Circle, Chime, and Klarna have made successful public debuts. JC Bahr-de Stefano, one of the industry's most active fintech early-stage investors, says the launch of BTV Fund III comes at the perfect time, as AI and financial infrastructure begin to converge. He believes vertical, regulated fintech builders, not general AI players, are best positioned to seize this next wave of data-rich, compliance-heavy, high-impact opportunities. Agentic Commerce: Excitement, Caution, and Confidence (8 minute read) The next era of digital payments might not begin with instant payments, stablecoins, or a brand-new rail. It might start with something far quieter but equally transformative: software that can make payments on our behalf. As of 2025, we have already seen applications that can search, catalog, and suggest items for us to shop. Agentic Commerce takes this to the next step, where AI can search, decide, and pay within limits we define. The company posted a strong quarter, $92 billion in GMV and 32% revenue growth, underscoring how conversational commerce is quickly becoming a new growth engine for global e-commerce. The deal strengthens Ripple Custody's institutional offering, adding fast wallet provisioning, multi-chain support, and advanced MPC-based security to serve fintechs, corporates, and crypto-native clients. With Palisade's tech integrating into Ripple Payments, the company is positioning itself as a one-stop provider for institutions, from long-term asset storage to real-time global payments and treasury operations. Backed by PayPal, Mastercard, Temasek, and Peak XV Partners, the firm cut both its primary offering (by 20%) and offer-for-sale (by 44%), citing investor confidence and long-term focus. CEO Amrish Rau said Pine Labs plans to leverage India's fintech expertise to expand across markets like Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and the US. The company, now profitable, serves 980,000 merchants in 20 countries, handling over ₹11.4 trillion ($128B) in transactions. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Apple Pay Comes to India's Cross-Border Payments (10 minute read) Apple Pay has arrived in India's cross-border payments ecosystem. Apple's entry isn't about adding another feature to the market - it's about strategic positioning - a quiet yet deliberate move in one of the world's most dynamic and regulated financial landscapes. The trio explored stablecoins as “Starlink for money,” who should issue them, yield dynamics, blockchain infrastructure, and open issuance models, painting a picture of how digital dollars could reshape global banking and payments. Through the convergence of tools like natural language processing, predictive analytics, and Open Banking, the industry is moving toward hyper-personalized, transparent, and compliant financial experiences. Yet Datta emphasizes the continued need for human oversight — especially in complex cases — ensuring fairness and accountability remain at the core of AI-driven finance. 💻 Launches & Products Humanity Protocol + Mastercard: Verify income and assets without uploading documents (Sponsor) Humanity Protocol now integrates with Mastercard's open finance technology to enable financial verification through zero-knowledge proofs . Users can prove claims like "I earn over $75K" or "I own Tesla shares" without sharing pay slips or bank statements. Verification happens once, then works across lending platforms, financial apps, and Web3 / crypto. CEO Mike Lyons called the effort a “transformation agenda” to make Fiserv a higher-quality, more productive business. The company will outline costs and expected returns in early 2026. The firm acquires small, profitable software businesses—often serving overlooked sectors like youth sports, manufacturing, and unions—and rebuilds them with an AI-powered tech stack. Backed by General Catalyst, Lightspeed, and D1 Capital, Beacon calls itself an “anti–private equity firm,” emphasizing permanent ownership and reinvestment over quick exits. Profitable as a group, Beacon buys a new company roughly every two weeks and uses an internal AI “acceleration team” to automate and modernize operations. The UK has emerged as the firm's fastest-growing market, with revenue up 109% year over year and headcount in EMEA rising 35%. The payments and banking platform has relocated its EMEA HQ to Fitzrovia, London, and launched a major “Broken Banking” campaign alongside partnerships with McLaren F1 and Arsenal FC. CEO Jack Zhang said the growth reflects rising adoption of Airwallex's global accounts, payments, and embedded finance tools as businesses look to escape outdated banking systems. ⚡️ Quick Links Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong trolls the prediction markets (5 minute read) During Coinbase's Q3 earnings call, Brian Armstrong dropped words like “Bitcoin,” “Ethereum,” and “Web3” seemingly at random — just to trigger payouts on prediction markets betting on what he'd say. title: TLDR Fintech 2025-11-10
text: TLDR Fintech 2025-11-10 Ripple $40B valuation 🚀, Household debt rises 📈, Apple travel spending ✈️ 📱 News & Trends Fintech Ripple gets $40 billion valuation after $500 million funding (6 minute read) Ripple has raised $500 million at a $40 billion valuation, marking a year of major acquisitions and rapid expansion beyond payments. The company is moving deeper into crypto infrastructure with products spanning stablecoins, asset custody, prime brokerage, and corporate treasury management. Led by investors including Fortress, Citadel Securities, and Pantera, the round strengthens Ripple's position as a leading institutional player in digital finance amid renewed optimism for US crypto regulation under the GENIUS Act. The Trump White House is working on a 50-year option to break the housing market gridlock (7 minute read) The Trump administration is reportedly developing a 50-year fixed-rate mortgage to tackle the housing affordability crisis, aiming to lower monthly payments for millions of would-be buyers. FHFA Director Bill Pulte called the plan “a complete game changer,” comparing it to FDR's 30-year mortgage innovation during the New Deal. Critics argue the move could backfire. While easing payments short term, it may inflate home prices, slow equity growth, and trap borrowers in longer debt cycles. Rising household debt balances point to worsening ‘K-shaped' economic divide (6 minute read) Americans now owe a record $1.23 trillion on credit cards, with the average balance reaching $6,523, but the impact of that debt is far from equal. While wealthier households have grown stronger thanks to stock market gains and rising home values, many lower-income families are slipping further behind, struggling to pay bills and facing rising borrowing costs. Experts warn that this widening gap, fueled by high interest rates, stagnant wages, and inflation, is deepening the country's “K-shaped” economic divide, where the rich continue to build wealth as others drown in debt. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Stablecoin affinity cards could rewrite co-brand economics (6 minute read) Stablecoin-linked cards let brands reclaim control of UX and unit economics, funding rewards with stablecoin yield instead of bank rev-shares, while offering debit-like risk or collateralized credit options. This model could break the “co-brand trap,” though it adds new tech/UX/reg risks managed by emerging providers (Bridge/Rain, Fireblocks/Privy, Morpho, and trust issuers). How Current's long game built a consumer fintech growth engine (3 minute read) Current's decade-long bet on building its own banking core is fueling sustained growth and innovation in consumer fintech. The platform now serves over 6 million members and reports 70% year-over-year revenue growth, powered by seamless integration across spending, credit, and liquidity products. Features like early paycheck access, fee-free overdraft, and the Build Card for credit building have made Current a standout among digital banks. CEO Stuart Sopp says new launches, Current Max, Pay Anyone, and fee-free credit overdraft, are designed to expand value for customers living paycheck to paycheck while deepening loyalty and differentiation. 💻 Launches & Products More products, more pressure, less time? How to unblock your devs (Sponsor) You're scrambling to meet deadlines but keep getting stuck chasing edge-case failures. Sound familiar? This Temporal eBook explains how engineering teams at JP Morgan Chase and ANZ are unblocking devs without constantly rebuilding from scratch. See why velocity starts with a better foundation, and how to build one. Apple sets sights on travel spending (3 minute read) Apple is expanding its digital wallet across the travel ecosystem, aiming to make Apple Pay and Apple Wallet essential for every stage of a trip. Travelers can now use Apple Wallet to unlock cars, pass through TSA with digital IDs, board flights, and pay for transit in 800 cities, hotels, cafes, and ride-shares. The push deepens Apple's move into travel payments, where it faces stiff competition from Amex, Visa, JPMorgan, and other card issuers that dominate global travel spend. US Bank rolls out Split Card for auto-installment payments (4 minute read) The new Mastercard automatically divides every purchase into three equal monthly payments with no interest or annual fee. Users can extend payment plans to six or 12 months for a small fixed fee, combining flexibility with the convenience and protections of a traditional credit card. The product aims to attract Gen Z and budget-conscious consumers seeking predictable payments, positioning US Bank to compete more directly with BNPL players. Built Technologies launches AI agent to automate CRE lending (3 minute read) Built Technologies, a $1.5B-valued fintech focused on construction and real estate finance, has launched an AI agent to automate draw requests—the process developers use to access new tranches of loan funding. The “Draw Agent” processes approvals up to 95% faster, with 400% higher risk detection and full compliance to lender policies. Used by banks like US Bank, Citi, and Fifth Third, the agent has delivered 300–500% ROI in pilots by cutting manual review time from days to minutes, powered by Built's decade of proprietary construction loan data. Lloyds Banking to launch AI-powered financial assistant in 2026 (5 minute read) Lloyds is preparing to roll out an AI-driven conversational financial assistant in early 2026, marking the UK's first use of agentic AI in consumer banking. The assistant, already being tested by 7,000 employees, will help users manage spending, savings, and investments through natural dialogue in the Lloyds app. Over time, it's expected to handle mortgages and car finance, giving 21 million customers personalized, task-executing support that blends automation with human expertise when needed. 🎁 Miscellaneous Amazon sues Perplexity over AI shopping agents (3 minute read) Amazon filed a lawsuit against Perplexity in federal court, alleging its Comet AI agents secretly access and make purchases on Amazon without authorization. The company claims Perplexity violated computer fraud laws and misused Prime benefits. Perplexity called the suit a “threat to user choice.” CEO Andy Jassy said Amazon remains open to partnering with third-party agents, but only under terms that ensure accuracy, personalization, and customer trust. The case underscores rising tensions in the new wave of agentic commerce. Yodlee and JPMorgan Chase expand 20-year data partnership (2 minute read) JPMorgan Chase and Yodlee renewed and expanded their two-decade data access agreement, introducing new pricing terms and shared commitments to strengthen open finance collaboration. The updated deal aims to enhance financial wellness tools for JPMC's US customers and drive innovation in data-sharing standards across the industry. Yodlee CEO Farouk Ferchichi said the partnership leverages “trillions of dollars in spending, income, and wealth data” to build the next generation of open finance infrastructure. Toast mulls eat-and-run options (2 minute read) Toast is exploring a checkout-free flow that lets restaurants keep a card on file via its reservations/waitlist tools so diners can “just walk out.” Q3 highlights: $1.63B revenue (+25% YoY), $105M net income (~2x YoY), ARR > $2B (+30%), and footprint up to ~156,000 locations, with wins including TGI Friday's and Nordstrom dining. Management is targeting $10B ARR over the next decade while investing to expand against Square, Clover, and Lightspeed. ⚡️ Quick Links Affirm stock soars as Q1 earnings smash expectations, guidance lift (2 minute read) The buy-now-pay-later firm reported strong fiscal Q1 2026 results and raised its full-year outlook. Schwab to buy Forge Global for $660 million as demand for pre-IPO shares grows (5 minute read) The all-cash $660 million deal marks the brokerage giant's push into the booming pre-IPO market. Meta is hiding $30 billion in AI infrastructure debt off its balance sheet using special purpose vehicles (2 minute read) Echoing the financial engineering that preceded Enron's collapse and the 2008 crisis, Meta is reportedly hiding $30 billion in AI infrastructure debt off its balance sheet through special purpose vehicles with Blue Owl Capital.
