title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-01
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-01 State of fintech 🏆, Jevon's paradox for knowledge work 📚, agentic commerce 🤖 📱 News & Trends State of fintech 2026: The rise of fintech compounders (6 minute read) Agentic commerce, stablecoins, and prediction markets reshaped fintech in 2025 without toppling banks, which still posted record years. Scale players like Nubank, Klarna, and Revolut compounded rapidly, while AI and stablecoins quietly rewired payments, treasury, and asset management workflows heading into 2026. Trade Republic hits €12.5 billion valuation in secondary share sale (4 minute read) Berlin-based fintech Trade Republic reached a €12.5 billion valuation after a €1.2 billion secondary share sale that provided liquidity to early investors without raising new capital. Backed by top global funds, including Sequoia, Accel, and Founders Fund, the deal signals strong investor confidence in profitable, regulated European retail investing platforms. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports The $40B-to-$2B spectrum: What 2025's top 10 US funding rounds reveal about where capital flows (5 minute read) US venture funding in 2025 was increasingly skewed toward a handful of mega-rounds. Fintech stood alongside AI and data infrastructure as one of the few sectors that were still able to attract multi-billion-dollar checks. Deals like Acrisure's $2.1B raise and Polymarket's $2B investment highlighted investor appetite for scaled, cash-flow-oriented platforms that blend technology with real financial rails. The takeaway for fintech founders is clear: capital is still available, but it's flowing to companies that look less like experiments and more like durable financial institutions in the making. Jevons' paradox is coming to knowledge work (5 minute read) AI agents dramatically lower the cost of non-deterministic knowledge work, triggering Jevons' Paradox where efficiency leads to more work, not less. By giving every company access to capabilities once reserved for Fortune 500 firms, AI expands experimentation, ambition, and demand across marketing, software, research, and operations. Rather than eliminating jobs, AI shifts humans toward higher-level judgment and orchestration as total work scales 10–100×. 💻 Launches & Products Fiserv partners with Visa and Mastercard to power agentic commerce (3 minute read) Fiserv is partnering with Visa and Mastercard to give merchants access to emerging agentic commerce tools that authenticate AI shopping agents and enable secure, programmable payments. The collaborations aim to help merchants distinguish trusted AI agents from malicious bots as agents evolve from comparison tools into systems that can transact on consumers' behalf. Together, the move signals growing coordination between processors and card networks to shape standards for AI-driven commerce. Klarna and Shift4 push deeper into stablecoins as payments shift on-chain (3 minute read) Klarna and Shift4 both announced new stablecoin initiatives, highlighting growing momentum for digital currencies in mainstream payments. Klarna plans to raise short-term funding in USDC via a partnership with Coinbase, while Shift4 launched a stablecoin settlement platform enabling merchants to move funds outside traditional banking hours. The moves come as recent US regulation is expected to accelerate stablecoin adoption across the payments ecosystem. 🎁 Miscellaneous Why we're going higher in 2026: The Altitude thesis (10 minute read) Stablecoins have crossed into real payments infrastructure, flipping the old fintech moat from “licenses + bank partners” toward protocol-level execution, security, and owning more of the stack. The most promising project is Altitude: self-custodial, programmable business “banking” on Solana that plugs into fiat where needed, adds yield and agentic automations, and expands from crypto-native treasuries to global startups. Tesla's insurance operation is growing up (4 minute read) Tesla is quietly turning its insurance arm into a fully integrated, tech-first business that looks more like a software platform than a traditional carrier. The company is embedding quoting, underwriting, claims, and billing directly into its app while staffing aggressively across engineering, claims, legal, and collision partnerships to control the full insurance and repair loop. The strategy signals a long-term bet that owning insurance infrastructure, data, and claims execution can reduce costs, speed up resolution, and deepen customer lock-in across Tesla's broader ecosystem. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech and proptech startups take the spotlight at TechCrunch Disrupt Startup Battlefield (4 minute read) This post highlights 14 fintech, real estate, and proptech startups that tackle fraud detection, investing, banking infrastructure, construction, and real estate discovery with software-first approaches. Chime and the next chapter for fintech innovation (6 minute read) Chime's rise shows how a mobile-first approach, paired with a clear mission and disciplined execution, can unlock massive opportunity in consumer financial services long underserved by traditional banks. Innovations ⚙️ and trends 📈 in financial markets 🌐 and fintech 💳. Subscribe Join 310,000 readers for one daily email Privacy Careers Advertise

title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-05
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-05 Polymarket and Kalshi hit it big 🚀,  Predictions for 2026 🔮, Mercury applies for OCC bank charter 🏦 📱 News & Trends Polymarket and Kalshi hit it big on 2025 fintech funding surge (5 minute read) Venture investment into fintech rebounded in 2025 after a four-year slump, driven largely by a surge of capital flowing into prediction markets amid growing interest in regulated online wagering. Polymarket and Kalshi alone raised $3.7 billion, securing two of the largest US fintech rounds of the year and signaling a sharp concentration of funding among perceived category leaders. The trend reflects a broader shift in venture behavior, with investors favoring scaled winners like Ramp as fintech funding remains far below its 2021 peak despite improving regulatory tailwinds. How bots, banking, and stablecoins will dominate fintech in 2026 (5 minute read) Fintech leaders expect 2026 to mark a shift as more companies pursue bank charters, expand stablecoin-based payments, and deploy AI agents that can complete purchases on consumers' behalf. With a more permissive regulatory backdrop in the US, firms like Coinbase, Stripe, Mercury, Visa, and Mastercard are positioning for a future where fintechs operate closer to the core banking system while stablecoins and agentic commerce move into the mainstream. Cathie Wood's ARK fintech ETF gains 30% in 2025 by leaning into AI (4 minute read) Cathie Wood's ARK Blockchain & Fintech Innovation ETF returned about 29% in 2025 by broadening its definition of fintech and adding AI-linked stocks such as Palantir and Roku, while traditional payments and crypto names lagged. The strategy helped ARK outperform rival fintech ETFs during a down year for payments and digital assets, though investor flows into the fund remained largely flat despite the strong performance. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports QED investors shares AI and fintech outlook for 2026 (4 minute read) QED Investors sees 2026 as a turning point where AI shifts from experimentation to execution across fintech, with dynamic credit scoring, workflow automation, and disciplined growth moving to the forefront. The firm expects alternative data and continuously updated underwriting models to expand access to credit, while warning that early AI traction is often mistaken for defensible advantage as competition rapidly compresses margins. Looking ahead, QED believes valuations will normalize as AI unit economics become clearer, rewarding companies that own deep workflows, integrations, and trust rather than those relying on “AI magic” alone. Six fintech startup predictions for 2026 (6 minute read) Alex Lazarow argues that fintech's next phase will be defined less by hype and faster scaling, and more by depth, discipline, and hard trade-offs. He predicts slower global expansion but faster copycat replication, rising AI-driven capital efficiency that reshapes early-stage funding norms, and an overdue reckoning as AI churn exposes fragile revenue models and inflated valuations. Looking ahead to 2026, winners are likely to be companies that prioritize local execution, valuation restraint, M&A-led growth, and hybrid tech-plus-services models over noisy growth-at-all-costs strategies. Fintech grows up: The great race to become a bank (7 minute read) Fintech's surge in OCC charter applications marks a coming-of-age moment as companies like Mercury, Stripe, and Circle move from sponsor-bank dependency to owning the rails for better economics, control, and legitimacy. At the same time, others like Ramp, Brex, and Navan are opting out, betting that the real value sits in software and workflow layers, creating a sharp split between vertically integrated financial institutions and asset-light “software wrappers” that risk turning banks into commoditized pipes. Open standards will unlock agentic AI's next breakthrough in fintech (7 minute read) Agentic AI has begun reshaping fintech operations, but the industry's fragmentation with divergent APIs and data formats risks locking in siloed solutions unless stakeholders adopt open standards that let AI agents work across systems. The formation of the Agentic AI Foundation (AAIF) and growing protocols like the Model Context Protocol (MCP) are early steps toward interoperability that could unlock richer cross-platform workflows and more meaningful automation. With shared schemas and governance frameworks, next-generation agentic systems could orchestrate tasks across payroll, banking, payments, and forecasting, leveling the playing field for smaller fintechs and enabling innovation beyond proprietary stacks. 💻 Launches & Products CARF activates global crypto tax reporting across 48 countries (3 minute read) As of January 1, the OECD's Crypto-Asset Reporting Framework (CARF) requires crypto service providers to report user transaction data annually, bringing crypto under a coordinated global tax transparency regime. Starting in 2027, participating jurisdictions will automatically exchange this data cross-border, significantly reducing the ability to hide assets in crypto and aligning digital assets with traditional financial reporting standards. Mercury applies for OCC bank charter as fintech pushes deeper into regulated banking (3 minute read) Mercury has applied for a national bank charter with the OCC and appointed former SoFi Bank CFO Jon Auxier as CEO of the proposed bank, signaling a major step toward becoming a fully regulated financial institution. If approved, the charter would allow Mercury to hold deposits directly, operate as an FDIC-insured bank, and expand product innovation while reducing reliance on partner banks. 🎁 Miscellaneous The biggest fintech trends of 2025 (8 minute read) With the advantage of hindsight, the financial landscape of 2025 can be characterised by the enforcement of transformative payment regulations, the rise in support for stablecoins, and the moment the rubber hit the road for artificial intelligence. AI will demolish 200,000 jobs according to expert (3 minute read) Morgan Stanley estimates that the adoption of artificial intelligence could eliminate about 200,000 banking jobs across the European Union by 2030, especially in back-office, middle-office, compliance, and risk roles, highlighting AI's potential to reshape work in established sectors. While some leaders stress that AI will also create new opportunities and support work-life improvements like shorter work weeks, the looming cuts underscore broader debates over automation's dual impact on job displacement and economic productivity. Sovereign funds push into tech as assets swell to $15 Trillion (3 minute read) Global sovereign wealth funds grew assets to a record $15 trillion in 2025 as strong markets and a heavier push into technology and AI boosted returns. Funds collectively invested $66 billion into AI and digital initiatives. The US emerged as the top destination for state-backed capital, drawing $131.8 billion, underscoring how sovereign investors are becoming increasingly influential backers of American tech and innovation ecosystems. ⚡️ Quick Links America stopped making banks for 15 years, that just changed (2 minute read) For nearly 15 years, the US effectively stopped creating new banks - now fintechs are rushing back to bank charters. Crypto is dead, and that's why it finally wins (8 minute read) Blockchains, stablecoins, and on-chain rails will succeed precisely by becoming invisible infrastructure behind real products in payments, markets, and commerce. AI's trillion-dollar opportunity: Context graphs (5 minute read) AI agents won't replace systems of record but will create a new, more valuable category by capturing decision traces, the exceptions, approvals, and precedents that live outside databases today. When Payments Became Infrastructure: The Irreversible Shift of 2025 (8 minute read) By the end of 2025, what changed was not the availability of technology but the cost of absence. title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-08
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-08 Airwallex doubles down on Europe 🌏, JPMorgan takes over Apple Card 💳, How AI agents are rewiring fin serv 🤖 📱 News & Trends JPMorgan Chase takes over Apple Card from Goldman Sachs (4 minute read) JPMorgan Chase is stepping in as the new issuer of Apple Card, replacing Goldman Sachs after years of reported losses and operational friction tied to the consumer credit product. The transition hands stewardship of the high-profile card, built with Apple Card, to the largest US bank by assets, signaling confidence that scale and payments infrastructure can make the partnership work. Airwallex doubles down on Europe with €200m Netherlands investment (2 minute read) Airwallex plans to invest around €200 million in the Netherlands over five years, expanding its Amsterdam team and using the country as a hub for European growth as it shifts focus beyond Asia-Pacific. The move follows recent funding rounds that valued the company at $8 billion and comes as Airwallex prepares for a potential IPO while competing with players like Adyen, Mollie, and Bunq. US will ban large institutional investors from buying single-family homes, Trump says (2 minute read) The US plans to prohibit large institutional buyers from purchasing single-family homes as part of a broader push to improve housing affordability. Donald Trump said the policy would target Wall Street-style landlords that have expanded rapidly in residential real estate, arguing they crowd out individual buyers and first-time homeowners. If implemented, the move could reshape housing supply dynamics, potentially easing competition for entry-level homes while forcing institutional capital to shift toward other real estate assets. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports From chat to checkout: how AI agents are rewiring financial services (5 minute read) Partnerships between PayPal, OpenAI, and Stripe show how conversational AI is moving beyond support into payments and commerce, turning chat interfaces into full transaction channels and reshaping how financial services reach users at scale. Dealmakers see M&A momentum carrying into 2026 (3 minute read) A KPMG survey of 300 US corporate and private equity dealmakers shows strong optimism for 2026, with roughly two-thirds expecting higher deal volumes and nearly three-quarters anticipating better deal quality. Expansion into new markets, core business growth, and acquiring technology capabilities are the top drivers, while political uncertainty and regulatory enforcement remain the biggest risks. Money 20/20 Everything is Fintech 2026 Predictions (19 minute podcast) In this podcast, Money20/20's team outlines four themes shaping fintech's next phase, led by AI dramatically speeding up product development while largely preserving today's market winners, even as small teams gain the power to build at near-enterprise scale. They also point to Asia driving a new model of “community finance orchestrators,” pairing AI infrastructure with local, human agents, alongside tighter global regulation aimed at safer, more interoperable payments across crypto, stablecoins, and tokenization. Finally, they challenge whether ChatGPT will evolve into true Western financial super apps, with questions around consumer trust, regulatory readiness, and whether AI alone can change entrenched financial behaviors. 💻 Launches & Products Stripe partners with Crypto.com (2 minute read) Crypto.com has teamed up with Stripe to let its users spend their cryptocurrency holdings directly at online merchants without first converting to fiat currency. Fiserv's Clover embeds face and palm-based payments (2 minute read) Fiserv's Clover point-of-sale business is working with biometrics outfit Wink to integrate face and palm payments technology directly into its platform. Mastercard and LoanPro Partner to Launch ‘Loan on Card' Solution (4 minute read) A new installment lending model is set to give borrowers instant access to loan funds through a card-based credential instead of traditional bank transfers. Built on Mastercard's Installments Program and powered by LoanPro's infrastructure, the solution lets consumers and small businesses spend approved funds anywhere cards are accepted while keeping fixed repayment terms. The move highlights how lenders are modernizing disbursement and control as real-time, digital-first borrowing becomes table stakes. 🎁 Miscellaneous Bank of America's AI-driven forecasting tool saved clients 250K hours in 2025 (2 minute read) Bank of America says its AI-powered cash-flow forecasting tool helped corporate clients dramatically cut the time spent on manual financial planning tasks last year. The system analyzes transaction data and liquidity patterns to automate forecasts, freeing up finance teams to focus on strategy rather than spreadsheets. Brazil's PicPay applies for US IPO (3 minute read) Brazil-based fintech PicPay has filed a registration statement with the US Securities and Exchange Commission to pursue an initial public offering on the Nasdaq under the ticker PICS, marking its second attempt at a US listing after withdrawing a 2021 IPO plan. The company reported sharply improved financials, roughly $1.37 billion in revenue, and a net profit for the first nine months of 2025. Klarna hit with investor lawsuit over BNPL credit risk disclosures (2 minute read) Klarna faces a proposed class-action lawsuit alleging it understated consumer credit risks in its IPO filings, leading to investor losses after its stock fell post-listing. The complaint claims Klarna failed to disclose that many customers were under financial stress, though the company says the allegations lack merit and notes its loan delinquency rate remains below 1%. ⚡️ Quick Links Crypto VCs expect cautious rebound in 2026 funding as stablecoins lead and token sales stay secondary (4 minute read) Crypto investors expect a modest, disciplined recovery in 2026. PayPal boosts advertising platform with cross-merchant purchase data (2 minute read) PayPal has strengthened its advertising platform with a new programme that provides firms with a unique, cross-merchant view into real shopper behaviour, campaign effectiveness, and data-driven recommendations. Goldman turns bullish on Coinbase, but warns 2026 won't be easy (3 minute read) Goldman Sachs upgraded COIN to Buy on confidence in Coinbase's growing non-trading revenue and long-term optionality in areas like custody, prediction markets, and tokenization, while cautioning that intensifying competition, rate sensitivity, and regulatory uncertainty could keep margins under pressure in 2026. title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-12
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-12 Stablecoins go mainstream 💸, AI agents take over checkout 🤖, Wealth tax clashes with super-voting shares ⚖️ Setting meaningful KPIs: advice from a business coach with downloadable templates (Sponsor) Acclaimed business coach Bernie Smith shares his comprehensive guide to developing meaningful, reliable KPIs, including 50 checklists and downloadable templates. Using brief explanations and practical checklists, this eBook provides the tools to deliver meaningful measures that work, create reports that support decision-making, and deploy the necessary tools to engage your organization. Download your free copy today to unlock: Customizable resources like templates, checklists, spreadsheets, and sample SLAs. Process maps showing the production flow for each measure or KPI. User guides that include the tools and practical advice your team needs to succeed. Get the eBook (free from NetSuite) 📱 News & Trends Wealth tax meets super-voting shares: Tech founders warn of unintended fallout (3 minute read) A proposed California billionaire wealth tax is drawing backlash from tech leaders who argue it would tax founders based on voting control rather than economic ownership, potentially inflating taxable wealth far beyond actual holdings. Critics warn the language could hit founders with super-voting shares especially hard, incentivize capital flight, and create severe consequences for illiquid startup founders if enacted as written. Rain's valuation jumps to $1.95bn as stablecoin funding accelerates (2 minute read) Stablecoin infrastructure firm Rain raised $250 million in a Series C led by ICONIQ that values the company at $1.95 billion, as regulatory tailwinds and institutional interest fuel crypto adoption. The company plans to use the capital to expand into licensed markets and scale its stablecoin-linked cards and payments platform, which already supports transactions wherever Visa is accepted. Google announces a new protocol to facilitate commerce using AI agents (5 minute read) Google has introduced an open standard designed to let AI-driven shopping assistants work seamlessly across product discovery, checkout, and post-purchase support without custom integrations for each retailer. Built alongside partners like Shopify, Walmart, and Target, the framework will soon power direct purchases inside Google Search and Gemini, using Google Pay and stored wallet details. The move signals a broader push to make conversational commerce native to search, while giving merchants more control over how their products and promotions surface in AI-driven buying moments. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Tokenized stocks could redefine global equity markets (23 minute video) Tokenized stocks have the potential to massively expand global access to equities by enabling fractional ownership, 24/7 trading, real-time settlement, and new instruments like perpetual futures. Tokenization is the logical next step after stablecoins, unlocking faster, more flexible capital markets and new governance models. Why capping credit card APRs could quietly kill rewards and shrink access (4 minute read) A proposed 10% cap on credit card APRs may sound consumer-friendly, but fintech veterans warn it would undermine the cross-subsidies that fund rewards, lounges, and broad credit access. The likely outcome is fewer perks, higher fees for premium cards, and the disappearance of subprime credit, pushing riskier borrowers toward less regulated and more expensive alternatives. Global fintech funding surges 21% in 2025, according to new Innovate Finance report (2 minute read) Global fintech investment rebounded in 2025, with startups raising $53 billion across nearly 6,000 deals, signaling a clear recovery in investor confidence after several down years, according to Innovate Finance. Capital flowed heavily into payments and crypto, led by mega-rounds for Binance, Kraken, and Ripple, while secondary transactions, most notably at Revolut, pushed valuations higher without new primary funding. The US remained the dominant market by a wide margin, but faster growth outside Europe highlights intensifying global competition for fintech capital and talent. 💻 Launches & Products Stripe opens the door to crypto-funded checkout with Crypto.com partnership (3 minute read) Stripe will enable consumers to pay merchants using cryptocurrency balances through a new partnership with Crypto.com, signaling growing interest among payment processors in crypto-funded checkout. While stablecoins are seen as the most likely driver of adoption, industry experts caution that widespread use will depend on actual consumer demand and comfort with paying directly in digital assets. Microsoft pushes retail into the agentic era with AI-powered commerce and operations (4 minute read) Microsoft unveiled a suite of agentic AI tools that embed intelligent automation across the entire retail value chain, from merchandising and checkout to store operations and fulfillment. The launch includes Copilot Checkout for in-chat purchasing, brand and shopping agents for personalized discovery, and operational agents that help retailers automate decisions, improve efficiency, and convert intent into action without breaking the customer experience. Pluto launches AI-powered lending platform to unlock liquidity in private markets (4 minute read) Pluto Financial Technologies has raised $8.6 million in seed funding to build an AI-powered credit infrastructure that allows financial advisors and high-net-worth investors to borrow against private equity, venture, and alternative assets without selling them. Its flagship Wealth Equity Line of Credit (WELOC) lets investors access liquidity at competitive rates with repayment tied to future fund distributions rather than monthly interest, keeping capital deployed while unlocking cash. Through distribution partners Allocate and Moonfare, Pluto already reaches thousands of investors managing over $6 billion in alternative assets, positioning the platform as a new liquidity layer for private markets. 🎁 Miscellaneous JPMorgan ditches proxy advisers in favor of AI-driven shareholder voting (2 minute read) JPMorgan Asset Management has replaced third-party proxy advisers like ISS and Glass Lewis with an internal AI system, Proxy IQ, to analyze data and vote shares across more than 3,000 US company meetings. The move reflects long-standing criticism from CEO Jamie Dimon and aligns with broader political and regulatory efforts to curb the influence of proxy advisers, particularly on ESG-related issues. Saudis open stocks to all foreign investors to boost inflows (3 minute read) Saudi Arabia has removed long-standing eligibility rules that limited who could buy local shares, opening its main equity market to all foreign investors starting February 1. The move, announced by the Saudi Capital Market Authority, scraps the “qualified foreign investor” framework and is part of a broader push to revive a market that underperformed other emerging peers last year. Analysts say the change could unlock billions in new passive inflows from global index trackers like MSCI and FTSE, while supporting a growing pipeline of IPOs as the kingdom seeks fresh capital amid rising fiscal pressure. ⚡️ Quick Links Markets signal a brighter UK future after lost decade (5 minute read) UK bank stocks have outperformed the Magnificent Seven over the past two years, signaling investor confidence in higher margins, cheaper valuations, and a UK recovery narrative that markets appear to believe ahead of economists. Fireblocks buys TRES for $130M as institutions demand clean crypto books (3 minute read) Fireblocks just spent $130 million to buy TRES, a company that handles crypto taxes and record-keeping for large banks and trading firms. Fintech wins 2025's most lucrative league table (5 minute read) A booming 2025 market generated enormous paper wealth, but fintech and crypto founders far outpaced bank CEOs, with Robinhood's Vlad Tenev up roughly $3.3 billion and Circle's Jeremy Allaire adding close to $900 million as shares surged. title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-15
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-15 Affirm updates underwriting 💸, Klarna moves into P2P 👥, Banks getting unbundled 🏦 Experts Weigh-in: 2026 AI Trends: Is Your Services Business Ready? (Sponsor) Is your data fueling AI success or are silos holding you back? As professional services shift toward a hybrid human-agent workforce, the stakes are high: 95% of GenAI pilots fail because they aren't embedded in real-world workflows, and 88% of leaders say fragmented processes are their biggest hurdle. Breaking these silos requires more than new tech—it demands a cohesive framework where AI effectively augments operations. Scaling from simple automation to 90% market capture starts with a unified services solution. Join experts from Diabsolut and Certinia on January 29 to explore the core competencies of Agentic AI. Are you ready for the AI economy? Register for the webinar today! 📱 News & Trends Affirm updates underwriting with enhanced signals (2 minute read) Building on its longstanding approach of underwriting every transaction, Affirm's latest underwriting now incorporates richer real-time signals like account balances and cash flow trends. These insights give Affirm an even clearer, more current view of someone's financial standing at checkout, leading to more informed and responsible credit decisions. Trump calls for a one-year 10% cap on credit card interest in a Truth Social post (4 minute read) Donald Trump said he wants to temporarily limit how much banks can charge on revolving balances, positioning the move as consumer relief amid frustration over high borrowing costs. The proposal, announced on Truth Social, would require congressional approval and has already drawn pushback from banking trade groups that argue it would shrink access to credit and push borrowers toward costlier alternatives. The call comes as the administration has simultaneously reduced funding for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, adding uncertainty around whether the idea becomes policy or remains political signaling. Clover and Toast lead a crowded fight for small restaurant payments (2 minute read) Fiserv's Clover holds the largest share of the small restaurant POS market with about 20% of locations, narrowly ahead of Toast at 17%, with Square and Global Payments close behind. The battle is concentrated in smaller eateries, which make up roughly 75% of the $1.1 trillion US restaurant market and offer higher processing margins than large chains. Toast is expected to continue gaining share through focused restaurant-only software and payments. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Why Fintechs are Racing for Bank Charters again (10 minute read) Bank charter applications in the US reached their highest level in five years. Most applications come from the fintech sector, but the underlying segments vary widely. Companies range from neobanks like Nubank, to payments players like Checkout.com, stablecoin issuers like Circle, and crypto exchange Coinbase. This article digs into the strategic drivers behind this trend, how charters add value for fintechs, and why banks are not all created equally. Banks aren't disrupted — they're getting unbundled (12 minute read) Banks are still printing money, but the “universal bank” is being carved up by shadow banks (private credit + money markets/stablecoins) taking risk and yield and fintech UX layers that either get charters, stay OTT software, or bypass rails with stablecoins. The week's proof points: Trump floats a 10% credit card APR cap, JPM takes over Apple Card as Goldman exits, and Kontingo gets hit by wallet hacks right after getting deplatformed. 💻 Launches & Products FIS brings agentic commerce to banks with Visa and Mastercard (3 minute read) FIS launched an industry-first platform that enables banks to securely support AI-initiated payments within existing card network frameworks, in partnership with Visa and Mastercard. The offering, expected by Q1 2026, positions banks to authorize agent-driven transactions, enhance fraud protection, and stay central as agentic commerce scales. Payments giants look beyond stablecoins to identity and embedded rails in 2026 (2 minute read) Visa and Mastercard expect digital identity and agent authentication to become critical as AI-powered, agentic commerce drives new fraud risks, while ACI sees faster adoption of real-time payments and new digital asset rails. Wex predicts embedded payments will surge in B2B, citing forecasts of $7 trillion in embedded payment volume in 2026 as payments become increasingly invisible and integrated into workflows. 🎁 Miscellaneous Ripple turns down IPO after $500M raise — here's what they're planning instead (5 minute read) Ripple is opting to stay private after raising $500 million at a $40 billion valuation, with President Monica Long saying the company prefers flexibility over the scrutiny and short-term pressures of public markets. Ripple will instead use its strong balance sheet to build a full-stack enterprise crypto platform, completing nearly $4 billion in acquisitions across prime brokerage, custody, treasury management, and stablecoin payments, with RLUSD positioned at the center of the strategy. While the company's valuation has surged, XRP's price has lagged, underscoring a growing disconnect between private company fundamentals and token market sentiment as CEO Brad Garlinghouse signals more deals ahead in 2026. Moneyhub selected by Nationwide to create AI-driven spending insights for all customers (2 minute read) Moneyhub will deploy its AI-powered categorisation and enrichment engine across Nationwide's 16 million customers, analyzing everything from card payments to direct debits. The technology adds richer context to transactions, including merchant identification, location data, and clearer payment details, helping customers manage spending and flag potential fraud more quickly. Over time, the deeper data layer is expected to enable more personalised products and services across Nationwide's digital banking experience. Fed's Powell resists DOJ probe (5 minute read) Jerome Powell says a Justice Department investigation into his congressional testimony and cost overruns tied to a headquarters renovation is being used as a pretext to undermine the independence of the Federal Reserve. He argues the probe follows months of pressure from Donald Trump over interest rate decisions, despite the Fed cutting rates at its last three meetings. The standoff raises broader concerns about whether monetary policy can remain insulated from political influence as Powell's term nears its end. Polygon Labs buys two crypto startups for $250 million as it looks to compete with Stripe (5 minute read) Polygon Labs is expanding beyond pure blockchain infrastructure by acquiring US-based crypto startups Coinme and Sequence in deals totaling over $250 million, aiming to build out regulated stablecoin payments and money-movement services. The deal adds Coinme's licensed fiat on- and off-ramps and Sequence's wallet and cross-chain infrastructure to Polygon's Open Money Stack, positioning the company to challenge established payments players like Stripe while tapping growing demand for stablecoin-based settlement. title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-19
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-19 Stripe acquires Metronome 🤝, AI can do your whole job now 🤖, risk in fintech ⚠️ 📱 News & Trends Affirm moves BNPL into rent payments through Esusu partnership (5 minute read) Affirm plans to offer buy now, pay later installment loans for rent through a partnership with Esusu, making it one of the largest BNPL providers to enter recurring housing payments. The pilot will allow eligible renters to split rent into two biweekly, zero-interest payments, drawing praise for flexibility but concern from consumer advocates about the risk of debt cycles tied to essential expenses. Stripe completes Metronome acquisition (2 minute read) Stripe has completed its acquisition of Metronome to strengthen its billing and metering capabilities for complex usage-based pricing models, especially those used by fast-growing AI companies. Stripe CEO Patrick Collison framed usage-based billing as a core interface between product and business, while Metronome's team emphasized building flexible, data-driven monetization infrastructure that scales from startups to global enterprises. Betterment confirms data breach after hackers send fake crypto scam notification to users (4 minute read) Betterment disclosed a security incident after attackers used compromised internal systems to send fraudulent crypto-related messages to customers, prompting confusion and concern among users. The company said the breach did not expose customer funds or core investment accounts, but did involve limited personal data and misuse of its customer communications infrastructure. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports The thermodynamics of risk in fintech (6 minute read) Fintech doesn't eliminate risk by lowering costs and friction — it redistributes it, shifting exposure into forms the business must be explicitly built to manage. Winners like Square, Toast, and BNPL providers succeed by intentionally transforming risk (e.g., underwriting into fraud or concentration risk) and developing the data, systems, and discipline to handle it, while failures come from ignoring where that risk ultimately lands. Brex's AI-fueled turnaround bets the company on agents, not headcount (25 minute read) Brex reversed stalled growth by flattening its org, holding engineering headcount flat, and pushing aggressive AI adoption across the company, helping it surpass $500M in annualized revenue. It built a multi-agent internal platform to automate ops like KYC, underwriting, and finance workflows while reshaping hiring, culture, and execution around AI fluency rather than scale. AI just grew arms, and it can do your whole job now (10 minute read) AI has crossed from “helpful assistant” to autonomous coworker. Tools like Claude Code and Claude Cowork can now execute full tasks end-to-end rather than just generate text. As execution becomes cheap and fast, the bottleneck for companies shifts from headcount to judgment, framing, and the ability to orchestrate agents around high-quality ideas. Are we done with digital transformation in banking? (32 minute podcast) Just as mobile was once a separate vertical within banking, has digital transformation simply become part of everything we do? In this podcast, hosts debate this and more as they discuss the current state of banking, the impact of fintech, and the evolution of digital banking. They cover the challenges posed by legacy systems, the rise of digital-only banks, and the role of AI and stablecoins in shaping the future of finance. 💻 Launches & Products The CFO Agenda: 12 Ways CFOs Can Flourish in 2026 (Sponsor) If the first weeks are any indication, 2026 will be unpredictable. Oracle NetSuite has compiled 12 CFO action items to add to your agenda so you can control costs and drive operational excellence — no matter what the year throws at you. Download the annual CFO Agenda to ground operations in real business data Visa Direct Adds Stablecoin Payouts via BVNK (5 minute read) Visa and BVNK announced a partnership where BVNK powers stablecoin services inside Visa Direct, Visa's $1.7T money movement network. The integration supports stablecoin prefunding for fiat payouts and payouts to stablecoin wallets, using existing Visa Direct APIs but passing wallet details instead of bank account details Hawk launches AI 'Analytics Studio' for banks and payment firms (2 minute read) Hawk, a leading provider of AI-powered anti-money laundering, screening, and fraud prevention solutions, today announced the launch of Analytics Studio, an AI lifecycle management solution that gives banks and payment companies more control over the creation, maintenance, and governance of their AI models. Stripe partners Crypto.com to offer merchants crypto payment option (2 minute read) Stripe is expanding its checkout capabilities by enabling businesses to process digital asset transactions with automatic settlement in traditional currencies. The setup shields merchants from price volatility and compliance complexity while tapping into crypto-native consumer demand. The partnership reflects a more utility-driven approach to crypto, positioning it as infrastructure for payments rather than a speculative asset class. Bloomberg adds prediction markets data to the Terminal via WSL PREDICT (1 minute read) Bloomberg has added prediction market data from Polymarket and Kalshi to its Terminal, giving users real-time insight into crowd expectations on topics ranging from Fed leadership to geopolitical outcomes. The new WSL PREDICT feature lets users load expert-curated security worksheets by asset class or region and then customize and save them inside the Security Worksheet function. Xero introduces enterprise-grade analytics (3 minute read) Xero is rolling out a new analytics layer aimed at giving larger businesses deeper visibility into financial performance without leaving the platform. The upgrade adds advanced reporting, customizable dashboards, and more flexible data analysis tools designed to support complex organizations and advisory workflows. The move signals Xero's push upmarket, positioning its cloud accounting software as a more serious contender for mid-sized and enterprise customers who expect richer, decision-ready insights. 🎁 Miscellaneous Enterprise CFO confidence rebounds as AI moves to the center of finance agendas (4 minute read) A new Deloitte survey shows confidence among CFOs at $1B+ companies rebounded sharply at the end of 2025, with technology and automation now topping finance priorities for 2026. AI adoption is accelerating fast, with most CFOs planning to embed AI agents directly into finance workflows, while rising interest in M&A reflects improved deal conditions and stronger balance sheets. Education Department pauses wage seizures for unpaid student loans (4 minute read) The US Department of Education has temporarily halted wage garnishment and other aggressive collection actions on defaulted federal student loans. The pause is designed to give borrowers breathing room as the agency works to expand access to repayment options and streamline pathways back into good standing. The move underscores the administration's broader effort to soften the financial shock of student debt enforcement after pandemic-era protections ended, while signaling that long-term repayment reform remains unresolved. Mastercard, Visa, and Revolut lose UK legal challenge over card fee cap plans (2 minute read) Mastercard, Visa, and Revolut have lost a legal challenge against the Payment Systems Regulator over the UK watchdog's decision to cap interchange fees on cross-border online payments. Electric rate shock triggers white house crackdown on data centers (4 minute read) Surging electricity costs tied to the rapid buildout of AI-focused data centers are pushing the White House to consider new rules aimed at protecting consumers and the power grid. Policymakers are weighing measures that could shift infrastructure costs away from households and toward hyperscalers and AI operators driving demand. The outcome could reshape how data centers are priced, permitted, and regulated, with meaningful implications for AI economics, cloud providers, and energy-intensive tech growth in the US. ⚡️ Quick Links Polymarket faces scrutiny for hosting prediction markets on war and conflict (4 minute read) Polymarket is attracting heightened attention from policymakers and ethicists over whether trading on real-time geopolitical violence crosses a line between information signaling and profiting from human harm. Portage takes over Point72 Ventures' fintech portfolio in $280M deal (1 minute read) Global fintech investor Portage will manage a $280 million continuation vehicle housing select fintech and crypto assets from Point72 Ventures, centralizing control of the portfolio under a dedicated fintech specialist. Fintech tycoon's startup platform merges with Milan tech hubs (2 minute read) Italian fintech billionaire Andrea Pignataro, who built his fortune through trading and market-infrastructure software firm ION Group, is merging his startup platform with several Milan tech hubs to consolidate early-stage innovation and strengthen the city's startup ecosystem. text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-22 Payoneer buys Boundless 📝, AI and jobs 🤖, model-market fit 🤝 50+ Risk Signals of Fraud Every Fintech Leader Should Know (Sponsor) Leading fintech fraud teams don't just rely on surface checks to spot suspicious activity. This Persona guide covers 50 KYC risk signals (and 24 additional KYB signals) the best teams use to spot suspicious activity. ⬇️ Download this guide to learn: How to blend active signals, passive signals, and behavioral signals to expand the types of fraud you're able to catch Which types of signals tend to correlate with which types of fraud The signals you're not tracking, but should be Get your fraud-fighting strategy up to speed with modern fraud tactics. Get the guide 📱 News & Trends Payoneer buys employer of record platform Boundless (2 minute read) Payoneer, a global financial technology company that powers business growth across borders, today announced it has acquired Boundless, an Ireland-based Employer of Record (EOR) platform that helps companies seamlessly and compliantly employ people worldwide. Trump calls on Congress to cap credit card rates (2 minute read) President Donald Trump says he will ask Congress to implement a one-year 10% cap on credit card interest rates in a move that JPMorgan chief Jamie Dimon says would spell “economic disaster” for the US. Sequoia targets major Anthropic investment (4 minute read) Sequoia Capital is preparing its first major investment in Anthropic as part of a massive funding round that could raise $25bn or more and value the AI company at roughly $350bn, more than double its valuation from four months ago. The move marks a strategic shift for Sequoia after a recent leadership overhaul and reflects how top investors now view leading AI labs less like traditional VC bets and more like long-term public-market assets. Anthropic is also laying the groundwork for a potential IPO as soon as this year. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Model-market fit: the missing prerequisite AI founders ignore (15 minute read) Before product-market fit can exist in AI, the underlying model must be capable of reliably performing the tasks customers actually need done, a threshold known as model-market fit (MMF). Markets only “explode” once model capability crosses a hard accuracy and autonomy threshold. Many AI startups fail not on distribution or UX, but because the model simply isn't ready yet. AI and jobs: The decline started before ChatGPT (10 minute read) A new analysis challenges the claim that ChatGPT caused a sharp drop in entry-level jobs, showing that hiring in AI-exposed roles began declining months before ChatGPT launched. The timing lines up far more closely with aggressive interest rate hikes starting in early 2022, and similar patterns appeared during COVID, suggesting macroeconomic forces, not AI automation, are the primary driver so far. 💻 Launches & Products Acquired.com integrates with Visa A2A for recurring and variable payments (2 minute read) Acquired.com, a payments infrastructure provider for recurring commerce, today announced a partnership with Visa in the UK to bring to market Visa A2A, a new account-to-account payment solution designed specifically for recurring and variable payments. NYSE bets on a parallel onchain exchange for tokenized equities (4 minute read) NYSE announced it is building an entirely new trading venue for natively issued tokenized securities, featuring 24/7 trading, instant onchain settlement, and stablecoin-based funding. Rather than retrofitting legacy infrastructure, NYSE plans to operate this digital-native market alongside its traditional exchange, signaling a strategy to run both the current financial system and its potential successor in parallel. PayPal introduces free DIY tax filing (2 minute read) PayPal today announced a new partnership with april, a leading embedded tax technology platform, allowing US PayPal Debit Mastercard customers to file their 2025 federal and state tax returns for free using april's DIY tax filing service, saving approximately $160 in typical costs. 🎁 Miscellaneous Bermuda plans a fully on-chain national economy with Coinbase and Circle (3 minute read) Bermuda announced plans to move its economy on-chain with support from Coinbase and Circle, starting with government stablecoin pilots and tokenization efforts across banks and insurers. The initiative aims to lower transaction costs and expand access to global finance. It positions Bermuda as the world's first fully on-chain national economy through public-private collaboration. Stripe's Collison Says No Rush for Payment Firm to Go Public (3 minute read) Stripe CEO Patrick Collison says the payments giant isn't feeling pressure to go public, despite renewed IPO chatter across tech and fintech. He pointed to strong revenue growth, expanding products across payments, treasury, and embedded finance, and the flexibility that comes with staying private. Stripe is optimizing for long-term platform scale rather than timing the public markets, even as peers inch closer to listings. Founder effect: Airwallex CEO Jack Zhang's journey from blue-collar work to an $8B fintech (6 minute read) Jack Zhang, the co-founder and CEO of Airwallex, worked up to 100 hours a week across four blue-collar jobs as a teenager to put himself through university after moving alone from China to Australia. That survival-driven work ethic later shaped Airwallex, which he founded in 2015 to reinvent cross-border payments and is now valued at $8 billion with more than $1 billion in annualized revenue. ⚡️ Quick Links Winston Weinberg on building Harvey and the future of professional services AI (70 minute video) Harvey CEO and co-founder Winston Weinberg breaks down why enterprise AI adoption is misunderstood, how AI reshapes law and professional services, and hard-earned lessons on hiring, deal-making, and scaling trust-heavy products globally. The problem with prediction markets (6 minute read) Prediction markets remain structurally constrained by thin liquidity, adverse selection, and poor capital efficiency, and will only scale into institutional-grade infrastructure if they evolve toward standardized, margin-enabled event contracts that balance blockchain efficiency with traditional counterparty trust. title: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-26
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-26 Capital One buys Brex 💳, Wall Street tests tokenized Treasurys 🧱, Databricks taps private credit 💰 EBITDA Doesn't Pay the Bills (Sponsor) Great EBITDA doesn't matter if you can't keep the lights on. To do that, you need something simple: cash flow. In this NetSuite guide , you'll learn why EBITDA is overrated - and operating cashflow is king. Topics include: >> Why cash flow is the key to business health >> Common misconceptions that make people focus on EBITDA >> Practical steps you can take to improve cash visibility and discipline Get the guide 📱 News & Trends Affirm just filed to become a bank. Are all fintechs becoming banks? (2 minute read) Affirm's push to become a regulated bank is fundamentally about shaving hundreds of basis points off its cost of capital by funding loans with deposits instead of wholesale or partner-bank funding. Even a 200–400 bps improvement can materially expand margins at scale, which helps explain why incumbents like Ford and General Motors already operate approved banking arms, while newer players like Nissan and Mercury have more recently filed or received approvals. The broader signal is clear: once fintechs reach sufficient volume, owning the balance sheet becomes less about innovation optics and more about structural economics. Capital One to buy fintech Brex for $5.15 billion (5 minute read) Capital One Financial has agreed to acquire the fintech Brex in a roughly $5.15 billion cash-and-stock deal, giving the bank access to Brex's corporate card tech and expense management platform used by thousands of companies. Brex, founded in 2017 and known for blending business credit cards with financial software, will continue under CEO Pedro Franceschi after the transaction closes, expected in mid-2026. Databricks lines up $1.8 billion debt raise as private credit appetite grows (2 minute read) Databricks secured $1.8 billion in new financing from syndicated loans and private credit investors, lifting its total debt to about $7.05 billion as it continues to fund growth and employee liquidity following its $134 billion valuation round. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Will AI Kill Fintech SaaS? (15 minute read) Agents commoditize code. They don't commoditize network effects, data moats, or the 1,000 idiosyncratic edge cases that only matter when you're operating at scale. The last 15 years of Fintech infrastructure were built on companies that made building a bank-like app 10x faster. The next 15 years will still need them—but they'll have to ship 10x faster too. Lessons from building AI agents for financial services (12 minute read) A practitioner's guide to building AI agents for financial services. Risk, correctness, and trust, not model intelligence, are the real constraints. The guide explains why sandboxes, filesystem-first design, domain-specific “skills,” rigorous evaluations, and production monitoring are essential to making AI agents reliable enough for high-stakes financial decisions. New foundation aims to bridge the gap between banks and blockchains (4 minute read) A new global initiative backed by the World Economic Forum is launching to formalize collaboration between traditional financial institutions and blockchain-native players. The foundation builds on themes emphasized at recent WEF meetings in Davos, where regulators, banks, and crypto leaders increasingly framed digital assets as infrastructure rather than a parallel system. The move reflects a broader shift toward pragmatic integration, with interoperability, governance, and regulatory clarity emerging as shared priorities for global finance. BlackRock CEO Larry Fink wants the entire financial system on one common blockchain (6 minute read) BlackRock CEO Larry Fink is pushing a vision where global markets run on a single shared blockchain infrastructure rather than fragmented financial rails. He argues that tokenizing assets like stocks, bonds, and funds on-chain could dramatically reduce settlement times, costs, and operational risk across the system. If realized, this shift would position firms like BlackRock at the center of a re-architected financial stack where blockchain becomes core infrastructure, not a niche experiment. 💻 Launches & Products Manage your cap table like it's 2026 (Sponsor) Running your cap table in spreadsheets is risky. Carta is the connected platform that issues shares directly, stays compliant by design, and streamlines stakeholder communication - so you spend less time chasing consent forms and more time building your startup. Try it Claude comes to Excel with model-aware AI assistance (2 minute read) Anthropic has launched Claude in Excel, letting users query, debug, and modify complex spreadsheets with cell-level citations while preserving formulas, and enabling faster scenario analysis, error tracing, and financial modeling inside Excel. Ramp launches live budget tracking solution (3 minute read) Ramp Budgets is a real-time budget tracking tool that uses AI to tie a company's financial plan directly to live spend, giving finance teams up-to-the-minute insight without spreadsheets or manual reconciliation. It automatically maps all transactions to budget lines, adds contextual approvals and dashboards, and extends visibility and control across teams, with future AI-driven budget creation on the roadmap. Its launch marks a push toward smarter spend management where businesses can act on budget data as dollars are spent, not weeks later. Affirm expands buy now, pay later into rent payments (4 minute read) Affirm is partnering with property management platforms to let renters split monthly rent into installments using its pay-over-time product. The rollout targets large landlords and professionally managed properties, positioning BNPL as a budgeting tool rather than short-term credit for discretionary purchases. If adoption scales, the move could push BNPL deeper into essential living expenses, raising both new revenue opportunities and fresh regulatory scrutiny around consumer debt. 🎁 Miscellaneous Australia orders compliance audit of $8bn Airwallex platform (3 minute read) Australia's financial intelligence agency, AUSTRAC, has ordered Airwallex to commission an external audit of its AML and counter-terrorism financing controls, citing concerns that transaction monitoring and customer due diligence have not kept pace with the fintech's rapid global expansion. Trump sues JPMorgan and Jamie Dimon over alleged “debanking” (4 minute read) Trump has filed a $5 billion lawsuit accusing JPMorgan Chase and CEO Jamie Dimon of improperly cutting off banking services to him and affiliated businesses following the January 6 Capitol riot. The suit claims the account closures were politically motivated, caused financial and reputational harm, and effectively forced Trump's businesses into less favorable banking relationships. JPMorgan denies the allegations, saying account decisions are driven by legal and regulatory risk, not political views, setting up a high-profile test of the growing “debanking” debate in US financial services. A $6 billion treasury ETF becomes test bed for tokenized finance (6 minute read) Wall Street's traditional ETF market is being nudged toward blockchain. F/m Investments has filed with the US Securities and Exchange Commission to record ownership of its $6.3 billion US Treasury 3-Month Bill ETF (TBIL) on a permissioned blockchain, making it one of the first regulated funds to explore tokenized shares. If approved, tokenized and conventional TBIL shares would retain the same legal structure, rights, and fees, but be tradable and transferable via blockchain platforms. This initiative could signal a broader shift toward integrating blockchain record-keeping into core financial infrastructure, potentially reducing settlement friction and serving as a blueprint for future regulated tokenization efforts. ⚡️ Quick Links PayPal acquires Cymbio to deepen agentic commerce push (2 minute read) PayPal has acquired Israeli AI firm Cymbio to strengthen its agentic commerce capabilities, bringing multi-channel orchestration, merchant catalog sync, and fulfillment tooling in-house. Ledger seeks US IPO at $4 billion valuation  (2 minute read) Ledger, a maker of popular cryptocurrency hardware wallets, is preparing a US initial public offering that could value the company at more than $4 billion. US fintech seen benefiting from Trump's affordability push (2 minute read) Citi says a populist shift toward affordability could favor fintech challengers like Affirm, SoFi, Block, Toast, and Shopify over traditional banks. Crypto firm BitGo raises $212.8 million in US IPO (2 minute read) BitGo Holdings has priced its US initial public offering at $18 per share. text: TLDR Fintech 2026-01-29 Decagon hits $4.5B 🤖, banks–crypto forced showdown 🏛️, Brex & Capital One 💳 📱 News & Trends White House summons Coinbase and banks to hash out crypto bill (3 minute read) The White House is convening crypto and banking leaders as negotiations stall over a digital-asset market structure bill, after Coinbase pulled support for a key Senate draft. The dispute centers on whether crypto exchanges can offer token-based rewards, with banks warning of deposit flight and crypto firms arguing for consumer choice as midterm pressure mounts. AI customer support startup Decagon hits $4.5B valuation (3 minute read) Decagon raised $250 million in a Coatue and Index-led round that tripled its valuation, as enterprises race to deploy AI agents in customer support. Founded in 2023, the company now serves 100+ large customers and is betting that proactive, continuously learning AI agents can transform a market where businesses spend hundreds of billions annually but still deliver poor customer experiences. Brex CFO Erica Dorfman on the logic behind the Capital One deal (6 minute read) Brex CFO Erica Dorfman details how the $5.15B Capital One acquisition came together in just four weeks, reframing Brex's future around scale, distribution, and access to bank-level capital. She explains why the board prioritized public-market multiples and full-company liquidity over peak private valuations, and how operating within Capital One accelerates Brex's long-term ambitions without disrupting near-term execution. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports I Built a Causal AI Model to Find What Actually Causes Stock Drawdowns (9 minute read) The author builds a causal inference framework on ~10 years of S&P 500 data to test whether traits like high valuation (P/E), high beta, and low profitability cause deeper drawdowns during stress windows (COVID 2020, rate shock 2022), rather than merely correlating with them. Using propensity-score weighting and a doubly robust estimator, they found all three amplify downside, especially low margins, then validate results with overlap and covariate-balance checks to argue the effects are structural, not just statistical storytelling. Anthropic is winning by trying to lose (8 minute read) Anthropic's safety-first philosophy, treating AI like hazardous infrastructure rather than a consumer toy, has quietly produced the most trusted enterprise AI platform, driving massive revenue growth, profitability, and Fortune 500 adoption while competitors chase hype, benchmarks, and ad-driven engagement. 💻 Launches & Products The $5 million Bots bill (Sponsor) Most traffic on the internet is driven by bots, and it's crushing companies' bottom lines. Unwanted bot traffic can bypass firewalls, hit origin servers, and trigger six-figure overcharges from ISPs. Hydrolix accurately classifies human and bot traffic in real time, identifying good bots, AI scrapers, credential-stuffing bots, impersonators, and emerging agentic threats - then mitigates them instantly. See how it works. Mine raises $14M to launch AI money agent for young adults (4 minute read) Mine (formerly Fizz) raised a $14M Series A led by 359 Capital, with participation from Kleiner Perkins, FJ Labs, YC, and US News & World Report, to launch MoneyGPT, an AI-powered personal finance agent tailored to young adults' variable income, debt, and credit-building needs. The company is rebranding around a broader platform vision, combining AI-driven guidance, credit tools, and unified account tracking to reduce financial stress and improve confidence for a generation underserved by traditional finance products. Zelle network expands by 15% (3 minute read) Zelle added 337 mostly small community banks and credit unions in 2025, growing its network to roughly 2,537 financial institutions and extending reach into rural and underserved communities. Partnerships with Velera and Alacriti helped smaller banks integrate Zelle despite uneven tech stacks, though community banks remain a minority of the 8,700+ US financial institutions and face concerns over Zelle's pricing. Revolut launches full banking operations in Mexico (4 minute read) Revolut has officially launched its first bank outside Europe, rolling out full digital banking services in Mexico after its beta phase, backed by over $100M in capital, strong initial credit ratings, and a broad product suite spanning savings, payments, FX, and family accounts as it accelerates global expansion. 🎁 Miscellaneous Y Combinator drops Canada from countries it will invest in (2 minute read) Y Combinator has quietly removed Canada from the list of jurisdictions it invests in, reinforcing its push for startups to be based in the US. The change has raised concerns among Canadian investors and founders, who see it as another signal that top-tier capital and talent are concentrating further in Silicon Valley. Coinbase forms independent advisory board on quantum risks to blockchain (5 minute read) Coinbase has launched an independent advisory board of leading cryptography and quantum computing experts to assess how future quantum advances could impact blockchain security. The group will publish research, issue recommendations, and guide the industry on post-quantum preparedness as part of Coinbase's longer-term security roadmap. ⚡️ Quick Links How Legora scaled legal AI to 750 law firms in two years (1 hour video) Legora CEO Max Junestrand explains why Anthropic is winning legal AI workloads, how the company scaled rapidly across Europe and the US, why legal AI won't be winner-take-all, and how they raised $200M without a pitch deck. Using brief explanations and practical checklists, this book provides the tools to deliver meaningful measures that work , create reports that support decision-making, and deploy the necessary tools to engage your organization. Get my Copy 📱 News & Trends AI boom rattles software loans as credit fears mount (5 minute read) Software companies' leveraged loans are sliding as investors worry that rapid advances in AI could make parts of the traditional software stack obsolete. Fears that businesses will increasingly build custom tools, rather than buy off-the-shelf software, are hitting debt tied to highly leveraged, PE-backed firms, even as broader credit markets remain strong. Nubank gets conditional OCC approval for charter (2 minute read) Brazilian digital challenger Nubank has received conditional approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a US national bank. The company is entering the organization phase and is awaiting approval from the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. and the Federal Reserve. Nubank will focus on fully capitalizing the institution within 12 months and opening the bank within 18 months, as required by regulators. PicPay prices US IPO at top in first Brazilian debut since 2021 (3 minute read) PicPay (PicS NV), a fintech company controlled by Brazil's Batista family, priced its US IPO at the top of the range, raising $434M by selling 22.86M shares at $19 each and implying a ~$2.5B market value. Demand was ~12x shares available, with interest from tech, fintech, and emerging market investors, marking the first sizable Brazilian listing since Nubank's 2021 NYSE debut. PicPay has evolved from a digital wallet into a full digital bank with ~67M clients. It reported 270.4M reais net income on 7.26B reais revenue for the nine months ending September 30. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports The Agentic Payments Map (15 minute read) ACP, UCP, A2P, AXTP, x402. If your eyes just glazed over, you're not alone. The agentic payments conversation has become an alphabet soup of competing protocols, each claiming to solve the problem of how agents pay for things, and that's before you look at anything the card networks are doing, with their own emerging standards. There are so many because they're not all solving the same problem. Is Fintech or DeFi a better financial system? (20 minute read) A few years back, we would not have been able to compare DeFi and Fintech head-to-head, yet here we are. Crypto has built a financial infrastructure that rivals fintech on volume, users, and assets. Stablecoin rails are more global than traditional payment players. However, crypto doesn't capture as much value as traditional fintech counterparts when it comes to take rate. Crypto builds the most efficient open infrastructure, and distributes economic capture as a result. This is either a bug or a feature, depending on your perspective. If you believe financial services are destined to become commoditized utilities, crypto is simply accelerating the inevitable. If you believe businesses need revenue to survive, most tokens have a problem in capturing value. Silicon Valley legend Kleiner Perkins was written off. Then an unlikely VC showed up (18 minute read) Once seen as a fading legacy firm, Kleiner Perkins has staged a rare venture capital turnaround under Mamoon Hamid, who joined in 2017 and reshaped the firm's culture, speed, and focus. Backed by major wins like Figma and a strong slate of AI investments, the slimmer, more disciplined Kleiner is again competing at the top of Silicon Valley, this time by staying small, founder-centric, and relentlessly paranoid about complacency. 💻 Launches & Products Bolt partners with Aethir to power open-web checkout for games (2 minute read) Bolt, a checkout, identity, and payments leader powering faster, smarter commerce, today announced a partnership with Aethir, the world's largest distributed GPU cloud infrastructure provider, to power open-web checkout for games distributed beyond traditional app stores. N26 launches kids debit card (2 minute read) N26 is rolling out a debit card designed for children, giving parents more control over how their kids spend and learn to manage money. The product allows parents to set spending limits, monitor transactions in real time, and transfer allowances directly through the app, positioning it as a practical financial education tool. The move signals N26's push to deepen household engagement and build long-term customer relationships by onboarding users earlier in life. Wealth.com launches proprietary tax planning platform to unify tax and estate strategy (3 minute read) Wealth.com has introduced a new proprietary tax planning platform designed to integrate tax planning directly into its broader estate planning ecosystem for financial advisors, allowing unified strategy development and execution. The platform enables advisors to model forward-looking tax strategies, evaluate multi-state tax implications, and tie outcomes into estate and legacy planning workflows, addressing common pain points from disconnected planning tools. By embedding tax planning alongside estate strategy and execution, Wealth.com aims to help advisors deliver more holistic, multigenerational advice and improve client outcomes. 🎁 Miscellaneous OpenAI expands finance team as AI economics draw scrutiny (5 minute read) OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar announced two senior finance hires: Ajmere Dale as Chief Accounting Officer (to lead accounting and financial operations) and Cynthia Gaylor as Business Finance Officer, Corporate (to oversee corporate finance, long-range planning, capital strategy, and investor relations). The moves signal OpenAI is scaling its financial governance as AI becomes increasingly capital-intensive and investors/regulators scrutinize whether AI business models can sustainably match infrastructure and talent costs. Visa expands stablecoin advisory services amid cautious U.S. outlook (4 minute read) Visa has begun offering advisory services to help banks, fintechs, and merchants develop stablecoin strategies, responding to rising demand across the payments ecosystem. While Visa sees significant opportunity in cross-border settlement and emerging markets, CEO Ryan McInerney said stablecoins are unlikely to gain broad consumer traction in the US, where digital payment options are already abundant. Zocks lands $45m Series B to expand agentic AI capabilities (1 minute read) Zocks raised a $45M Series B co-led by Lightspeed Venture Partners and QED Investors, bringing total funding to $65M since launch. The company provides an AI assistant for financial advisors that automates onboarding, meeting prep, and document workflows, and says it's used by 5,000+ financial firms while saving advisors 10+ hours/week. Zocks will use the new capital to expand beyond admin automation into more agentic AI capabilities while scaling enterprise integrations, security, and compliance. ⚡️ Quick Links Embedded finance set to surge across everyday apps (4 minute read) Bank executives predict that embedded finance will rapidly expand in 2026 as payments, lending, and financial tools are woven into social, mobility, and consumer apps. Tiger Global and Microsoft to fully exit Walmart-backed PhonePe via its IPO (2 minute read) PhonePe is heading toward an IPO designed primarily as a liquidity event, enabling early investors Tiger Global and Microsoft to fully exit while Walmart maintains control. PayPal to Acquire Cymbio, Accelerating Agentic Commerce Capabilities (2 minute read) PayPal has agreed to acquire Cymbio, a multi-channel orchestration platform that helps brands sell across agentic surfaces. JPMorgan Buys UK Pensions Platform WealthOS (2 minute read) JPMorgan Chase & Co.has completed a deal to acquire WealthOS, a UK-based pensions technology platform. title: TLDR Fintech 2026-02-05
text: TLDR Fintech 2026-02-05 Anthropic shakes legal data giants ⚖️, Crypto.com prediction markets 🏈, Experian AI mortgages 🏡 📱 News & Trends Experian acquires Own Up (3 minute read) Experian is set to acquire Own Up, an AI-powered mortgage shopping platform, to expand mortgage access and integrate home loan guidance into its Experian Marketplace. The deal brings Own Up's advisory model, network of 40+ lenders, and AI tools for comparing rates and moving from shopping to preapproval directly into Experian's financial ecosystem. This strategic move deepens Experian's role in the homeownership journey by combining its data reach with Own Up's technology to offer more personalized lending options for consumers and better-qualified leads for lenders. Crypto.com launches predictions-only platform ahead of Super Bowl (2 minute read) Crypto.com is rolling out a standalone prediction markets platform, OG, after its event contracts business grew 40x week over week over the past six months. The CFTC-registered platform will offer leveraged and margin trading on prediction contracts tied to sports, entertainment, politics, and public companies, as regulators prepare new rules for the fast-growing sector. Anthropic's move into legal rattles data services stocks (4 minute read) Shares of legal software and data providers fell sharply after Anthropic unveiled new legal automation capabilities for its Cowork product, intensifying fears that AI could erode their core businesses. Investors worry that Anthropic's position as a model builder, not just an app layer, gives it an edge in disrupting both incumbents like Thomson Reuters and Wolters Kluwer, as well as AI-native legal startups. 🚀 Deep Dives & Reports Private equity's giant software bet has been upended by AI (6 minute read) Private equity firms are rethinking their long-held belief that software is a safe, recurring-revenue asset as AI threatens to commoditize and disrupt traditional SaaS models. Lenders and investors are cutting exposure, repricing risk, and stress-testing portfolios as tools like Anthropic's Claude lower barriers to building custom software and challenge debt-heavy buyout economics. Accenture's six big trends in banking for 2026 (6 minute read) The six forces reshaping banks this year include AI-driven operating models and growing pressure to modernize legacy infrastructure without breaking customer trust. Generative AI, embedded finance, and ecosystem partnerships are moving from experimentation to execution, while regulation, cyber risk, and cost discipline remain critical constraints. Banks that treat these shifts as structural rather than incremental will be better positioned to defend margins and relevance in an increasingly platform-driven financial system. AI's impact on 2026 budgets (7 minute read) AI is absorbing the majority of incremental budget in 2026 as boards and CEOs push for visible adoption, often at the expense of traditional SaaS spend and headcount. Durable moats are shifting toward vertical, multi-product software, while internal “vibe coding” rarely delivers ROI due to security, maintenance, and process risks. The result is a market where AI features are easier to fund than anything else, and pricing, margins, and go-to-market strategies are being rapidly rewritten. 💻 Launches & Products 95% of Banks See Zero ROI from AI Pilots (Sponsor) The problem isn't technology. It's architecture. Fintech firms are forced to predict workflows before observing how traders, analysts, personal bankers, and CSRs actually work. Liminal's Behavioral Agent Automation Platform inverts this: start by observing real behavior, identify friction, and deploy secure, compliant automations automatically. Human oversight and audit trails included. Download the free whitepaper (no sign up required). Y Combinator opens stablecoin funding option for startups (3 minute read) Y Combinator will allow startups to receive their YC funding in USDC stablecoins starting with the Spring 2026 batch, marking its first-ever stablecoin payout option. The move reflects cheaper, faster cross-border settlement after US regulatory clarity, and applies to all YC startups, not just crypto companies, without changing funding terms. Elon Musk's xAI dives into crypto and tradfi amid SpaceX merger (3 minute read) Elon Musk's xAI is hiring aggressively across crypto and traditional finance roles to train its AI models, including experts in DeFi, tokenomics, and quantitative trading. The push comes as SpaceX moves to merge with xAI in a deal reportedly valuing the combined entity at over $1 trillion, deepening Musk's consolidation across AI, space, and financial markets. 🎁 Miscellaneous New York AG warns consumers on Super Bowl prediction markets (4 minute read) New York Attorney General Letitia James cautioned consumers against using prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi ahead of the Super Bowl, arguing they function as unregulated gambling with limited consumer protections. She raised concerns about insider trading risks and the lack of oversight by state gaming regulators, even as billions of dollars in volume are expected to flow through these platforms. Operators counter that some markets are CFTC-regulated, but regulatory scrutiny is intensifying as prediction markets expand into sports and cultural events. PayPal replaces CEO after earnings miss, taps HP chief Enrique Lores (3 minute read) PayPal named HP CEO and current board chair Enrique Lores as its new CEO, replacing Alex Chriss after the board said execution was falling short of expectations. The leadership change follows a weak quarter, a downbeat profit outlook, and a nearly 18% drop in PayPal's shares as the company grapples with slowing consumer spending and intensifying competition in payments. ⚡️ Quick Links Fintech CEO and Forbes 30 Under 30 alum has been charged for alleged fraud (4 minute read) Gökçe Güven, the founder of New York–based fintech Kalder, was indicted after US prosecutors alleged she raised $7 million by misleading investors about customer traction, revenue growth, and the company's financial health. Agentic commerce gatekeeping problem (8 minute read) Agentic commerce protocols are emerging as “open” standards controlled by early distribution partners like Shopify, creating data asymmetries that risk shifting incentives from individual merchant success toward platform-level GMV optimization.
